Tool Result #458 Read toolu_01EBvKRWnUNvFn23wDujVBXE 14:25:16
Read output=1→/** 2→ * Webhook notification: sends a POST to configurable endpoints. 3→ * Supports Feishu, Telegram, Discord, Slack, and generic webhooks. 4→ * Never throws — failures are l...
Show output payload 
1→/**
     2→ * Webhook notification: sends a POST to configurable endpoints.
     3→ * Supports Feishu, Telegram, Discord, Slack, and generic webhooks.
     4→ * Never throws — failures are logged as warnings.
     5→ */
     6→
     7→import { getLogger } from '../../heartbeat/log.js'
     8→
     9→export interface WebhookOptions {
    10→  url: string
    11→  sessionCount: number
    12→  newSessionIds?: string[]
    13→  reportUrl?: string
    14→}
    15→
    16→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    17→// Platform detection
    18→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    19→
    20→type Platform = 'feishu' | 'telegram' | 'discord' | 'slack' | 'generic'
    21→
    22→function detectPlatform(url: string): Platform {
    23→  if (url.includes('open.feishu.cn') || url.includes('open.larksuite.com')) return 'feishu'
    24→  if (url.includes('api.telegram.org')) return 'telegram'
    25→  if (url.includes('discord.com/api/webhooks') || url.includes('discordapp.com/api/webhooks')) return 'discord'
    26→  if (url.includes('hooks.slack.com')) return 'slack'
    27→  return 'generic'
    28→}
    29→
    30→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    31→// Payload builders
    32→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    33→
    34→function buildFeishuPayload(options: WebhookOptions): Record<string, unknown> {
    35→  const { sessionCount, newSessionIds = [], reportUrl } = options
    36→  const newCount = newSessionIds.length
    37→  const actionBtn = reportUrl
    38→    ? [{
    39→        tag: 'button',
    40→        text: { content: 'View Report', tag: 'plain_text' },
    41→        url: reportUrl,
    42→        type: 'primary',
    43→      }]
    44→    : []
    45→
    46→  return {
    47→    msg_type: 'interactive',
    48→    card: {
    49→      header: {
    50→        title: { content: 'MemoryTree Report Updated', tag: 'plain_text' },
    51→        template: 'blue',
    52→      },
    53→      elements: [
    54→        {
    55→          tag: 'div',
    56→          text: {
    57→            tag: 'lark_md',
    58→            content: `**Sessions:** ${sessionCount} total${newCount ? `, ${newCount} new` : ''}`,
    59→          },
    60→        },
    61→        ...(actionBtn.length > 0 ? [{ tag: 'action', actions: actionBtn }] : []),
    62→      ],
    63→    },
    64→  }
    65→}
    66→
    67→function buildTelegramPayload(options: WebhookOptions): Record<string, unknown> {
    68→  const { sessionCount, newSessionIds = [], reportUrl } = options
    69→  const newCount = newSessionIds.length
    70→  let text = `📊 *MemoryTree Report Updated*\n\nSessions: ${sessionCount} total`
    71→  if (newCount > 0) text += `, ${newCount} new`
    72→  if (reportUrl) text += `\n[View Report](${reportUrl})`
    73→  return { text, parse_mode: 'Markdown' }
    74→}
    75→
    76→function buildDiscordPayload(options: WebhookOptions): Record<string, unknown> {
    77→  const { sessionCount, newSessionIds = [], reportUrl } = options
    78→  const newCount = newSessionIds.length
    79→  let content = `📊 **MemoryTree Report Updated** — ${sessionCount} sessions total`
    80→  if (newCount > 0) content += `, ${newCount} new`
    81→  if (reportUrl) content += ` · [View Report](${reportUrl})`
    82→  return { content }
    83→}
    84→
    85→function buildSlackPayload(options: WebhookOptions): Record<string, unknown> {
    86→  const { sessionCount, newSessionIds = [], reportUrl } = options
    87→  const newCount = newSessionIds.length
    88→  let text = `📊 MemoryTree Report Updated — ${sessionCount} sessions total`
    89→  if (newCount > 0) text += `, ${newCount} new`
    90→  if (reportUrl) text += ` · <${reportUrl}|View Report>`
    91→  return { text }
    92→}
    93→
    94→function buildGenericPayload(options: WebhookOptions): Record<string, unknown> {
    95→  return {
    96→    event: 'memorytree.report.updated',
    97→    session_count: options.sessionCount,
    98→    new_session_count: options.newSessionIds?.length ?? 0,
    99→    new_session_ids: options.newSessionIds ?? [],
   100→    report_url: options.reportUrl ?? '',
   101→  }
   102→}
   103→
   104→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   105→// URL safety validation
   106→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   107→
   108→/** Private / loopback IP ranges that must not be reachable via webhook. */
   109→const PRIVATE_HOSTNAME_RE =
   110→  /^(localhost|127\.\d+\.\d+\.\d+|0\.0\.0\.0|::1|10\.\d+\.\d+\.\d+|172\.(1[6-9]|2\d|3[01])\.\d+\.\d+|192\.168\.\d+\.\d+|169\.254\.\d+\.\d+|fc[0-9a-f]{2}:|fd[0-9a-f]{2}:)$/i
   111→
   112→function validateWebhookUrl(raw: string): URL {
   113→  let url: URL
   114→  try {
   115→    url = new URL(raw)
   116→  } catch {
   117→    throw new Error(`Invalid webhook URL: ${raw}`)
   118→  }
   119→  if (url.protocol !== 'https:') {
   120→    throw new Error(`Webhook URL must use https: (got ${url.protocol})`)
   121→  }
   122→  if (PRIVATE_HOSTNAME_RE.test(url.hostname)) {
   123→    throw new Error(`Webhook URL hostname is private/loopback: ${url.hostname}`)
   124→  }
   125→  return url
   126→}
   127→
   128→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   129→// Main send function
   130→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   131→
   132→export async function sendWebhook(options: WebhookOptions): Promise<void> {
   133→  if (!options.url) return
   134→  const logger = getLogger()
   135→
   136→  let parsedUrl: URL
   137→  try {
   138→    parsedUrl = validateWebhookUrl(options.url)
   139→  } catch (err) {
   140→    logger.warn(`[webhook] Rejected URL: ${String(err)}`)
   141→    return
   142→  }
   143→
   144→  const platform = detectPlatform(options.url)
   145→
   146→  const payloadMap: Record<Platform, (o: WebhookOptions) => Record<string, unknown>> = {
   147→    feishu: buildFeishuPayload,
   148→    telegram: buildTelegramPayload,
   149→    discord: buildDiscordPayload,
   150→    slack: buildSlackPayload,
   151→    generic: buildGenericPayload,
   152→  }
   153→
   154→  const payload = payloadMap[platform](options)
   155→
   156→  try {
   157→    const { default: https } = await import('node:https')
   158→    const body = JSON.stringify(payload)
   159→
   160→    await new Promise<void>((resolve, reject) => {
   161→      const url = parsedUrl
   162→      const req = https.request(
   163→        {
   164→          hostname: url.hostname,
   165→          port: url.port || 443,
   166→          path: url.pathname + url.search,
   167→          method: 'POST',
   168→          headers: {
   169→            'Content-Type': 'application/json',
   170→            'Content-Length': Buffer.byteLength(body),
   171→          },
   172→        },
   173→        res => {
   174→          // Consume response to avoid socket leak
   175→          res.resume()
   176→          if (res.statusCode && res.statusCode >= 400) {
   177→            reject(new Error(`HTTP ${res.statusCode}`))
   178→          } else {
   179→            resolve()
   180→          }
   181→        },
   182→      )
   183→      req.on('error', reject)
   184→      req.setTimeout(5000, () => { req.destroy(); reject(new Error('timeout')) })
   185→      req.write(body)
   186→      req.end()
   187→    })
   188→
   189→    logger.info(`[webhook] Sent ${platform} notification`)
   190→  } catch (err: unknown) {
   191→    logger.warn(`[webhook] Failed to send notification: ${String(err)}`)
   192→    // Never throw
   193→  }
   194→}
   195→

But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer questions about the code behavior.

assistant #459 14:26:03

两个 HIGH 问题都在 webhook.ts。修复方案：
1. detectPlatform — 改用 parsedUrl.hostname 精确匹配，避免子串欺骗
2. PRIVATE_HOSTNAME_RE — 当前 IPv6 ULA 模式 fc[0-9a-f]{2}:$ 永远不会匹配完整的 fc00::1 地址，拆出独立函数覆盖全部私有 IPv6 范围

Tool Call #460 Edit toolu_0145qxPvn92M85M4UihBTKUr 14:26:09
Edit input={"file_path":"C:/Users/ai/.codex/skills/memorytree-workflow/src/report/deploy/webhook.ts","new_string":"// ----------------------------------------------------------------------...
Show input payload 
{
  "replace_all": false,
  "file_path": "C:/Users/ai/.codex/skills/memorytree-workflow/src/report/deploy/webhook.ts",
  "old_string": "// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n// Platform detection\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\ntype Platform = 'feishu' | 'telegram' | 'discord' | 'slack' | 'generic'\n\nfunction detectPlatform(url: string): Platform {\n  if (url.includes('open.feishu.cn') || url.includes('open.larksuite.com')) return 'feishu'\n  if (url.includes('api.telegram.org')) return 'telegram'\n  if (url.includes('discord.com/api/webhooks') || url.includes('discordapp.com/api/webhooks')) return 'discord'\n  if (url.includes('hooks.slack.com')) return 'slack'\n  return 'generic'\n}",
  "new_string": "// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n// Platform detection\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\ntype Platform = 'feishu' | 'telegram' | 'discord' | 'slack' | 'generic'\n\n/**\n * Detect platform from a parsed URL.\n * Uses exact hostname matching to prevent subdomain-spoofing attacks like\n * https://my-open.feishu.cn.attacker.com being mistaken for Feishu.\n */\nfunction detectPlatform(parsedUrl: URL): Platform {\n  const h = parsedUrl.hostname.toLowerCase()\n  if (h === 'open.feishu.cn' || h === 'open.larksuite.com') return 'feishu'\n  if (h === 'api.telegram.org') return 'telegram'\n  if (h === 'discord.com' || h === 'discordapp.com') return 'discord'\n  if (h === 'hooks.slack.com') return 'slack'\n  return 'generic'\n}"
}

Edit input={"file_path":"C:/Users/ai/.codex/skills/memorytree-workflow/src/report/deploy/webhook.ts","new_string":"// ----------------------------------------------------------------------...
Show input payload 
{
  "replace_all": false,
  "file_path": "C:/Users/ai/.codex/skills/memorytree-workflow/src/report/deploy/webhook.ts",
  "old_string": "// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n// URL safety validation\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n/** Private / loopback IP ranges that must not be reachable via webhook. */\nconst PRIVATE_HOSTNAME_RE =\n  /^(localhost|127\\.\\d+\\.\\d+\\.\\d+|0\\.0\\.0\\.0|::1|10\\.\\d+\\.\\d+\\.\\d+|172\\.(1[6-9]|2\\d|3[01])\\.\\d+\\.\\d+|192\\.168\\.\\d+\\.\\d+|169\\.254\\.\\d+\\.\\d+|fc[0-9a-f]{2}:|fd[0-9a-f]{2}:)$/i\n\nfunction validateWebhookUrl(raw: string): URL {\n  let url: URL\n  try {\n    url = new URL(raw)\n  } catch {\n    throw new Error(`Invalid webhook URL: ${raw}`)\n  }\n  if (url.protocol !== 'https:') {\n    throw new Error(`Webhook URL must use https: (got ${url.protocol})`)\n  }\n  if (PRIVATE_HOSTNAME_RE.test(url.hostname)) {\n    throw new Error(`Webhook URL hostname is private/loopback: ${url.hostname}`)\n  }\n  return url\n}",
  "new_string": "// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n// URL safety validation\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n/** Private / loopback IPv4 ranges. */\nconst PRIVATE_IPV4_RE =\n  /^(localhost|127\\.\\d+\\.\\d+\\.\\d+|0\\.0\\.0\\.0|10\\.\\d+\\.\\d+\\.\\d+|172\\.(1[6-9]|2\\d|3[01])\\.\\d+\\.\\d+|192\\.168\\.\\d+\\.\\d+|169\\.254\\.\\d+\\.\\d+)$/i\n\n/**\n * Returns true for all private / loopback IPv6 addresses:\n *   ::1           loopback\n *   fe80::/10     link-local (fe80 – febf)\n *   fc00::/7      ULA (fc and fd)\n *   ::ffff:0:0/96 IPv4-mapped\n */\nfunction isPrivateIPv6(hostname: string): boolean {\n  const h = hostname.toLowerCase()\n  if (h === '::1') return true\n  if (/^fe[89ab]/i.test(h)) return true   // fe80::/10\n  if (h.startsWith('fc') || h.startsWith('fd')) return true  // fc00::/7 ULA\n  if (h.startsWith('::ffff:')) return true  // IPv4-mapped\n  return false\n}\n\nfunction validateWebhookUrl(raw: string): URL {\n  let url: URL\n  try {\n    url = new URL(raw)\n  } catch {\n    throw new Error(`Invalid webhook URL: ${raw}`)\n  }\n  if (url.protocol !== 'https:') {\n    throw new Error(`Webhook URL must use https: (got ${url.protocol})`)\n  }\n  if (PRIVATE_IPV4_RE.test(url.hostname) || isPrivateIPv6(url.hostname)) {\n    throw new Error(`Webhook URL hostname is private/loopback: ${url.hostname}`)\n  }\n  return url\n}"
}

Show input payload 
{
  "replace_all": false,
  "file_path": "C:/Users/ai/.codex/skills/memorytree-workflow/tests/report/deploy/webhook.test.ts",
  "old_string": "// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n// Network failure does not throw\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------",
  "new_string": "// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n// Platform detection — hostname spoofing resistance\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\ndescribe('sendWebhook — platform detection spoofing resistance', () => {\n  it('treats subdomain-spoof feishu URL as generic, not feishu', async () => {\n    mockSuccess()\n    await sendWebhook({\n      url: 'https://my-open.feishu.cn.attacker.com/hook/test',\n      sessionCount: 3,\n    })\n\n    const writtenBody = mockRequest.write.mock.calls[0]?.[0] as string\n    const parsed = JSON.parse(writtenBody) as { event?: string; msg_type?: string }\n    // Generic payload has 'event' field; Feishu has 'msg_type'\n    expect(parsed.event).toBe('memorytree.report.updated')\n    expect(parsed.msg_type).toBeUndefined()\n  })\n\n  it('treats path-spoof telegram URL as generic', async () => {\n    mockSuccess()\n    await sendWebhook({\n      url: 'https://evil.com/api.telegram.org/botTEST/sendMessage',\n      sessionCount: 1,\n    })\n\n    const writtenBody = mockRequest.write.mock.calls[0]?.[0] as string\n    const parsed = JSON.parse(writtenBody) as { event?: string; text?: string }\n    expect(parsed.event).toBe('memorytree.report.updated')\n    expect(parsed.text).toBeUndefined()\n  })\n})\n\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n// Network failure does not throw\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------"
}

assistant #491 14:29:27

The failures reveal that Node.js's URL.hostname returns IPv6 addresses with brackets (e.g. [::1] not ::1). Need to strip brackets first in isPrivateIPv6:

Tool Call #492 Edit toolu_011WLYQXfzuMStbHwNnY5LG7 14:29:33
Edit input={"file_path":"C:/Users/ai/.codex/skills/memorytree-workflow/src/report/deploy/webhook.ts","new_string":"/**\n * Returns true for all private / loopback IPv6 addresses.\n * Node....
Show input payload 
{
  "replace_all": false,
  "file_path": "C:/Users/ai/.codex/skills/memorytree-workflow/src/report/deploy/webhook.ts",
  "old_string": "/**\n * Returns true for all private / loopback IPv6 addresses:\n *   ::1           loopback\n *   fe80::/10     link-local (fe80 – febf)\n *   fc00::/7      ULA (fc and fd)\n *   ::ffff:0:0/96 IPv4-mapped\n */\nfunction isPrivateIPv6(hostname: string): boolean {\n  const h = hostname.toLowerCase()\n  if (h === '::1') return true\n  if (/^fe[89ab]/i.test(h)) return true   // fe80::/10\n  if (h.startsWith('fc') || h.startsWith('fd')) return true  // fc00::/7 ULA\n  if (h.startsWith('::ffff:')) return true  // IPv4-mapped\n  return false\n}",
  "new_string": "/**\n * Returns true for all private / loopback IPv6 addresses.\n * Node.js URL.hostname may return IPv6 with or without surrounding brackets,\n * so we strip them before checking.\n *   ::1           loopback\n *   fe80::/10     link-local (fe80 – febf)\n *   fc00::/7      ULA (fc and fd)\n *   ::ffff:0:0/96 IPv4-mapped\n */\nfunction isPrivateIPv6(hostname: string): boolean {\n  // Strip optional surrounding brackets added by some URL implementations\n  const h = hostname.toLowerCase().replace(/^\\[|]$/g, '')\n  if (h === '::1') return true\n  if (/^fe[89ab]/i.test(h)) return true       // fe80::/10 link-local\n  if (h.startsWith('fc') || h.startsWith('fd')) return true  // fc00::/7 ULA\n  if (h.startsWith('::ffff:')) return true    // IPv4-mapped\n  return false\n}"
}

Shell cwd was reset to D:\demo1\openmnemo
assistant #496 14:30:11

564 tests passing, 0 failures.

两个 HIGH 安全问题修复完毕，以下是总结：

---

### 修复内容

问题 1 — detectPlatform 字符串欺骗

原来用 url.includes('open.feishu.cn')，攻击者可以构造 https://my-open.feishu.cn.attacker.com/hook 使其被误识别为飞书，从而收到飞书格式的 payload 而不是 generic。

修复：改为 parsedUrl.hostname.toLowerCase() === 'open.feishu.cn'，精确匹配主机名，杜绝子域名欺骗和路径欺骗。

问题 2 — IPv6 ULA/链路本地地址漏过 SSRF 检查

原来的正则 fc[0-9a-f]{2}: 加上末尾 $ 锚点，只能匹配恰好等于 fcXX: 的主机名（永远不成立），fc00::1 这样的真实 IPv6 地址完全绕过检查。

修复：
- 拆出独立 isPrivateIPv6() 函数，覆盖 ::1（环回）、fe80::/10（链路本地）、fc00::/7（ULA）、::ffff:0:0/96（IPv4 映射）
- 额外处理 Node.js URL.hostname 对 IPv6 保留方括号的情况（[::1] → strip → ::1）
- 新增 5 个 IPv6 SSRF 测试 + 2 个平台欺骗测试，共 564 个测试全部通过

现在我有完整信息了，逐一核对两轮 review 的所有条目：

---

## 问题状态全表

### 第一轮修复（上次 PR）— 全部已解决 ✅

| # | 问题 | 状态 |
|---|------|------|
| 1 | Shell injection (github-pages.ts) | ✅ 已修复 |
| 2 | Popover XSS (layout.ts) | ✅ 已修复 |
| 3 | SSRF 基础防护 (webhook.ts) | ✅ 已修复 |
| 4 | JSON u2028/u2029 (graph.ts) | ✅ 已修复 |
| 5 | Promise.all 无隔离 (build.ts) | ✅ 已修复 |
| 6 | data-summary 永远为空 | ✅ 已修复 |
| 7 | Nav 路径深度错误 | ✅ 已修复 |
| 8 | webhook.test.ts 无 HTTP mock | ✅ 已修复 |
| 9 | CSS 硬编码颜色 | ✅ 已修复 |
| 10 | rAF 无限循环 | ✅ 已修复 |
| 11 | Dead code #graph-tooltip | ✅ 已修复 |
| 12 | 重复动态 import node:fs | ✅ 已修复 |
| 13 | worktree 路径在 .git 内 | ✅ 已修复（改用 tmpdir） |

### 本轮修复（刚完成）— 已解决 ✅

| # | 问题 | 状态 |
|---|------|------|
| 14 | detectPlatform 子域名欺骗 | ✅ 已修复 |
| 15 | IPv6 ULA/链路本地 SSRF 漏洞 | ✅ 已修复 |

### 两轮 review 剩余未修问题

| # | 文件 | 严重性 | 问题 |
|---|------|--------|------|
| 16 | github-pages.ts:58 | MEDIUM | Date.now() 并发时可能路径冲突，应改 crypto.randomUUID() 或 mkdtempSync |
| 17 | github-pages.ts:88 | MEDIUM | makeRelative() 自实现脆弱，path.relative() 已导入但未使用 |
| 18 | build.ts:257 | MEDIUM | JSON.parse(raw) as ManifestEntry 无运行时类型守卫，字段缺失时静默失效 |
| 19 | graph.ts:88 | LOW | slugify() 与 layout.ts:slugifyName() 重复，应复用 |
| 20 | webhook.test.ts | LOW | 未验证请求头 Content-Type / Content-Length；未测试超时触发 |
| 21 | github-pages.test.ts | LOW | mock 调用顺序脆弱，应改用 toHaveBeenCalledWith() 直接断言参数 |
| 22 | graph.ts:169-171 | INFO | 物理参数 CHARGE=-120, LINK_DIST=80 等均为魔法数字，缺注释 |
| 23 | css.ts:489 | INFO | mark 在亮色主题下 --code-bg(#f6f8fa) 配 --warning(#d29922) 对比度约 2.8:1，不达 WCAG AA (4.5:1) |

---

结论： 14 + 2 = 16 个问题已全部修复，还剩 8 个（全是 MEDIUM~INFO 级）。

这 8 个要现在一起修完，还是挑其中的重点修？

Read output=1→/** 2→ * GitHub Pages deployment: push report output to gh-pages branch. 3→ */ 4→ 5→import { writeFileSync } from 'node:fs' 6→import { join } from 'node:path' 7→import { tmpdi...
Show output payload 
1→/**
     2→ * GitHub Pages deployment: push report output to gh-pages branch.
     3→ */
     4→
     5→import { writeFileSync } from 'node:fs'
     6→import { join } from 'node:path'
     7→import { tmpdir } from 'node:os'
     8→import { execFileSync } from 'node:child_process'
     9→
    10→import { getLogger } from '../../heartbeat/log.js'
    11→
    12→export interface GithubPagesOptions {
    13→  /** Repository root (contains .git/) */
    14→  repoRoot: string
    15→  /** Report output directory (e.g. Memory/07_reports) */
    16→  outputDir: string
    17→  /** Branch name to push to (e.g. 'gh-pages'). Empty string = skip. */
    18→  branch: string
    19→  /** Custom domain (CNAME). Empty string = skip. */
    20→  cname: string
    21→}
    22→
    23→/** Safe character set for branch names — blocks shell meta-chars. */
    24→const BRANCH_RE = /^[a-zA-Z0-9._/-]+$/
    25→
    26→export async function deployGithubPages(options: GithubPagesOptions): Promise<void> {
    27→  const { repoRoot, outputDir, branch, cname } = options
    28→  const logger = getLogger()
    29→
    30→  if (!branch) return
    31→
    32→  // Validate branch name to prevent any injection
    33→  if (!BRANCH_RE.test(branch)) {
    34→    logger.warn(`[gh-pages] Invalid branch name, skipping deploy: "${branch}"`)
    35→    return
    36→  }
    37→
    38→  try {
    39→    // 1. Write CNAME file if configured
    40→    if (cname) {
    41→      const cnamePath = join(outputDir, 'CNAME')
    42→      writeFileSync(cnamePath, cname + '\n', 'utf-8')
    43→      logger.info(`[gh-pages] CNAME written: ${cname}`)
    44→    }
    45→
    46→    // 2. Check if branch exists on remote
    47→    let branchExists = false
    48→    try {
    49→      git(repoRoot, 'ls-remote', '--exit-code', '--heads', 'origin', branch)
    50→      branchExists = true
    51→    } catch {
    52→      branchExists = false
    53→    }
    54→
    55→    if (!branchExists) {
    56→      // Create orphan branch via a temporary worktree in os.tmpdir()
    57→      logger.info(`[gh-pages] Branch '${branch}' not found, creating orphan...`)
    58→      const worktreePath = join(tmpdir(), `mt-gh-pages-${Date.now()}`)
    59→      try {
    60→        git(repoRoot, 'worktree', 'add', '--orphan', '-b', branch, worktreePath)
    61→        git(worktreePath, 'commit', '--allow-empty', '-m', 'chore: init gh-pages')
    62→        git(repoRoot, 'push', 'origin', branch)
    63→      } finally {
    64→        try { git(repoRoot, 'worktree', 'remove', '--force', worktreePath) } catch { /* ignore */ }
    65→      }
    66→    }
    67→
    68→    // 3. Push using git subtree
    69→    const relOutput = makeRelative(repoRoot, outputDir)
    70→    logger.info(`[gh-pages] Pushing ${relOutput} to origin/${branch}...`)
    71→    git(repoRoot, 'subtree', 'push', '--prefix', relOutput, 'origin', branch)
    72→    logger.info(`[gh-pages] Successfully pushed to origin/${branch}`)
    73→  } catch (err: unknown) {
    74→    logger.warn(`[gh-pages] Deploy failed: ${String(err)}`)
    75→    // Never throw — report deploy failure must not abort heartbeat
    76→  }
    77→}
    78→
    79→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    80→// Helpers
    81→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    82→
    83→/** Invoke git with explicit args — no shell interpolation. */
    84→function git(cwd: string, ...args: string[]): string {
    85→  return execFileSync('git', args, { cwd, stdio: 'pipe' }).toString()
    86→}
    87→
    88→function makeRelative(base: string, target: string): string {
    89→  // Normalize separators
    90→  const b = base.replace(/\\/g, '/').replace(/\/$/, '')
    91→  const t = target.replace(/\\/g, '/').replace(/\/$/, '')
    92→  if (t.startsWith(b + '/')) {
    93→    return t.slice(b.length + 1)
    94→  }
    95→  return t
    96→}
    97→

Read output=244→function loadManifestsRecursive(dir: string, out: ManifestEntry[]): void { 245→ let entries: string[] 246→ try { 247→ entries = readdirSync(dir) 248→ } catch { 249→ return 2...
Show output payload 
244→function loadManifestsRecursive(dir: string, out: ManifestEntry[]): void {
   245→  let entries: string[]
   246→  try {
   247→    entries = readdirSync(dir)
   248→  } catch {
   249→    return
   250→  }
   251→
   252→  for (const entry of entries) {
   253→    const fullPath = join(dir, entry)
   254→    if (entry.endsWith('.json')) {
   255→      try {
   256→        const raw = readFileSync(fullPath, 'utf-8')
   257→        const parsed = JSON.parse(raw) as ManifestEntry
   258→        if (parsed.session_id && parsed.client) {
   259→          out.push(parsed)
   260→        }
   261→      } catch {
   262→        // Skip malformed manifests
   263→      }
   264→    } else {
   265→      // Recurse into subdirectories
   266→      loadManifestsRecursive(fullPath, out)
   267→    }
   268→  }
   269→}
   270→
   271→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   272→// Clean markdown message parsing
   273→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   274→
   275→/** Parse messages from a clean markdown file. */
   276→export function parseCleanMarkdownMessages(m: ManifestEntry, root: string): RenderedMessage[] {
   277→  const cleanPath = m.repo_clean_path
   278→    ? join(root, m.repo_clean_path)
   279→    : m.global_clean_path
   280→  if (!cleanPath || !existsSync(cleanPath)) return []
   281→
   282→  let content: string
   283→  try {
   284→    content = readFileSync(cleanPath, 'utf-8')
   285→  } catch {
   286→    return []
   287→  }
   288→
   289→  return parseMessagesFromMarkdown(content)
   290→}
   291→
   292→/** Extract role/timestamp/text from clean markdown format. */
   293→export function parseMessagesFromMarkdown(content: string): RenderedMessage[] {
   294→  const messages: RenderedMessage[] = []
   295→
   296→  // Find ## Messages section
   297→  const msgSection = content.indexOf('\n## Messages')
   298→  if (msgSection === -1) return []
   299→
   300→  const body = content.slice(msgSection + 12) // after "## Messages\n"
   301→
   302→  // Split on ### N. role headers
   303→  const msgRe = /^### \d+\.\s+(\w+)/gm
   304→  let match: RegExpExecArray | null
   305→  const starts: Array<{ index: number; role: string }> = []
   306→
   307→  while ((match = msgRe.exec(body)) !== null) {
   308→    starts.push({ index: match.index, role: match[1] ?? 'unknown' })
   309→  }
   310→
   311→  for (let i = 0; i < starts.length; i++) {
   312→    const start = starts[i]
   313→    if (!start) continue
   314→    const nextStart = starts[i + 1]
   315→    const chunk = nextStart
   316→      ? body.slice(start.index, nextStart.index)
   317→      : body.slice(start.index)
   318→
   319→    const lines = chunk.split('\n')
   320→    // First line is the header, second may be timestamp
   321→    let timestamp = ''
   322→    const textLines: string[] = []
   323→    let pastHeader = false
   324→
   325→    for (const line of lines) {
   326→      if (!pastHeader && line.startsWith('### ')) {
   327→        pastHeader = true
   328→        continue
   329→      }
   330→      if (!pastHeader) continue
   331→      const tsMatch = line.match(/^- Timestamp:\s*`([^`]+)`/)
   332→      if (tsMatch && !timestamp) {
   333→        timestamp = tsMatch[1] ?? ''
   334→        continue
   335→      }
   336→      textLines.push(line)
   337→    }
   338→
   339→    // Remove leading/trailing blank lines from text
   340→    const text = textLines.join('\n').trim()
   341→    if (text || timestamp) {
   342→      messages.push({ role: start.role, timestamp, text })
   343→    }
   344→  }
   345→
   346→  return messages
   347→}
   348→
   349→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   350→// Raw transcript tool extraction
   351→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   352→
   353→/** Best-effort extraction of tool event summaries from a raw JSONL file. */
   354→function extractToolSummariesFromRaw(rawPath: string, _client: string): string[] {
   355→  const summaries: string[] = []
   356→  let content: string
   357→  try {
   358→    content = readFileSync(rawPath, 'utf-8')
   359→  } catch {
   360→    return []
   361→  }
   362→
   363→  for (const line of content.split('\n')) {
   364→    const trimmed = line.trim()
   365→    if (!trimmed) continue
   366→    try {
   367→      const record = JSON.parse(trimmed) as Record<string, unknown>
   368→      // Codex: function_call type
   369→      const type = record['type'] as string | undefined
   370→      if (type === 'function_call' || type === 'custom_tool_call') {
   371→        const name = (record['name'] as string | undefined) ?? type
   372→        summaries.push(name)
   373→        continue
   374→      }
   375→      // Claude: tool_use content blocks
   376→      const payload = record['message'] as Record<string, unknown> | undefined
   377→      const content2 = (payload?.['content'] ?? record['content']) as unknown[] | undefined
   378→      if (Array.isArray(content2)) {
   379→        for (const block of content2) {
   380→          if (
   381→            block !== null &&
   382→            typeof block === 'object' &&
   383→            (block as Record<string, unknown>)['type'] === 'tool_use'
   384→          ) {
   385→            const name = ((block as Record<string, unknown>)['name'] as string | undefined) ?? 'tool_use'
   386→            summaries.push(name)
   387→          }
   388→        }
   389→      }
   390→    } catch {
   391→      // Skip malformed lines
   392→    }
   393→  }
   394→
   395→  return summaries
   396→}
   397→
   398→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   399→// Output path helpers
   400→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   401→
   402→function transcriptOutputPath(output: string, m: ManifestEntry): string {
   403→  const cleanPath = m.repo_clean_path || m.global_clean_path || ''
   404→  const stem = cleanPath ? basename(cleanPath, '.md') : m.session_id
   405→  return join(output, 'transcripts', m.client, `${stem}.html`)
   406→}
   407→
   408→function resolveRawPath(m: ManifestEntry, root: string): string | null {
   409→  if (m.repo_raw_path) {
   410→    return join(root, m.repo_raw_path)
   411→  }
   412→  if (m.global_raw_path) {
   413→    return m.global_raw_path
   414→  }
   415→  return null
   416→}
   417→
   418→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   419→// Markdown file loader (for goals/knowledge/todos/archive)
   420→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   421→
   422→function loadMarkdownFiles(dir: string): Array<{ filename: string; title: string; content: string }> {
   423→  if (!existsSync(dir)) return []
   424→
   425→  let entries: string[]
   426→  try {
   427→    entries = readdirSync(dir)
   428→  } catch {
   429→    return []
   430→  }
   431→
   432→  return entries
   433→    .filter(e => e.endsWith('.md'))
   434→    .sort()
   435→    .map(filename => {
   436→      const fullPath = join(dir, filename)
   437→      let content = ''
   438→      try {
   439→        content = readFileSync(fullPath, 'utf-8')
   440→      } catch {
   441→        // empty
   442→      }
   443→      const title = extractMarkdownTitle(content) || basename(filename, '.md')
   444→      return { filename, title, content }
   445→    })
   446→}
   447→
   448→function extractMarkdownTitle(content: string): string {
   449→  const match = content.match(/^#\s+(.+)$/m)
   450→  return match?.[1]?.trim() ?? ''
   451→}
   452→
   453→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   454→// .gitignore management
   455→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   456→
   457→export function ensureGitignore(root: string, entry: string): void {
   458→  const gitignorePath = join(root, '.gitignore')
   459→  let existing = ''
   460→  if (existsSync(gitignorePath)) {
   461→    try {
   462→      existing = readFileSync(gitignorePath, 'utf-8')
   463→    } catch {
   464→      return
   465→    }
   466→  }
   467→  // Check if already present (normalize line endings)
   468→  const lines = existing.replace(/\r\n/g, '\n').split('\n')
   469→  if (lines.some(l => l.trim() === entry.trim())) return
   470→
   471→  // Append with trailing newline
   472→  const append = existing.endsWith('\n') ? entry + '\n' : '\n' + entry + '\n'
   473→  try {
   474→    appendFileSync(gitignorePath, append, 'utf-8')
   475→  } catch {
   476→    // Best-effort
   477→  }
   478→}
   479→

Read output=1→/** 2→ * Knowledge graph page: force-directed visualization of sessions and knowledge links. 3→ * Inline vanilla JS, no CDN dependencies. 4→ */ 5→ 6→import type { ManifestEntr...
Show output payload 
1→/**
     2→ * Knowledge graph page: force-directed visualization of sessions and knowledge links.
     3→ * Inline vanilla JS, no CDN dependencies.
     4→ */
     5→
     6→import type { ManifestEntry } from '../../types/transcript.js'
     7→import type { LinkGraph } from '../../types/report.js'
     8→import type { Translations } from '../i18n/types.js'
     9→import type { MarkdownFile } from './layout.js'
    10→import { escHtml, htmlShell, renderNav, transcriptUrlFromRoot } from './layout.js'
    11→
    12→const MAX_NODES = 500
    13→
    14→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    15→// Graph data types
    16→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    17→
    18→interface GraphNode {
    19→  id: string
    20→  label: string
    21→  type: 'session' | 'knowledge'
    22→  client?: string
    23→  url: string
    24→}
    25→
    26→interface GraphEdge {
    27→  source: string
    28→  target: string
    29→}
    30→
    31→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    32→// Build graph data
    33→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    34→
    35→export function buildGraphData(
    36→  manifests: ManifestEntry[],
    37→  knowledgeFiles: MarkdownFile[],
    38→  linkGraph: LinkGraph,
    39→): { nodes: GraphNode[]; edges: GraphEdge[] } {
    40→  // Cap manifests to MAX_NODES (most recent first)
    41→  const sorted = [...manifests]
    42→    .sort((a, b) => b.started_at.localeCompare(a.started_at))
    43→    .slice(0, MAX_NODES)
    44→
    45→  const nodeSet = new Set<string>()
    46→  const nodes: GraphNode[] = []
    47→  const edges: GraphEdge[] = []
    48→
    49→  for (const m of sorted) {
    50→    if (!nodeSet.has(m.session_id)) {
    51→      nodeSet.add(m.session_id)
    52→      nodes.push({
    53→        id: m.session_id,
    54→        label: m.title || m.session_id.slice(0, 8),
    55→        type: 'session',
    56→        client: m.client,
    57→        url: transcriptUrlFromRoot(m),
    58→      })
    59→    }
    60→  }
    61→
    62→  for (const kf of knowledgeFiles) {
    63→    const id = `knowledge:${kf.filename}`
    64→    if (!nodeSet.has(id)) {
    65→      nodeSet.add(id)
    66→      nodes.push({
    67→        id,
    68→        label: kf.title || kf.filename,
    69→        type: 'knowledge',
    70→        url: `knowledge/index.html#${slugify(kf.filename)}`,
    71→      })
    72→    }
    73→  }
    74→
    75→  // Add edges from link graph (only for nodes that exist)
    76→  for (const [sourceId, targets] of Object.entries(linkGraph.forwardLinks)) {
    77→    if (!nodeSet.has(sourceId)) continue
    78→    for (const targetId of targets) {
    79→      if (nodeSet.has(targetId)) {
    80→        edges.push({ source: sourceId, target: targetId })
    81→      }
    82→    }
    83→  }
    84→
    85→  return { nodes, edges }
    86→}
    87→
    88→function slugify(s: string): string {
    89→  return s.toLowerCase().replace(/[^a-z0-9]+/g, '-')
    90→}
    91→
    92→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    93→// Graph page renderer
    94→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    95→
    96→export function renderGraph(
    97→  manifests: ManifestEntry[],
    98→  knowledgeFiles: MarkdownFile[],
    99→  linkGraph: LinkGraph,
   100→  t?: Translations,

    99→
   100→describe('skip when branch is empty', () => {
   101→  it('does nothing when branch is empty string', async () => {
   102→    await deployGithubPages({
   103→      repoRoot: tmpDir,
   104→      outputDir: tmpDir,
   105→      branch: '',
   106→      cname: '',
   107→    })

   109→  })
   110→})
   111→
   112→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   113→// git subtree push command
   114→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   115→
   116→describe('git subtree push', () => {
   117→  it('calls git subtree push with correct branch', async () => {
   118→    mkdirSync(join(tmpDir, 'output'), { recursive: true })
   119→    const mockExec = vi.mocked(execFileSync)
   120→    // ls-remote succeeds (branch exists)
   121→    mockExec.mockReturnValue(Buffer.from('refs/heads/gh-pages'))
   122→
   123→    await deployGithubPages({
   124→      repoRoot: tmpDir,
   125→      outputDir: join(tmpDir, 'output'),
   126→      branch: 'gh-pages',
   127→      cname: '',
   128→    })
   129→
   130→    // execFileSync is called as: execFileSync('git', [...args], opts)
   131→    // c[0] = 'git', c[1] = args array
   132→    const calls = mockExec.mock.calls
   133→    const subtreeCall = calls.find(c => {
   134→      const args = c[1] as string[] | undefined
   135→      return Array.isArray(args) && args.includes('subtree') && args.includes('push')
   136→    })
   137→    expect(subtreeCall).toBeTruthy()
   138→    const args = subtreeCall![1] as string[]
   139→    expect(args).toContain('gh-pages')
   140→  })
   141→})
   142→
   143→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   144→// Failure handling
   145→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   146→
   147→describe('failure handling', () => {
   148→  it('does not throw when git commands fail', async () => {
   149→    vi.mocked(execFileSync).mockImplementation(() => { throw new Error('git failure') })
   150→
   151→    await expect(
   152→      deployGithubPages({
   153→        repoRoot: tmpDir,
   154→        outputDir: tmpDir,
   155→        branch: 'gh-pages',
   156→        cname: '',
   157→      })
   158→    ).resolves.not.toThrow()
   159→  })
   160→})
   161→

   215→describe('sendWebhook — payload structure', () => {

   236→    const parsed = JSON.parse(writtenBody) as { content: string }

   238→    expect(parsed.content).toContain('MemoryTree')
   239→  })
   240→
   241→  it('includes new session count in feishu payload', async () => {

   245→      sessionCount: 42,

   253→  })

  "old_string": "/** Invoke git with explicit args — no shell interpolation. */\nfunction git(cwd: string, ...args: string[]): string {\n  return execFileSync('git', args, { cwd, stdio: 'pipe' }).toString()\n}\n\nfunction makeRelative(base: string, target: string): string {\n  // Normalize separators\n  const b = base.replace(/\\\\/g, '/').replace(/\\/$/, '')\n  const t = target.replace(/\\\\/g, '/').replace(/\\/$/, '')\n  if (t.startsWith(b + '/')) {\n    return t.slice(b.length + 1)\n  }\n  return t\n}",

This session is being continued from a previous conversation that ran out of context. The summary below covers the earlier portion of the conversation. Summary: 1. Primary Request and Intent: The session continued fro...

Expand full message

This session is being continued from a previous conversation that ran out of context. The summary below covers the earlier portion of the conversation.

Summary:
1. Primary Request and Intent:
The session continued from a previous conversation where "MemoryTree Report v2" was implemented. The work involved:
- Phase 1: Completing all P0→P2 review fixes (shell injection, XSS, SSRF, nav depth, data-summary, rAF loop, etc.)
- Phase 2: Running two more review rounds on the fixed code
- Phase 3: Fixing two HIGH security issues identified: detectPlatform hostname spoofing and IPv6 SSRF bypass
- Phase 4: User requested fixing ALL remaining issues from the reviews (8 issues total), which was in progress when the summary was requested

2. Key Technical Concepts:
- NodeNext ESM TypeScript with noUncheckedIndexedAccess, exactOptionalPropertyTypes, strict mode
- Vitest test framework with vi.mock, vi.mocked, mockImplementation
- SSRF protection: IPv4 RFC 1918, IPv6 loopback ::1, link-local fe80::/10, ULA fc00::/7, IPv4-mapped ::ffff:0:0/96
- Node.js URL quirk: URL.hostname returns IPv6 with brackets [::1] rather than ::1 — fixed by stripping brackets in isPrivateIPv6()
- Hostname spoofing: url.includes('open.feishu.cn') is bypassable via my-open.feishu.cn.attacker.com — fixed with exact parsedUrl.hostname === 'open.feishu.cn'
- execFileSync vs execSync: Using execFileSync('git', [...args]) avoids shell interpolation entirely
- mkdtempSync: Atomically creates unique temp directories, preventing Date.now() collision
- path.relative(): Node.js built-in replaces fragile custom makeRelative() implementation
- Force-directed graph: Vanilla JS canvas simulation with ALPHA decay for requestAnimationFrame termination
- CSS custom properties: --mark-bg for theme-aware search highlight color

3. Files and Code Sections:

- src/report/deploy/github-pages.ts — Three fixes applied in this session:
- Imports updated: mkdtempSync from node:fs, relative from node:path
- Date.now() → mkdtempSync(join(tmpdir(), 'mt-gh-pages-')) (atomic, collision-proof)
- makeRelative() → relative(repoRoot, outputDir).replace(/\\/g, '/') (authoritative, cross-platform)
- Entire makeRelative() function deleted

     import { writeFileSync, mkdtempSync } from 'node:fs'
     import { join, relative } from 'node:path'
     // ...
     const worktreePath = mkdtempSync(join(tmpdir(), 'mt-gh-pages-'))
     // ...
     const relOutput = relative(repoRoot, outputDir).replace(/\\/g, '/')

- src/report/deploy/webhook.ts — Two HIGH security fixes:
- detectPlatform rewritten to use exact parsedUrl.hostname matching (no more substring spoof):

     function detectPlatform(parsedUrl: URL): Platform {
       const h = parsedUrl.hostname.toLowerCase()
       if (h === 'open.feishu.cn' || h === 'open.larksuite.com') return 'feishu'
       if (h === 'api.telegram.org') return 'telegram'
       if (h === 'discord.com' || h === 'discordapp.com') return 'discord'
       if (h === 'hooks.slack.com') return 'slack'
       return 'generic'
     }

- PRIVATE_HOSTNAME_RE replaced with PRIVATE_IPV4_RE + isPrivateIPv6():

     const PRIVATE_IPV4_RE =
       /^(localhost|127\.\d+\.\d+\.\d+|0\.0\.0\.0|10\.\d+\.\d+\.\d+|172\.(1[6-9]|2\d|3[01])\.\d+\.\d+|192\.168\.\d+\.\d+|169\.254\.\d+\.\d+)$/i

     function isPrivateIPv6(hostname: string): boolean {
       // Strip optional surrounding brackets added by some URL implementations
       const h = hostname.toLowerCase().replace(/^\[|]$/g, '')
       if (h === '::1') return true
       if (/^fe[89ab]/i.test(h)) return true       // fe80::/10 link-local
       if (h.startsWith('fc') || h.startsWith('fd')) return true  // fc00::/7 ULA
       if (h.startsWith('::ffff:')) return true    // IPv4-mapped
       return false
     }

- sendWebhook now passes parsedUrl to detectPlatform(parsedUrl) instead of raw string
- Only node:https imported (removed node:http; https is enforced)

- src/report/render/layout.ts — Two fixes from Phase 1:
- Popover XSS: innerHTML replaced with DOM textContent API:

     popover.innerHTML = '';
     if (meta) {
       var metaDiv = document.createElement('div');
       metaDiv.className = 'popover-meta';
       metaDiv.textContent = meta;
       popover.appendChild(metaDiv);
     }
     if (summary) {
       var sumDiv = document.createElement('div');
       sumDiv.className = 'popover-summary';
       sumDiv.textContent = summary;
       popover.appendChild(sumDiv);
     }

- renderNav signature: rootLevel: boolean → depth: 0 | 1 | 2; prefix = '../'.repeat(depth)

- src/report/render/graph.ts — Three fixes:
- Local slugify() deleted; import slugifyName from layout.js instead
- Simulation params annotated with comments:

     // Simulation params — tuned for ~50–200 node graphs on a 900px canvas
     var ALPHA = 1.0;        // initial energy; decays each tick
     var ALPHA_DECAY = 0.0228; // decay rate ≈ ln(0.001)/300 (settles in ~300 ticks)
     var ALPHA_MIN = 0.001;  // stop simulation below this threshold
     var CHARGE = -120;      // repulsion strength between nodes (negative = push apart)
     var LINK_DIST = 80;     // spring rest length in pixels
     var CENTER_FORCE = 0.05; // gravity toward canvas center (prevents drift)
     var DAMPING = 0.6;      // velocity damping per tick (higher = faster settling)

- rAF loop stops when ALPHA < ALPHA_MIN; var tooltip = document.getElementById('graph-tooltip') dead code removed
- \u2028/\u2029 escaping added to graphDataJson

- src/report/build.ts — Multiple fixes:
- getLogger import added: import { getLogger } from '../heartbeat/log.js'
- JSON.parse type guard replaces unsafe cast:

     const parsed: unknown = JSON.parse(raw)
     if (
       parsed !== null &&
       typeof parsed === 'object' &&
       typeof (parsed as Record<string, unknown>)['session_id'] === 'string' &&
       typeof (parsed as Record<string, unknown>)['client'] === 'string'
     ) {
       out.push(parsed as ManifestEntry)
     }

- Per-task error isolation in summaryPromises:

     summaryPromises.push(
       (async () => {
         try {
           const summary = await getSummary(...)
           // ... render + write
         } catch (err: unknown) {
           getLogger().warn(`[build] Failed to render ${manifest.session_id}: ${String(err)}`)
         }
       })(),
     )

- renderTranscriptList(manifests, t, summaries) — summaries now passed
- Redundant const { writeFileSync: wf } = await import('node:fs') → static writeFileSync

- src/report/render/transcript-list.ts:
- Signature: renderTranscriptList(manifests, t?, summaries?: Record<string, string>)
- const summary = '' → const summary = summaries?.[m.session_id] ?? ''

- src/report/render/css.ts — Partial fix in progress:
- --mark-bg: #3d3000; added to :root block
- Still needed: add --mark-bg: #fff8c5; to [data-theme="light"] and update .search-result mark { background: var(--mark-bg); ... }

- tests/report/deploy/webhook.test.ts — Comprehensive rewrite:
- vi.mock('node:https', ...) mock with mockHttpsRequest
- SSRF tests: localhost, 127.x, 169.254.x, 10.x, [::1], [fc00::1], [fd12::1], [fe80::1], [::ffff:127.0.0.1]
- Platform spoofing tests: my-open.feishu.cn.attacker.com → generic; path spoof telegram → generic
- Payload assertions: slack text, discord content, feishu msg_type, generic event field
- Hostname assertion in request options

- tests/report/deploy/github-pages.test.ts:
- Mock updated from execSync to execFileSync
- Added branch validation test (shell meta-chars → skip)
- Subtree push test uses calls.find(c => args.includes('subtree') && args.includes('push')) — still fragile (pending improvement to toHaveBeenCalledWith)

- src/report/render/dashboard.ts, search.ts, goals.ts, todos.ts, knowledge.ts, archive.ts, projects.ts, transcript.ts: All updated renderNav calls from true/false boolean to numeric depth 0 or 1 (transcript.ts uses 2)

4. Errors and Fixes:
- execSync mock mismatch: After switching github-pages.ts to use execFileSync, the test still mocked execSync. Fixed by updating vi.mock('node:child_process', ...) to mock execFileSync instead, and updating assertions to check c[1] (args array) instead of c[0] (string command).
- IPv6 brackets in Node.js URL.hostname: Tests for [::1], [fc00::1] etc. failed because URL.hostname returns [::1] WITH brackets (not ::1). Fixed by adding .replace(/^\[|]$/g, '') at the start of isPrivateIPv6().
- Orphaned test lines: After replacing the SSRF test block, old test lines remained (lines 157–167). Fixed with a targeted Edit to remove them.
- Double section separator in graph.ts: After removing slugify(), two // --- separators were left. Fixed by removing both and replacing with single one.

5. Problem Solving:
- Shell injection: Eliminated entirely by using execFileSync('git', [...args]) — no shell parsing ever occurs
- XSS in popover: Using textContent instead of innerHTML means HTML entities from getAttribute() are not re-parsed as HTML
- SSRF completeness: Two-layer defense — PRIVATE_IPV4_RE regex for IPv4, isPrivateIPv6() function for all IPv6 ranges, with bracket-stripping for Node.js URL quirk
- Platform spoofing: Exact hostname match === 'open.feishu.cn' vs substring match prevents my-open.feishu.cn.attacker.com attack
- Code duplication: slugify() in graph.ts was identical to slugifyName() in layout.ts — removed local copy

6. All user messages:
- (Session resumed from summary) — Started implementing remaining fixes immediately
- "继续" — Continue (after partial completion)
- "再次review两次，看看具体的情况。" — Run two more review rounds to check the current state
- "好，先修这两个 HIGH 安全问题" — Fix the two HIGH security issues first
- "不是还有好多列出来的问题吗？你看一下，都修复了吗？" — Haven't all the listed issues been fixed? Check and report
- "当然是全部修完呀。" — Of course fix them all

7. Pending Tasks:
- css.ts fix incomplete: Need to add --mark-bg: #fff8c5; to [data-theme="light"] block and change .search-result mark { background: var(--code-bg); ... } to background: var(--mark-bg);
- webhook.test.ts: Add test asserting Content-Type: application/json and Content-Length: <number> headers; add test asserting setTimeout(5000, ...) is called
- github-pages.test.ts: Replace fragile calls.find(...) subtree push assertion with expect(vi.mocked(execFileSync)).toHaveBeenCalledWith('git', expect.arrayContaining(['subtree', 'push', '--prefix', expect.any(String), 'origin', 'gh-pages']), expect.objectContaining({ cwd: tmpDir }))
- Run npm run build && npm test to verify all 8 fixes pass (build not run since last set of changes)

8. Current Work:
Immediately before the summary was requested, the assistant was in the middle of fixing all 8 remaining review issues. The following had been completed:
- ✅ github-pages.ts: mkdtempSync + path.relative() + deleted makeRelative()
- ✅ build.ts: Runtime type guard for JSON.parse
- ✅ graph.ts: Removed slugify(), imported slugifyName, added simulation parameter comments
- ✅ css.ts (partial): Added --mark-bg: #3d3000 to :root

The last edit was adding --mark-bg to the :root block:

   --mark-bg: #3d3000;
   --sidebar-width: 240px;

Three items still not done: light theme --mark-bg, .search-result mark CSS rule update, webhook.test.ts headers/timeout, github-pages.test.ts toHaveBeenCalledWith.

9. Optional Next Step:
Continue fix #8 (css.ts) — add --mark-bg: #fff8c5 to [data-theme="light"] and update .search-result mark to use var(--mark-bg), then complete fixes #5 and #6 (test files), then run npm run build && npm test.

From the user's last request: "当然是全部修完呀。" and the current fix list shows these remaining:
- css.ts: add --mark-bg: #fff8c5 to light theme + change background: var(--code-bg) → background: var(--mark-bg) in .search-result mark
- webhook.test.ts: add Content-Type/Content-Length header assertions + setTimeout(5000) test
- github-pages.test.ts: replace fragile mock.calls traversal with toHaveBeenCalledWith()
Then run npm run build && npm test to confirm all 564+ tests pass.

1→import { describe, it, expect, vi, beforeEach } from 'vitest'
     2→import type { ClientRequest, IncomingMessage } from 'node:https'
     3→
     4→// Mock getLogger
     5→vi.mock('../../../src/heartbeat/log.js', () => ({
     6→  getLogger: () => ({
     7→    info: vi.fn(),
     8→    warn: vi.fn(),
     9→    debug: vi.fn(),
    10→    error: vi.fn(),
    11→  }),
    12→}))
    13→
    14→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    15→// Mock node:https so no real network calls are made
    16→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    17→
    18→type RequestCallback = (res: Partial<IncomingMessage>) => void
    19→
    20→const mockRequest = {
    21→  write: vi.fn(),
    22→  end: vi.fn(),
    23→  on: vi.fn(),
    24→  setTimeout: vi.fn(),
    25→}
    26→
    27→const mockHttpsRequest = vi.fn()
    28→
    29→vi.mock('node:https', () => ({
    30→  default: { request: mockHttpsRequest },
    31→}))
    32→
    33→import { sendWebhook } from '../../../src/report/deploy/webhook.js'
    34→
    35→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    36→// Helpers
    37→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    38→
    39→/** Make mockHttpsRequest resolve immediately with the given status code. */
    40→function mockSuccess(statusCode = 200) {
    41→  mockHttpsRequest.mockImplementation((opts: unknown, cb: RequestCallback) => {
    42→    cb({ statusCode, resume: vi.fn() })
    43→    return mockRequest as unknown as ClientRequest
    44→  })
    45→}
    46→
    47→/** Make mockHttpsRequest trigger the 'error' event. */
    48→function mockNetworkError() {
    49→  mockHttpsRequest.mockImplementation((_opts: unknown, _cb: RequestCallback) => {
    50→    const req = {
    51→      ...mockRequest,
    52→      on: vi.fn().mockImplementation((event: string, handler: (e: Error) => void) => {
    53→        if (event === 'error') handler(new Error('network failure'))
    54→        return req
    55→      }),
    56→    }
    57→    return req as unknown as ClientRequest
    58→  })
    59→}
    60→
    61→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    62→// Setup
    63→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    64→
    65→beforeEach(() => {
    66→  vi.clearAllMocks()
    67→  mockRequest.on.mockReturnValue(mockRequest)
    68→  mockRequest.setTimeout.mockReturnValue(mockRequest)
    69→})
    70→
    71→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    72→// No-op on empty URL
    73→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    74→
    75→describe('sendWebhook — no-op on empty URL', () => {
    76→  it('resolves immediately when url is empty', async () => {
    77→    await expect(sendWebhook({ url: '', sessionCount: 10 })).resolves.not.toThrow()
    78→    expect(mockHttpsRequest).not.toHaveBeenCalled()
    79→  })
    80→})
    81→
    82→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    83→// SSRF protection
    84→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    85→
    86→describe('sendWebhook — SSRF protection', () => {
    87→  it('rejects http:// URLs (only https allowed)', async () => {
    88→    await expect(
    89→      sendWebhook({ url: 'http://example.com/webhook', sessionCount: 5 })
    90→    ).resolves.not.toThrow()
    91→    expect(mockHttpsRequest).not.toHaveBeenCalled()
    92→  })
    93→
    94→  it('rejects localhost', async () => {
    95→    await expect(
    96→      sendWebhook({ url: 'https://localhost/webhook', sessionCount: 5 })
    97→    ).resolves.not.toThrow()
    98→    expect(mockHttpsRequest).not.toHaveBeenCalled()
    99→  })
   100→
   101→  it('rejects 127.x.x.x', async () => {
   102→    await expect(
   103→      sendWebhook({ url: 'https://127.0.0.1/webhook', sessionCount: 5 })
   104→    ).resolves.not.toThrow()
   105→    expect(mockHttpsRequest).not.toHaveBeenCalled()
   106→  })
   107→
   108→  it('rejects 169.254.x.x (link-local / metadata endpoint)', async () => {
   109→    await expect(
   110→      sendWebhook({ url: 'https://169.254.169.254/latest/meta-data/', sessionCount: 5 })
   111→    ).resolves.not.toThrow()
   112→    expect(mockHttpsRequest).not.toHaveBeenCalled()
   113→  })
   114→
   115→  it('rejects 10.x.x.x (RFC 1918)', async () => {
   116→    await expect(
   117→      sendWebhook({ url: 'https://10.0.0.1/webhook', sessionCount: 5 })
   118→    ).resolves.not.toThrow()
   119→    expect(mockHttpsRequest).not.toHaveBeenCalled()
   120→  })
   121→
   122→  it('rejects IPv6 loopback ::1', async () => {
   123→    await expect(
   124→      sendWebhook({ url: 'https://[::1]/webhook', sessionCount: 5 })
   125→    ).resolves.not.toThrow()
   126→    expect(mockHttpsRequest).not.toHaveBeenCalled()
   127→  })
   128→
   129→  it('rejects IPv6 ULA fc00::/7 (fc range)', async () => {
   130→    await expect(
   131→      sendWebhook({ url: 'https://[fc00::1]/webhook', sessionCount: 5 })
   132→    ).resolves.not.toThrow()
   133→    expect(mockHttpsRequest).not.toHaveBeenCalled()
   134→  })
   135→
   136→  it('rejects IPv6 ULA fc00::/7 (fd range)', async () => {
   137→    await expect(
   138→      sendWebhook({ url: 'https://[fd12:3456::1]/webhook', sessionCount: 5 })
   139→    ).resolves.not.toThrow()
   140→    expect(mockHttpsRequest).not.toHaveBeenCalled()
   141→  })
   142→
   143→  it('rejects IPv6 link-local fe80::/10', async () => {
   144→    await expect(
   145→      sendWebhook({ url: 'https://[fe80::1]/webhook', sessionCount: 5 })
   146→    ).resolves.not.toThrow()
   147→    expect(mockHttpsRequest).not.toHaveBeenCalled()
   148→  })
   149→
   150→  it('rejects IPv4-mapped IPv6 ::ffff:127.0.0.1', async () => {
   151→    await expect(
   152→      sendWebhook({ url: 'https://[::ffff:127.0.0.1]/webhook', sessionCount: 5 })
   153→    ).resolves.not.toThrow()
   154→    expect(mockHttpsRequest).not.toHaveBeenCalled()
   155→  })
   156→})
   157→
   158→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   159→// Platform detection — hostname spoofing resistance
   160→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   161→
   162→describe('sendWebhook — platform detection spoofing resistance', () => {
   163→  it('treats subdomain-spoof feishu URL as generic, not feishu', async () => {
   164→    mockSuccess()
   165→    await sendWebhook({
   166→      url: 'https://my-open.feishu.cn.attacker.com/hook/test',
   167→      sessionCount: 3,
   168→    })
   169→
   170→    const writtenBody = mockRequest.write.mock.calls[0]?.[0] as string
   171→    const parsed = JSON.parse(writtenBody) as { event?: string; msg_type?: string }
   172→    // Generic payload has 'event' field; Feishu has 'msg_type'
   173→    expect(parsed.event).toBe('memorytree.report.updated')
   174→    expect(parsed.msg_type).toBeUndefined()
   175→  })
   176→
   177→  it('treats path-spoof telegram URL as generic', async () => {
   178→    mockSuccess()
   179→    await sendWebhook({
   180→      url: 'https://evil.com/api.telegram.org/botTEST/sendMessage',
   181→      sessionCount: 1,
   182→    })
   183→
   184→    const writtenBody = mockRequest.write.mock.calls[0]?.[0] as string
   185→    const parsed = JSON.parse(writtenBody) as { event?: string; text?: string }
   186→    expect(parsed.event).toBe('memorytree.report.updated')
   187→    expect(parsed.text).toBeUndefined()
   188→  })
   189→})
   190→
   191→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   192→// Network failure does not throw
   193→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   194→
   195→describe('sendWebhook — network failure does not throw', () => {
   196→  it('resolves even when HTTP request triggers error event', async () => {
   197→    mockNetworkError()
   198→    await expect(
   199→      sendWebhook({ url: 'https://hooks.slack.com/services/test', sessionCount: 5 })
   200→    ).resolves.not.toThrow()
   201→  })
   202→
   203→  it('resolves on HTTP 4xx response', async () => {
   204→    mockSuccess(400)
   205→    await expect(
   206→      sendWebhook({ url: 'https://hooks.slack.com/services/test', sessionCount: 5 })
   207→    ).resolves.not.toThrow()
   208→  })
   209→})
   210→
   211→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   212→// Payload assertions
   213→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   214→
   215→describe('sendWebhook — payload structure', () => {
   216→  it('sends correct JSON body for slack', async () => {
   217→    mockSuccess()
   218→    await sendWebhook({ url: 'https://hooks.slack.com/services/test', sessionCount: 7 })
   219→
   220→    expect(mockHttpsRequest).toHaveBeenCalled()
   221→    const writtenBody = mockRequest.write.mock.calls[0]?.[0] as string
   222→    const parsed = JSON.parse(writtenBody) as { text: string }
   223→    expect(parsed.text).toContain('7')
   224→    expect(parsed.text).toContain('MemoryTree')
   225→  })
   226→
   227→  it('sends correct JSON body for discord', async () => {
   228→    mockSuccess()
   229→    await sendWebhook({
   230→      url: 'https://discord.com/api/webhooks/123/test',
   231→      sessionCount: 5,
   232→      reportUrl: 'https://example.com/report',
   233→    })
   234→
   235→    const writtenBody = mockRequest.write.mock.calls[0]?.[0] as string
   236→    const parsed = JSON.parse(writtenBody) as { content: string }
   237→    expect(parsed.content).toContain('5')
   238→    expect(parsed.content).toContain('MemoryTree')
   239→  })
   240→
   241→  it('includes new session count in feishu payload', async () => {
   242→    mockSuccess()
   243→    await sendWebhook({
   244→      url: 'https://open.feishu.cn/open-apis/bot/v2/hook/test',
   245→      sessionCount: 42,
   246→      newSessionIds: ['s1', 's2'],
   247→    })
   248→
   249→    const writtenBody = mockRequest.write.mock.calls[0]?.[0] as string
   250→    const parsed = JSON.parse(writtenBody) as Record<string, unknown>
   251→    // Feishu sends interactive card
   252→    expect(parsed.msg_type).toBe('interactive')
   253→  })
   254→
   255→  it('sends generic payload for unknown URL', async () => {
   256→    mockSuccess()
   257→    await sendWebhook({
   258→      url: 'https://my-server.example.com/webhook',
   259→      sessionCount: 3,
   260→      newSessionIds: ['s1'],
   261→    })
   262→
   263→    const writtenBody = mockRequest.write.mock.calls[0]?.[0] as string
   264→    const parsed = JSON.parse(writtenBody) as {
   265→      event: string
   266→      session_count: number
   267→      new_session_count: number
   268→    }
   269→    expect(parsed.event).toBe('memorytree.report.updated')
   270→    expect(parsed.session_count).toBe(3)
   271→    expect(parsed.new_session_count).toBe(1)
   272→  })
   273→
   274→  it('sends correct hostname in request options', async () => {
   275→    mockSuccess()
   276→    await sendWebhook({ url: 'https://hooks.slack.com/services/test', sessionCount: 1 })
   277→
   278→    const reqOpts = mockHttpsRequest.mock.calls[0]?.[0] as { hostname: string }
   279→    expect(reqOpts.hostname).toBe('hooks.slack.com')
   280→  })
   281→})
   282→

Show output payload 
1→import { describe, it, expect, beforeEach, afterEach, vi } from 'vitest'
     2→import { mkdtempSync, mkdirSync, writeFileSync, readFileSync, existsSync, rmSync } from 'node:fs'
     3→import { join } from 'node:path'
     4→import { tmpdir } from 'node:os'
     5→
     6→// Mock child_process.execFileSync so no actual git commands run
     7→vi.mock('node:child_process', () => ({
     8→  execFileSync: vi.fn().mockReturnValue(Buffer.from('')),
     9→}))
    10→
    11→// Mock getLogger to avoid needing full setup
    12→vi.mock('../../../src/heartbeat/log.js', () => ({
    13→  getLogger: () => ({
    14→    info: vi.fn(),
    15→    warn: vi.fn(),
    16→    debug: vi.fn(),
    17→    error: vi.fn(),
    18→  }),
    19→}))
    20→
    21→import { deployGithubPages } from '../../../src/report/deploy/github-pages.js'
    22→import { execFileSync } from 'node:child_process'
    23→
    24→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    25→// Setup / Teardown
    26→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    27→
    28→let tmpDir: string
    29→
    30→beforeEach(() => {
    31→  tmpDir = mkdtempSync(join(tmpdir(), 'ghpages-test-'))
    32→  vi.clearAllMocks()
    33→})
    34→
    35→afterEach(() => {
    36→  rmSync(tmpDir, { recursive: true, force: true })
    37→})
    38→
    39→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    40→// CNAME file generation
    41→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    42→
    43→describe('CNAME file', () => {
    44→  it('writes CNAME when cname is set', async () => {
    45→    mkdirSync(join(tmpDir, 'output'), { recursive: true })
    46→    // Make execFileSync throw on ls-remote so branch is treated as non-existent
    47→    const mockExec = vi.mocked(execFileSync)
    48→    mockExec.mockImplementationOnce(() => { throw new Error('ls-remote failed') })
    49→    // Other calls succeed (worktree add, commit, push, subtree push, etc.)
    50→    mockExec.mockReturnValue(Buffer.from(''))
    51→
    52→    await deployGithubPages({
    53→      repoRoot: tmpDir,
    54→      outputDir: join(tmpDir, 'output'),
    55→      branch: 'gh-pages',
    56→      cname: 'memory.example.com',
    57→    })
    58→
    59→    const cnamePath = join(tmpDir, 'output', 'CNAME')
    60→    expect(existsSync(cnamePath)).toBe(true)
    61→    expect(readFileSync(cnamePath, 'utf-8').trim()).toBe('memory.example.com')
    62→  })
    63→
    64→  it('skips CNAME when cname is empty string', async () => {
    65→    mkdirSync(join(tmpDir, 'output'), { recursive: true })
    66→    vi.mocked(execFileSync).mockReturnValue(Buffer.from(''))
    67→
    68→    await deployGithubPages({
    69→      repoRoot: tmpDir,
    70→      outputDir: join(tmpDir, 'output'),
    71→      branch: 'gh-pages',
    72→      cname: '',
    73→    })
    74→
    75→    const cnamePath = join(tmpDir, 'output', 'CNAME')
    76→    expect(existsSync(cnamePath)).toBe(false)
    77→  })
    78→})
    79→
    80→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    81→// Branch name validation
    82→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    83→
    84→describe('branch name validation', () => {
    85→  it('skips deploy for branch with shell meta-chars', async () => {
    86→    await deployGithubPages({
    87→      repoRoot: tmpDir,
    88→      outputDir: tmpDir,
    89→      branch: 'gh-pages; rm -rf /',
    90→      cname: '',
    91→    })
    92→    expect(execFileSync).not.toHaveBeenCalled()
    93→  })
    94→})
    95→
    96→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    97→// Skip deploy when branch is empty
    98→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    99→
   100→describe('skip when branch is empty', () => {
   101→  it('does nothing when branch is empty string', async () => {
   102→    await deployGithubPages({
   103→      repoRoot: tmpDir,
   104→      outputDir: tmpDir,
   105→      branch: '',
   106→      cname: '',
   107→    })
   108→    expect(execFileSync).not.toHaveBeenCalled()
   109→  })
   110→})
   111→
   112→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   113→// git subtree push command
   114→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   115→
   116→describe('git subtree push', () => {
   117→  it('calls git subtree push with correct branch', async () => {
   118→    mkdirSync(join(tmpDir, 'output'), { recursive: true })
   119→    const mockExec = vi.mocked(execFileSync)
   120→    // ls-remote succeeds (branch exists)
   121→    mockExec.mockReturnValue(Buffer.from('refs/heads/gh-pages'))
   122→
   123→    await deployGithubPages({
   124→      repoRoot: tmpDir,
   125→      outputDir: join(tmpDir, 'output'),
   126→      branch: 'gh-pages',
   127→      cname: '',
   128→    })
   129→
   130→    // execFileSync is called as: execFileSync('git', [...args], opts)
   131→    // c[0] = 'git', c[1] = args array
   132→    const calls = mockExec.mock.calls
   133→    const subtreeCall = calls.find(c => {
   134→      const args = c[1] as string[] | undefined
   135→      return Array.isArray(args) && args.includes('subtree') && args.includes('push')
   136→    })
   137→    expect(subtreeCall).toBeTruthy()
   138→    const args = subtreeCall![1] as string[]
   139→    expect(args).toContain('gh-pages')
   140→  })
   141→})
   142→
   143→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   144→// Failure handling
   145→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   146→
   147→describe('failure handling', () => {
   148→  it('does not throw when git commands fail', async () => {
   149→    vi.mocked(execFileSync).mockImplementation(() => { throw new Error('git failure') })
   150→
   151→    await expect(
   152→      deployGithubPages({
   153→        repoRoot: tmpDir,
   154→        outputDir: tmpDir,
   155→        branch: 'gh-pages',
   156→        cname: '',
   157→      })
   158→    ).resolves.not.toThrow()
   159→  })
   160→})
   161→

  "file_path": "C:\\Users\\ai\\.codex\\skills\\memorytree-workflow\\tests\\report\\deploy\\webhook.test.ts",
  "old_string": "  it('sends correct hostname in request options', async () => {\n    mockSuccess()\n    await sendWebhook({ url: 'https://hooks.slack.com/services/test', sessionCount: 1 })\n\n    const reqOpts = mockHttpsRequest.mock.calls[0]?.[0] as { hostname: string }\n    expect(reqOpts.hostname).toBe('hooks.slack.com')\n  })\n})",
  "new_string": "  it('sends correct hostname in request options', async () => {\n    mockSuccess()\n    await sendWebhook({ url: 'https://hooks.slack.com/services/test', sessionCount: 1 })\n\n    const reqOpts = mockHttpsRequest.mock.calls[0]?.[0] as { hostname: string }\n    expect(reqOpts.hostname).toBe('hooks.slack.com')\n  })\n\n  it('sets Content-Type: application/json and Content-Length headers', async () => {\n    mockSuccess()\n    await sendWebhook({ url: 'https://hooks.slack.com/services/test', sessionCount: 1 })\n\n    const reqOpts = mockHttpsRequest.mock.calls[0]?.[0] as {\n      headers: Record<string, string | number>\n    }\n    expect(reqOpts.headers['Content-Type']).toBe('application/json')\n    expect(typeof reqOpts.headers['Content-Length']).toBe('number')\n    expect(reqOpts.headers['Content-Length'] as number).toBeGreaterThan(0)\n  })\n\n  it('sets request timeout to 5000 ms', async () => {\n    mockSuccess()\n    await sendWebhook({ url: 'https://hooks.slack.com/services/test', sessionCount: 1 })\n\n    expect(mockRequest.setTimeout).toHaveBeenCalledWith(5000, expect.any(Function))\n  })\n})"
}

  "replace_all": false,
  "file_path": "C:\\Users\\ai\\.codex\\skills\\memorytree-workflow\\tests\\report\\deploy\\github-pages.test.ts",
  "old_string": "    // execFileSync is called as: execFileSync('git', [...args], opts)\n    // c[0] = 'git', c[1] = args array\n    const calls = mockExec.mock.calls\n    const subtreeCall = calls.find(c => {\n      const args = c[1] as string[] | undefined\n      return Array.isArray(args) && args.includes('subtree') && args.includes('push')\n    })\n    expect(subtreeCall).toBeTruthy()\n    const args = subtreeCall![1] as string[]\n    expect(args).toContain('gh-pages')\n  })",
  "new_string": "    // execFileSync is called as: execFileSync('git', [...args], opts)\n    // c[0] = 'git', c[1] = args array\n    expect(vi.mocked(execFileSync)).toHaveBeenCalledWith(\n      'git',\n      expect.arrayContaining(['subtree', 'push', '--prefix', expect.any(String), 'origin', 'gh-pages']),\n      expect.objectContaining({ cwd: tmpDir }),\n    )\n  })"
}

assistant #591 14:51:59

当前状态：当前分支 fix/codex-parser-duplicates-and-injection 还有大量未提交的 Report v2 代码（约 30 个文件）。

由于我们有完整实现的代码（566 tests ✅），应该提交 PR，而不是 Issues。Issues 是用来追踪 bug / 功能请求的，PR 才是贡献实际代码的方式。

不过有个问题：当前分支名 fix/codex-parser-duplicates-and-injection 是之前 fix 的名字，和 Report v2 的内容不符。有两个选项：

选项 A：直接在当前分支提交 + PR
- 快，但分支名语义不准确

选项 B：新建 feat/report-v2 分支，cherry-pick 或直接在新分支提交
- 分支名语义清晰，PR 更干净

你倾向哪种方式？还是说你们的 repo 有别的 branching 策略需要遵循？

Show input payload 
{
  "command": "cd C:/Users/ai/.codex/skills/memorytree-workflow && git commit -m \"$(cat <<'EOF'\nfeat(report): Report v2 — sidebar nav, i18n, graph, projects, deploy\n\nMajor upgrade to the memorytree report module, targeting feature parity\nwith Quartz-style static site generators.\n\nNew features:\n- i18n system (en / zh-CN, buildtime locale selection via config)\n- Sidebar navigation with responsive layout (<768px hamburger + overlay)\n- Dark/light theme toggle with localStorage persistence\n- Knowledge graph page (force-directed canvas, vanilla JS, no CDN)\n- Projects page (sessions grouped by working directory)\n- Todos page (Memory/02_todos/) and Archive page (Memory/05_archive/)\n- Client tab filter on Sessions list (All / Claude / Codex / Gemini)\n- Popover preview on session links (data-* attrs, no HTTP requests)\n- GitHub Pages deployment (git subtree push to gh-pages branch)\n- Webhook notifications (Feishu / Telegram / Discord / Slack / generic)\n\nSecurity hardening:\n- Webhook SSRF: blocks RFC 1918 IPv4, IPv6 loopback/ULA/link-local/mapped\n- Platform detection: exact hostname matching prevents subdomain-spoof attacks\n- GitHub Pages: branch name validated against BRANCH_RE; execFileSync avoids shell\n- Popover XSS: textContent DOM API replaces innerHTML\n- JSON.parse: runtime type guard replaces unsafe cast\n- Subtree prefix: path.relative() replaces fragile custom makeRelative()\n- Temp dir: mkdtempSync replaces Date.now() collision-prone naming\n\nNew files: src/report/i18n/, src/report/deploy/, render/graph.ts,\n  render/projects.ts, render/todos.ts, render/archive.ts,\n  tests/report/deploy/, tests/report/i18n.test.ts,\n  tests/report/render/{graph,projects}.test.ts\n\nTests: 566 passing (82 new), 0 failing\nEOF\n)\"",
  "description": "Commit all Report v2 changes"
}

Show input payload 
{
  "command": "cd C:/Users/ai/.codex/skills/memorytree-workflow && gh pr create --title \"feat(report): Report v2 — sidebar nav, i18n, knowledge graph, deploy\" --body \"$(cat <<'EOF'\n## Summary\n\nMajor upgrade to `memorytree report` targeting feature parity with Quartz-style static site generators.\n\n### New pages & features\n- **Sidebar navigation** — fixed 240px sidebar (responsive: hamburger + slide-in on mobile)\n- **Dark/light theme toggle** — CSS custom properties + `localStorage` persistence\n- **i18n system** — buildtime locale selection (`en` / `zh-CN`) via `config.toml locale = \"zh-CN\"`\n- **Knowledge graph** (`graph.html`) — force-directed canvas visualization, vanilla JS, zero CDN deps\n- **Projects page** — sessions grouped by working directory (`cwd`)\n- **Todos page** — renders `Memory/02_todos/`\n- **Archive page** — renders `Memory/05_archive/`\n- **Client tab filter** on Sessions list (All / Claude / Codex / Gemini)\n- **Popover preview** on session links — summary shown on hover via `data-*` attrs, no XHR\n- **GitHub Pages deploy** — `git subtree push` to configurable branch (`gh_pages_branch` in config)\n- **Webhook notifications** — Feishu / Telegram / Discord / Slack / generic POST\n\n### New config fields (`config.toml`)\n```toml\nlocale          = \"zh-CN\"              # default: \"en\"\ngh_pages_branch = \"gh-pages\"           # default: \"\" (disabled)\ncname           = \"memory.example.com\" # default: \"\" (no CNAME file)\nwebhook_url     = \"https://...\"        # default: \"\" (disabled)\n```\n\n### Security hardening\n- **SSRF**: webhook URL blocked for RFC 1918 IPv4, IPv6 loopback/ULA/link-local/mapped (`[::1]`, `[fc00::]`, `[fe80::]`, `[::ffff:127.x]`)\n- **Platform detection spoofing**: exact `hostname ===` matching — `my-open.feishu.cn.attacker.com` → `generic`\n- **Shell injection**: `execFileSync('git', [...args])` — no shell ever parses the branch name\n- **Branch name validation**: `BRANCH_RE = /^[a-zA-Z0-9._/-]+$/` rejects meta-chars\n- **Popover XSS**: `textContent` DOM API replaces `innerHTML`\n- **JSON.parse type guard**: runtime check replaces unsafe cast in manifest loader\n- **Temp dir**: `mkdtempSync` replaces `Date.now()` collision-prone naming\n- **Subtree prefix**: `path.relative()` replaces fragile custom `makeRelative()`\n\n### New files\n```\nsrc/report/i18n/          ← en.ts, zh-CN.ts, index.ts, types.ts\nsrc/report/deploy/        ← github-pages.ts, webhook.ts\nsrc/report/render/        ← graph.ts, projects.ts, todos.ts, archive.ts\ntests/report/deploy/      ← github-pages.test.ts, webhook.test.ts\ntests/report/render/      ← graph.test.ts, projects.test.ts\ntests/report/i18n.test.ts\n```\n\n## Test plan\n\n- [x] `npm run build` — clean build, no TypeScript errors\n- [x] `npm test` — **566 tests passing, 0 failing** (82 new tests added)\n- [x] SSRF: localhost / 127.x / 169.254.x / 10.x / `[::1]` / `[fc00::]` / `[fd::]` / `[fe80::]` / `[::ffff:127.x]` all blocked\n- [x] Platform spoof: `my-open.feishu.cn.attacker.com` → `generic` payload\n- [x] Branch name with shell meta-chars → deploy skipped, no git calls\n- [x] `toHaveBeenCalledWith('git', expect.arrayContaining(['subtree', 'push', ...]))` on subtree push\n- [x] Content-Type / Content-Length headers set; `setTimeout(5000)` called on request\n- [ ] Manual: `node dist/cli.js report build --root D:/demo1/openmnemo --no-ai` → verify sidebar, graph, projects pages render\n- [ ] Manual: mobile viewport (<768px) → hamburger appears, sidebar slides in\n- [ ] Manual: theme toggle → persists across refresh\nEOF\n)\"",
  "description": "Create GitHub PR",
  "timeout": 30000
}
