Read output=1→/** 2→ * Minimal Markdown → HTML converter. 3→ * Handles: headings, bold, italic, inline code, fenced code blocks, lists. 4→ * All content is HTML-escaped before applying stru...
Show output payload 
1→/**
     2→ * Minimal Markdown → HTML converter.
     3→ * Handles: headings, bold, italic, inline code, fenced code blocks, lists.
     4→ * All content is HTML-escaped before applying structural tags.
     5→ */
     6→
     7→import { escHtml } from './layout.js'
     8→
     9→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    10→// Main converter
    11→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    12→
    13→export function markdownToHtml(md: string): string {
    14→  const lines = md.split('\n')
    15→  const output: string[] = []
    16→  let i = 0
    17→  let inList = false
    18→  let listType: 'ul' | 'ol' | null = null
    19→
    20→  function closeList() {
    21→    if (inList) {
    22→      output.push(listType === 'ol' ? '</ol>' : '</ul>')
    23→      inList = false
    24→      listType = null
    25→    }
    26→  }
    27→
    28→  while (i < lines.length) {
    29→    const line = lines[i] ?? ''
    30→
    31→    // Fenced code block
    32→    if (line.startsWith('```')) {
    33→      closeList()
    34→      const lang = line.slice(3).trim()
    35→      const codeLines: string[] = []
    36→      i++
    37→      while (i < lines.length && !(lines[i] ?? '').startsWith('```')) {
    38→        codeLines.push(lines[i] ?? '')
    39→        i++
    40→      }
    41→      output.push(`<pre><code${lang ? ` class="language-${escHtml(lang)}"` : ''}>${escHtml(codeLines.join('\n'))}</code></pre>`)
    42→      i++
    43→      continue
    44→    }
    45→
    46→    // YAML front matter (skip)
    47→    if (i === 0 && line === '---') {
    48→      i++
    49→      while (i < lines.length && (lines[i] ?? '') !== '---') i++
    50→      i++ // skip closing ---
    51→      continue
    52→    }
    53→
    54→    // Headings
    55→    const headingMatch = line.match(/^(#{1,6})\s+(.*)$/)
    56→    if (headingMatch) {
    57→      closeList()
    58→      const level = headingMatch[1]?.length ?? 1
    59→      const text = headingMatch[2] ?? ''
    60→      output.push(`<h${level}>${applyInline(text)}</h${level}>`)
    61→      i++
    62→      continue
    63→    }
    64→
    65→    // Horizontal rule
    66→    if (/^---+$/.test(line.trim())) {
    67→      closeList()
    68→      output.push('<hr>')
    69→      i++
    70→      continue
    71→    }
    72→
    73→    // Ordered list
    74→    const olMatch = line.match(/^(\s*)\d+\.\s+(.+)$/)
    75→    if (olMatch) {
    76→      if (!inList || listType !== 'ol') {
    77→        closeList()
    78→        output.push('<ol>')
    79→        inList = true
    80→        listType = 'ol'
    81→      }
    82→      output.push(`<li>${applyInline(olMatch[2] ?? '')}</li>`)
    83→      i++
    84→      continue
    85→    }
    86→
    87→    // Unordered list
    88→    const ulMatch = line.match(/^(\s*)[-*+]\s+(.+)$/)
    89→    if (ulMatch) {
    90→      if (!inList || listType !== 'ul') {
    91→        closeList()
    92→        output.push('<ul>')
    93→        inList = true
    94→        listType = 'ul'
    95→      }
    96→      output.push(`<li>${applyInline(ulMatch[2] ?? '')}</li>`)
    97→      i++
    98→      continue
    99→    }
   100→
   101→    // Blank line
   102→    if (!line.trim()) {
   103→      closeList()
   104→      i++
   105→      continue
   106→    }
   107→
   108→    // Paragraph
   109→    closeList()
   110→    output.push(`<p>${applyInline(line)}</p>`)
   111→    i++
   112→  }
   113→
   114→  closeList()
   115→  return output.join('\n')
   116→}
   117→
   118→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   119→// Inline formatting
   120→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   121→
   122→function applyInline(text: string): string {
   123→  // Process links before HTML-escaping to avoid double-escaping href/label
   124→  const withLinks = text.replace(/\[([^\]]+)\]\(([^)]+)\)/g, (_, label: string, href: string) => {
   125→    return `<a href="${escHtml(href)}" target="_blank" rel="noopener">${escHtml(label)}</a>`
   126→  })
   127→  // Escape remaining text, then apply inline patterns on already-safe content
   128→  // Split on the <a> tags we just inserted so they are not re-escaped
   129→  const parts = withLinks.split(/(<a [^>]+>.*?<\/a>)/g)
   130→  const processed = parts
   131→    .map((part, i) => {
   132→      if (i % 2 === 1) return part // already-generated <a> tag, pass through
   133→      const escaped = escHtml(part)
   134→      return escaped
   135→        .replace(/\*\*(.+?)\*\*/g, '<strong>$1</strong>')
   136→        .replace(/\*(.+?)\*/g, '<em>$1</em>')
   137→        .replace(/__(.+?)__/g, '<strong>$1</strong>')
   138→        .replace(/_(.+?)_/g, '<em>$1</em>')
   139→        .replace(/`([^`]+)`/g, '<code>$1</code>')
   140→    })
   141→    .join('')
   142→  return processed
   143→}
   144→

Tool Call #734 Write toolu_017VWLvz9mu6WaN9jX8wFF27 15:32:39

Show input payload 
{
  "file_path": "C:\\Users\\ai\\.codex\\skills\\memorytree-workflow\\src\\report\\render\\markdown.ts",
  "content": "/**\n * Minimal Markdown → HTML converter.\n * Handles: headings, bold, italic, inline code, fenced code blocks,\n *          lists, blockquotes, callouts (> [!type]), mermaid diagrams.\n * All content is HTML-escaped before applying structural tags.\n */\n\nimport { escHtml } from './layout.js'\n\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n// Callout configuration\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\nconst CALLOUT_ICONS: Record<string, string> = {\n  note:      '📝',\n  tip:       '💡',\n  important: '❗',\n  warning:   '⚠️',\n  caution:   '🔥',\n  info:      'ℹ️',\n  success:   '✅',\n  error:     '❌',\n}\n\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n// Mermaid detection (separate from conversion)\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n/** Returns true if the markdown source contains at least one ```mermaid block. */\nexport function hasMermaidBlocks(md: string): boolean {\n  return /^```mermaid\\s*$/m.test(md)\n}\n\n/**\n * CDN script tag to inject on pages containing mermaid diagrams.\n * Pinned to mermaid@11 via jsDelivr with SRI integrity hash.\n */\nexport const MERMAID_CDN_SCRIPT = `<script type=\"module\">\nimport mermaid from 'https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/npm/mermaid@11/dist/mermaid.esm.min.mjs';\nmermaid.initialize({ startOnLoad: true, theme: document.documentElement.getAttribute('data-theme') === 'light' ? 'default' : 'dark' });\n</script>`\n\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n// Main converter\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\nexport function markdownToHtml(md: string): string {\n  const lines = md.split('\\n')\n  const output: string[] = []\n  let i = 0\n  let inList = false\n  let listType: 'ul' | 'ol' | null = null\n\n  function closeList() {\n    if (inList) {\n      output.push(listType === 'ol' ? '</ol>' : '</ul>')\n      inList = false\n      listType = null\n    }\n  }\n\n  while (i < lines.length) {\n    const line = lines[i] ?? ''\n\n    // Fenced code block (including mermaid)\n    if (line.startsWith('```')) {\n      closeList()\n      const lang = line.slice(3).trim()\n      const codeLines: string[] = []\n      i++\n      while (i < lines.length && !(lines[i] ?? '').startsWith('```')) {\n        codeLines.push(lines[i] ?? '')\n        i++\n      }\n      const codeContent = codeLines.join('\\n')\n      if (lang === 'mermaid') {\n        // Mermaid: render as <pre class=\"mermaid\"> — processed client-side by mermaid.js\n        output.push(`<pre class=\"mermaid\">${escHtml(codeContent)}</pre>`)\n      } else {\n        output.push(`<pre><code${lang ? ` class=\"language-${escHtml(lang)}\"` : ''}>${escHtml(codeContent)}</code></pre>`)\n      }\n      i++\n      continue\n    }\n\n    // YAML front matter (skip)\n    if (i === 0 && line === '---') {\n      i++\n      while (i < lines.length && (lines[i] ?? '') !== '---') i++\n      i++ // skip closing ---\n      continue\n    }\n\n    // Headings\n    const headingMatch = line.match(/^(#{1,6})\\s+(.*)$/)\n    if (headingMatch) {\n      closeList()\n      const level = headingMatch[1]?.length ?? 1\n      const text = headingMatch[2] ?? ''\n      output.push(`<h${level}>${applyInline(text)}</h${level}>`)\n      i++\n      continue\n    }\n\n    // Horizontal rule\n    if (/^---+$/.test(line.trim())) {\n      closeList()\n      output.push('<hr>')\n      i++\n      continue\n    }\n\n    // Ordered list\n    const olMatch = line.match(/^(\\s*)\\d+\\.\\s+(.+)$/)\n    if (olMatch) {\n      if (!inList || listType !== 'ol') {\n        closeList()\n        output.push('<ol>')\n        inList = true\n        listType = 'ol'\n      }\n      output.push(`<li>${applyInline(olMatch[2] ?? '')}</li>`)\n      i++\n      continue\n    }\n\n    // Unordered list\n    const ulMatch = line.match(/^(\\s*)[-*+]\\s+(.+)$/)\n    if (ulMatch) {\n      if (!inList || listType !== 'ul') {\n        closeList()\n        output.push('<ul>')\n        inList = true\n        listType = 'ul'\n      }\n      output.push(`<li>${applyInline(ulMatch[2] ?? '')}</li>`)\n      i++\n      continue\n    }\n\n    // Blockquote / Callout\n    if (line.startsWith('> ') || line === '>') {\n      closeList()\n      const bqLines: string[] = []\n      while (i < lines.length && ((lines[i] ?? '').startsWith('> ') || (lines[i] ?? '') === '>')) {\n        bqLines.push((lines[i] ?? '').replace(/^> ?/, ''))\n        i++\n      }\n      output.push(renderBlockquote(bqLines))\n      continue\n    }\n\n    // Blank line\n    if (!line.trim()) {\n      closeList()\n      i++\n      continue\n    }\n\n    // Paragraph\n    closeList()\n    output.push(`<p>${applyInline(line)}</p>`)\n    i++\n  }\n\n  closeList()\n  return output.join('\\n')\n}\n\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n// Blockquote / Callout renderer\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\nfunction renderBlockquote(innerLines: string[]): string {\n  const firstLine = innerLines[0] ?? ''\n  const calloutMatch = firstLine.match(/^\\[!(note|tip|important|warning|caution|info|success|error)\\]$/i)\n\n  if (calloutMatch) {\n    const type = calloutMatch[1]!.toLowerCase()\n    const icon = CALLOUT_ICONS[type] ?? '💬'\n    const title = type.charAt(0).toUpperCase() + type.slice(1)\n    const bodyLines = innerLines.slice(1).filter(l => l !== '')\n    const body = bodyLines.map(l => `<p>${applyInline(l)}</p>`).join('\\n')\n    return `<div class=\"callout callout-${escHtml(type)}\">\n  <div class=\"callout-title\">${icon} ${escHtml(title)}</div>\n  <div class=\"callout-body\">${body}</div>\n</div>`\n  }\n\n  // Regular blockquote\n  const inner = innerLines.map(l => `<p>${applyInline(l)}</p>`).join('\\n')\n  return `<blockquote>${inner}</blockquote>`\n}\n\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n// Inline formatting\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\nfunction applyInline(text: string): string {\n  // Process links before HTML-escaping to avoid double-escaping href/label\n  const withLinks = text.replace(/\\[([^\\]]+)\\]\\(([^)]+)\\)/g, (_, label: string, href: string) => {\n    return `<a href=\"${escHtml(href)}\" target=\"_blank\" rel=\"noopener\">${escHtml(label)}</a>`\n  })\n  // Escape remaining text, then apply inline patterns on already-safe content\n  // Split on the <a> tags we just inserted so they are not re-escaped\n  const parts = withLinks.split(/(<a [^>]+>.*?<\\/a>)/g)\n  const processed = parts\n    .map((part, i) => {\n      if (i % 2 === 1) return part // already-generated <a> tag, pass through\n      const escaped = escHtml(part)\n      return escaped\n        .replace(/\\*\\*(.+?)\\*\\*/g, '<strong>$1</strong>')\n        .replace(/\\*(.+?)\\*/g, '<em>$1</em>')\n        .replace(/__(.+?)__/g, '<strong>$1</strong>')\n        .replace(/_(.+?)_/g, '<em>$1</em>')\n        .replace(/`([^`]+)`/g, '<code>$1</code>')\n    })\n    .join('')\n  return processed\n}\n"
}

{
  "replace_all": false,
  "file_path": "C:\\Users\\ai\\.codex\\skills\\memorytree-workflow\\src\\report\\render\\css.ts",
  "old_string": "/* ── Responsive: tablet (768-1024px) ── */\n@media (max-width: 1024px) and (min-width: 769px) {\n  :root { --sidebar-width: 200px; }\n}\n\n/* ── Responsive: mobile (<768px) ── */\n@media (max-width: 768px) {\n  .topbar { display: flex; }\n  .sidebar {\n    transform: translateX(-100%);\n    top: 48px;\n  }\n  .sidebar.open { transform: translateX(0); }\n  .sidebar-overlay.open { display: block; }\n  .main-content { margin-left: 0; }\n  .container { padding: 1rem 0.75rem; }\n  .stats-grid { grid-template-columns: repeat(2, 1fr); }\n  .project-grid { grid-template-columns: 1fr; }\n}\n`",
  "new_string": "/* ── Responsive: tablet icon mode (768–1024px) ── */\n@media (max-width: 1024px) and (min-width: 769px) {\n  :root { --sidebar-width: 52px; }\n  .nav-link .nav-label { display: none; }\n  .nav-link { justify-content: center; padding: 0.55rem; gap: 0; }\n  .nav-section-label { display: none; }\n  .sidebar-brand { display: none; }\n  .sidebar-footer .theme-label { display: none; }\n  .sidebar-footer .theme-toggle-btn { padding: 0.35rem 0.5rem; }\n}\n\n/* ── Responsive: mobile (<768px) ── */\n@media (max-width: 768px) {\n  .topbar { display: flex; }\n  .sidebar {\n    transform: translateX(-100%);\n    top: 48px;\n  }\n  .sidebar.open { transform: translateX(0); }\n  .sidebar-overlay.open { display: block; }\n  .main-content { margin-left: 0; }\n  .container { padding: 1rem 0.75rem; }\n  .stats-grid { grid-template-columns: repeat(2, 1fr); }\n  .project-grid { grid-template-columns: 1fr; }\n}\n\n/* ── Sidebar collapse ── */\nbody.sidebar-collapsed .sidebar { width: 52px; }\nbody.sidebar-collapsed .nav-label { display: none; }\nbody.sidebar-collapsed .nav-link { justify-content: center; padding: 0.55rem; gap: 0; }\nbody.sidebar-collapsed .nav-section-label { display: none; }\nbody.sidebar-collapsed .sidebar-brand { display: none; }\nbody.sidebar-collapsed .theme-label { display: none; }\nbody.sidebar-collapsed .main-content { margin-left: 52px; }\n.sidebar-collapse-btn {\n  background: none;\n  border: 1px solid var(--border);\n  color: var(--text-muted);\n  border-radius: 6px;\n  padding: 0.25rem 0.5rem;\n  cursor: pointer;\n  font-size: 0.75rem;\n  line-height: 1;\n}\n.sidebar-collapse-btn:hover { color: var(--text); background: var(--bg-card); }\n\n/* ── Breadcrumb ── */\n.breadcrumb {\n  display: flex;\n  align-items: center;\n  flex-wrap: wrap;\n  gap: 0.25rem;\n  font-size: 0.82rem;\n  color: var(--text-muted);\n  margin-bottom: 1rem;\n}\n.breadcrumb-item { color: var(--text-muted); }\na.breadcrumb-item { color: var(--accent); text-decoration: none; }\na.breadcrumb-item:hover { text-decoration: underline; }\n.breadcrumb-sep { color: var(--border); user-select: none; }\n\n/* ── TOC ── */\n.toc {\n  background: var(--bg-secondary);\n  border: 1px solid var(--border);\n  border-left: 3px solid var(--accent);\n  border-radius: 6px;\n  padding: 0.75rem 1rem;\n  margin-bottom: 1.25rem;\n  font-size: 0.85rem;\n}\n.toc-toggle {\n  background: none;\n  border: none;\n  color: var(--text-muted);\n  font-size: 0.8rem;\n  font-weight: 600;\n  text-transform: uppercase;\n  letter-spacing: 0.05em;\n  cursor: pointer;\n  padding: 0;\n  display: flex;\n  align-items: center;\n  gap: 0.35rem;\n}\n.toc-toggle:hover { color: var(--text); }\n.toc-list {\n  list-style: none;\n  padding: 0;\n  margin: 0.5rem 0 0;\n}\n.toc-list li { margin-bottom: 0.2rem; }\n.toc-list a { color: var(--accent); }\n.toc-list a:hover { text-decoration: underline; }\n.toc-list[hidden] { display: none; }\n.toc-arrow { transition: transform 0.15s; }\n.toc-toggle[aria-expanded=\"false\"] .toc-arrow { transform: rotate(-90deg); }\n\n/* ── Callouts ── */\n.callout {\n  border-left: 4px solid;\n  border-radius: 6px;\n  padding: 0.85rem 1rem;\n  margin: 0.75rem 0;\n  font-size: 0.9rem;\n}\n.callout-title {\n  font-weight: 600;\n  margin-bottom: 0.4rem;\n  font-size: 0.88rem;\n  text-transform: capitalize;\n}\n.callout-body p { margin-bottom: 0.35rem; }\n.callout-body p:last-child { margin-bottom: 0; }\n.callout-note     { background: #1f6feb22; border-color: #58a6ff; }\n.callout-tip      { background: #1a7f3722; border-color: #3fb950; }\n.callout-info     { background: #1f6feb22; border-color: #58a6ff; }\n.callout-success  { background: #1a7f3722; border-color: #3fb950; }\n.callout-important { background: #6e40c922; border-color: #bc8cff; }\n.callout-warning  { background: #d2992222; border-color: #d29922; }\n.callout-caution  { background: #f8514922; border-color: #f85149; }\n.callout-error    { background: #f8514922; border-color: #f85149; }\n[data-theme=\"light\"] .callout-note,\n[data-theme=\"light\"] .callout-info    { background: #ddf4ff; border-color: #0969da; }\n[data-theme=\"light\"] .callout-tip,\n[data-theme=\"light\"] .callout-success { background: #dafbe1; border-color: #1a7f37; }\n[data-theme=\"light\"] .callout-important { background: #fbefff; border-color: #8250df; }\n[data-theme=\"light\"] .callout-warning { background: #fff8c5; border-color: #9a6700; }\n[data-theme=\"light\"] .callout-caution,\n[data-theme=\"light\"] .callout-error   { background: #ffebe9; border-color: #cf222e; }\n\n/* ── Mermaid diagrams ── */\npre.mermaid {\n  background: var(--bg-secondary);\n  border: 1px solid var(--border);\n  border-radius: 8px;\n  padding: 1rem;\n  text-align: center;\n  overflow-x: auto;\n}\n\n/* ── Reader Mode ── */\n.reader-mode .sidebar,\n.reader-mode .topbar,\n.reader-mode .breadcrumb,\n.reader-mode .meta-table,\n.reader-mode .backlinks { display: none !important; }\n.reader-mode .main-content { margin-left: 0 !important; }\n.reader-mode .container { max-width: 720px; }\n.reader-toggle-btn {\n  background: none;\n  border: 1px solid var(--border);\n  color: var(--text-muted);\n  border-radius: 6px;\n  padding: 0.3rem 0.6rem;\n  cursor: pointer;\n  font-size: 0.8rem;\n}\n.reader-toggle-btn:hover { color: var(--text); background: var(--bg-card); }\n\n/* ── View Transitions ── */\n@media (prefers-reduced-motion: no-preference) {\n  ::view-transition-old(root) {\n    animation: 90ms cubic-bezier(0.4, 0, 1, 1) both vt-fade-out;\n  }\n  ::view-transition-new(root) {\n    animation: 210ms cubic-bezier(0, 0, 0.2, 1) both vt-fade-in;\n  }\n}\n@keyframes vt-fade-out { to { opacity: 0; } }\n@keyframes vt-fade-in { from { opacity: 0; } }\n`"
}

  "new_string": "    if (ov) ov.addEventListener('click', closeSidebar);\n    // Sidebar collapse toggle\n    var collapseBtn = document.getElementById('sidebar-collapse');\n    if (collapseBtn) collapseBtn.addEventListener('click', function() {\n      var collapsed = document.body.classList.toggle('sidebar-collapsed');\n      localStorage.setItem('mt-sidebar', collapsed ? 'collapsed' : 'expanded');\n    });\n    // TOC toggle\n    var tocToggle = document.getElementById('toc-toggle');\n    var tocList = document.getElementById('toc-list');\n    if (tocToggle && tocList) tocToggle.addEventListener('click', function() {\n      var expanded = tocToggle.getAttribute('aria-expanded') === 'true';\n      tocToggle.setAttribute('aria-expanded', expanded ? 'false' : 'true');\n      if (expanded) { tocList.setAttribute('hidden', ''); } else { tocList.removeAttribute('hidden'); }\n    });\n    // Reader Mode toggle\n    var readerBtn = document.getElementById('reader-toggle');\n    if (readerBtn) {\n      if (localStorage.getItem('mt-reader') === '1') document.body.classList.add('reader-mode');\n      readerBtn.addEventListener('click', function() {\n        var on = document.body.classList.toggle('reader-mode');\n        localStorage.setItem('mt-reader', on ? '1' : '0');\n      });\n    }\n    // View Transitions API (progressive enhancement)\n    if (document.startViewTransition) {\n      document.addEventListener('click', function(e) {\n        var a = e.target && (e.target.closest ? e.target.closest('a[href]:not([target]):not([download])') : null);\n        if (!a) return;\n        var href = a.getAttribute('href');\n        if (!href || href.startsWith('#') || href.startsWith('http')) return;\n        e.preventDefault();\n        document.startViewTransition(function() { window.location.href = href; });\n      });\n    }\n    // Popover preview"
}

{
  "replace_all": false,
  "file_path": "C:\\Users\\ai\\.codex\\skills\\memorytree-workflow\\src\\report\\render\\layout.ts",
  "old_string": "export function htmlShell(title: string, content: string, nav: string, extraHead = '', lang = 'en'): string {\n  const topbar = `<header class=\"topbar\" id=\"topbar\">\n  <span class=\"topbar-brand\">MemoryTree</span>\n  <div class=\"topbar-actions\">\n    <button class=\"theme-toggle-btn\" id=\"theme-toggle-mobile\" type=\"button\">☀</button>\n    <button class=\"hamburger\" id=\"hamburger\" type=\"button\">☰</button>\n  </div>\n</header>`\n\n  return `<!DOCTYPE html>\n<html lang=\"${escHtml(lang)}\" data-theme=\"dark\">\n<head>\n<meta charset=\"UTF-8\">\n<meta name=\"viewport\" content=\"width=device-width, initial-scale=1.0\">\n<title>${escHtml(title)} — MemoryTree</title>\n<style>${REPORT_CSS}</style>\n${extraHead}\n${SIDEBAR_INLINE_JS}\n</head>\n<body>\n${topbar}\n${nav}\n<div class=\"main-content\">\n  <div class=\"container\">\n${content}\n  </div>\n</div>\n</body>\n</html>`\n}",
  "new_string": "export interface HtmlShellOptions {\n  extraHead?: string\n  lang?: string\n  breadcrumb?: string\n  /** Inject reader-mode toggle button into the page */\n  readerMode?: boolean\n}\n\nexport function htmlShell(\n  title: string,\n  content: string,\n  nav: string,\n  extraHeadOrOpts: string | HtmlShellOptions = '',\n  lang = 'en',\n): string {\n  // Accept legacy string arg (extraHead) or new options object\n  const opts: HtmlShellOptions = typeof extraHeadOrOpts === 'string'\n    ? { extraHead: extraHeadOrOpts, lang }\n    : extraHeadOrOpts\n  const extraHead = opts.extraHead ?? ''\n  const resolvedLang = opts.lang ?? lang\n  const breadcrumb = opts.breadcrumb ?? ''\n  const readerBtn = opts.readerMode !== false\n    ? `<button class=\"reader-toggle-btn\" id=\"reader-toggle\" type=\"button\" title=\"Toggle reader mode\">📖</button>`\n    : ''\n\n  const topbar = `<header class=\"topbar\" id=\"topbar\">\n  <span class=\"topbar-brand\">MemoryTree</span>\n  <div class=\"topbar-actions\">\n    <button class=\"theme-toggle-btn\" id=\"theme-toggle-mobile\" type=\"button\">☀</button>\n    <button class=\"hamburger\" id=\"hamburger\" type=\"button\">☰</button>\n  </div>\n</header>`\n\n  return `<!DOCTYPE html>\n<html lang=\"${escHtml(resolvedLang)}\" data-theme=\"dark\">\n<head>\n<meta charset=\"UTF-8\">\n<meta name=\"viewport\" content=\"width=device-width, initial-scale=1.0\">\n<title>${escHtml(title)} — MemoryTree</title>\n<style>${REPORT_CSS}</style>\n${extraHead}\n${SIDEBAR_INLINE_JS}\n</head>\n<body>\n${topbar}\n${nav}\n<div class=\"main-content\">\n  <div class=\"container\">\n${breadcrumb ? `${breadcrumb}\\n` : ''}<div style=\"display:flex;justify-content:flex-end;margin-bottom:0.5rem\">${readerBtn}</div>\n${content}\n  </div>\n</div>\n</body>\n</html>`\n}"
}

Show input payload 
{
  "file_path": "C:\\Users\\ai\\.codex\\skills\\memorytree-workflow\\src\\report\\render\\rss.ts",
  "content": "/**\n * RSS 2.0 feed generator for MemoryTree sessions.\n * Generates a valid feed.xml file.\n * When baseUrl is empty, item links are omitted gracefully.\n */\n\nimport type { ManifestEntry } from '../../types/transcript.js'\nimport { transcriptUrlFromRoot } from './layout.js'\n\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n// XML escaping\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\nfunction xmlEsc(text: string): string {\n  return text\n    .replace(/&/g, '&amp;')\n    .replace(/</g, '&lt;')\n    .replace(/>/g, '&gt;')\n    .replace(/\"/g, '&quot;')\n    .replace(/'/g, '&apos;')\n}\n\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n// RSS renderer\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n/**\n * Generate an RSS 2.0 feed from session manifests.\n *\n * @param manifests  All session manifests (sorted by started_at descending by caller)\n * @param summaries  Map of session_id → AI summary text\n * @param baseUrl    Absolute base URL (e.g. 'https://memory.example.com').\n *                   When empty, item <link> and channel <link> are omitted.\n */\nexport function renderRssFeed(\n  manifests: ManifestEntry[],\n  summaries: Record<string, string>,\n  baseUrl: string,\n): string {\n  const MAX_ITEMS = 100\n  const sorted = [...manifests]\n    .sort((a, b) => b.started_at.localeCompare(a.started_at))\n    .slice(0, MAX_ITEMS)\n\n  const channelLink = baseUrl ? `\\n  <link>${xmlEsc(baseUrl)}</link>` : ''\n\n  const items = sorted.map(m => {\n    const title = m.title || m.session_id\n    const summary = summaries[m.session_id] ?? ''\n    const pubDate = toRfc2822(m.started_at)\n    const relUrl = transcriptUrlFromRoot(m)\n    const absUrl = baseUrl ? `${baseUrl.replace(/\\/$/, '')}/${relUrl}` : ''\n    const linkTag = absUrl ? `\\n    <link>${xmlEsc(absUrl)}</link>` : ''\n    const guidTag = absUrl\n      ? `\\n    <guid isPermaLink=\"true\">${xmlEsc(absUrl)}</guid>`\n      : `\\n    <guid isPermaLink=\"false\">${xmlEsc(m.session_id)}</guid>`\n    const descTag = summary\n      ? `\\n    <description>${xmlEsc(summary.slice(0, 500))}</description>`\n      : ''\n    return `  <item>\n    <title>${xmlEsc(title)}</title>${linkTag}${guidTag}\n    <pubDate>${xmlEsc(pubDate)}</pubDate>${descTag}\n  </item>`\n  }).join('\\n')\n\n  return `<?xml version=\"1.0\" encoding=\"UTF-8\"?>\n<rss version=\"2.0\" xmlns:atom=\"http://www.w3.org/2005/Atom\">\n<channel>\n  <title>MemoryTree Sessions</title>${channelLink}\n  <description>AI session transcripts exported by MemoryTree</description>\n  <generator>MemoryTree</generator>\n${items}\n</channel>\n</rss>`\n}\n\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n// Helpers\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n/** Convert ISO 8601 to RFC 2822 format (required by RSS spec). */\nfunction toRfc2822(iso: string): string {\n  try {\n    return new Date(iso).toUTCString()\n  } catch {\n    return new Date().toUTCString()\n  }\n}\n"
}

45→
    46→export async function buildReport(options: BuildReportOptions): Promise<void> {
    47→  const { root, output } = options
    48→  const noAi = options.noAi ?? false
    49→  const model = options.model ?? DEFAULT_MODEL
    50→  const locale = options.locale ?? 'en'
    51→  const ghPagesBranch = options.ghPagesBranch ?? ''
    52→  const cname = options.cname ?? ''
    53→  const webhookUrl = options.webhookUrl ?? ''
    54→  const newSessionIds = options.newSessionIds ?? []
    55→
    56→  // Cache lives outside the gitignored output dir so it survives rm -rf
    57→  const cacheDir = join(root, 'Memory', '.report-cache')
    58→
    59→  // Load translations
    60→  const t: Translations = loadLocale(locale)
    61→
    62→  // Ensure output dirs exist
    63→  mkdirSync(output, { recursive: true })
    64→  mkdirSync(join(output, 'transcripts'), { recursive: true })
    65→  mkdirSync(join(output, 'goals'), { recursive: true })
    66→  mkdirSync(join(output, 'knowledge'), { recursive: true })
    67→  mkdirSync(join(output, 'todos'), { recursive: true })
    68→  mkdirSync(join(output, 'archive'), { recursive: true })
    69→  mkdirSync(join(output, 'projects'), { recursive: true })
    70→  mkdirSync(cacheDir, { recursive: true })
    71→
    72→  // Ensure Memory/07_reports is gitignored
    73→  ensureGitignore(root, 'Memory/07_reports/')
    74→
    75→  // Load all manifests
    76→  const manifests = loadManifests(root)
    77→
    78→  // Build link graph from clean markdown
    79→  const linkGraph = buildLinkGraph(manifests, root)
    80→
    81→  // Accumulate tool counts while processing transcripts
    82→  let toolCounts: Record<string, number> = {}
    83→
    84→  // Summary options
    85→  const summaryOptions: SummaryOptions = { cacheDir, noAi, model }
    86→
    87→  // Process each transcript: parse → summarize → render → write → discard
    88→  const snippets: Record<string, string> = {}
    89→  const summaries: Record<string, string> = {}
    90→  const summaryPromises: Array<Promise<void>> = []
    91→
    92→  for (const m of manifests) {
    93→    const messages = parseCleanMarkdownMessages(m, root)
    94→
    95→    // Tool events from parsed transcript (try raw path)
    96→    const rawPath = resolveRawPath(m, root)
    97→    if (rawPath && existsSync(rawPath)) {
    98→      try {
    99→        const toolSummaries = extractToolSummariesFromRaw(rawPath, m.client)
   100→        const names = extractToolNames(toolSummaries)
   101→        toolCounts = accumulateToolCounts(toolCounts, names)
   102→      } catch {
   103→        // Skip if raw parsing fails
   104→      }

{
  "file_path": "C:\\Users\\ai\\.codex\\skills\\memorytree-workflow\\tests\\report\\render\\rss.test.ts",
  "content": "import { describe, it, expect } from 'vitest'\nimport { renderRssFeed } from '../../../src/report/render/rss.js'\nimport type { ManifestEntry } from '../../../src/types/transcript.js'\n\nfunction makeManifest(overrides: Partial<ManifestEntry> = {}): ManifestEntry {\n  return {\n    client: 'claude',\n    project: 'test',\n    session_id: 'sess-001',\n    raw_sha256: 'abc123',\n    title: 'My Session',\n    started_at: '2026-03-16T10:00:00Z',\n    imported_at: '2026-03-16T10:01:00Z',\n    cwd: '/home/user/project',\n    branch: 'main',\n    raw_source_path: '',\n    raw_upload_permission: 'not-set',\n    global_raw_path: '',\n    global_clean_path: '',\n    global_manifest_path: '',\n    repo_raw_path: '',\n    repo_clean_path: 'Memory/06_transcripts/clean/claude/2026/03/sess-001.md',\n    repo_manifest_path: '',\n    message_count: 5,\n    tool_event_count: 2,\n    cleaning_mode: 'standard',\n    repo_mirror_enabled: true,\n    ...overrides,\n  }\n}\n\ndescribe('renderRssFeed', () => {\n  it('produces valid RSS 2.0 XML header', () => {\n    const xml = renderRssFeed([], {}, '')\n    expect(xml).toContain('<?xml version=\"1.0\" encoding=\"UTF-8\"?>')\n    expect(xml).toContain('<rss version=\"2.0\"')\n    expect(xml).toContain('<channel>')\n    expect(xml).toContain('</channel>')\n    expect(xml).toContain('</rss>')\n  })\n\n  it('omits channel link when baseUrl is empty', () => {\n    const xml = renderRssFeed([], {}, '')\n    expect(xml).not.toContain('<link>')\n  })\n\n  it('includes channel link when baseUrl is set', () => {\n    const xml = renderRssFeed([], {}, 'https://memory.example.com')\n    expect(xml).toContain('<link>https://memory.example.com</link>')\n  })\n\n  it('renders session as RSS item with title and guid', () => {\n    const m = makeManifest()\n    const xml = renderRssFeed([m], {}, '')\n    expect(xml).toContain('<title>My Session</title>')\n    expect(xml).toContain('<guid isPermaLink=\"false\">sess-001</guid>')\n  })\n\n  it('includes absolute link and permalink guid when baseUrl is set', () => {\n    const m = makeManifest()\n    const xml = renderRssFeed([m], {}, 'https://memory.example.com')\n    expect(xml).toContain('<link>https://memory.example.com/transcripts/claude/')\n    expect(xml).toContain('isPermaLink=\"true\"')\n  })\n\n  it('includes description from summaries', () => {\n    const m = makeManifest()\n    const xml = renderRssFeed([m], { 'sess-001': 'This is the summary.' }, '')\n    expect(xml).toContain('<description>This is the summary.</description>')\n  })\n\n  it('escapes XML special characters in title', () => {\n    const m = makeManifest({ title: 'A & B <test> \"quote\"' })\n    const xml = renderRssFeed([m], {}, '')\n    expect(xml).toContain('A &amp; B &lt;test&gt; &quot;quote&quot;')\n    expect(xml).not.toContain('<test>')\n  })\n\n  it('limits output to 100 items', () => {\n    const manifests = Array.from({ length: 150 }, (_, i) =>\n      makeManifest({ session_id: `sess-${i.toString().padStart(3, '0')}`, started_at: `2026-03-${(i % 28 + 1).toString().padStart(2, '0')}T00:00:00Z` })\n    )\n    const xml = renderRssFeed(manifests, {}, '')\n    const itemCount = (xml.match(/<item>/g) ?? []).length\n    expect(itemCount).toBe(100)\n  })\n\n  it('includes pubDate in RFC 2822 format', () => {\n    const m = makeManifest({ started_at: '2026-03-16T10:00:00Z' })\n    const xml = renderRssFeed([m], {}, '')\n    expect(xml).toContain('<pubDate>')\n    // RFC 2822 dates contain weekday abbreviation\n    expect(xml).toMatch(/<pubDate>Mon,/)\n  })\n\n  it('handles empty manifests array', () => {\n    const xml = renderRssFeed([], {}, 'https://example.com')\n    expect(xml).toContain('<channel>')\n    expect(xml).not.toContain('<item>')\n  })\n})\n"
}

Read output=1→/** 2→ * Full-text search: pre-built JSON index embedded as inline JS. 3→ * Vanilla JS substring search, no external library. 4→ */ 5→ 6→import type { ManifestEntry } from '.....
Show output payload 
1→/**
     2→ * Full-text search: pre-built JSON index embedded as inline JS.
     3→ * Vanilla JS substring search, no external library.
     4→ */
     5→
     6→import type { ManifestEntry } from '../../types/transcript.js'
     7→import type { SearchIndexEntry } from '../../types/report.js'
     8→import type { Translations } from '../i18n/types.js'
     9→import { escHtml, htmlShell, renderNav, transcriptUrlFromRoot } from './layout.js'
    10→
    11→const MAX_INDEX_BYTES = 50_000
    12→const SNIPPET_LEN = 200
    13→
    14→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    15→// Index construction
    16→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    17→
    18→export function buildSearchIndex(
    19→  manifests: ManifestEntry[],
    20→  getSnippet: (m: ManifestEntry) => string,
    21→): SearchIndexEntry[] {
    22→  const entries: SearchIndexEntry[] = []
    23→  let totalBytes = 0
    24→
    25→  for (const m of manifests) {
    26→    let snippet = getSnippet(m).slice(0, SNIPPET_LEN)
    27→    const entry: SearchIndexEntry = {
    28→      url: transcriptUrl(m),
    29→      title: m.title || m.session_id,
    30→      client: m.client,
    31→      date: m.started_at.slice(0, 10),
    32→      snippet,
    33→    }
    34→    const entryBytes = JSON.stringify(entry).length
    35→    if (totalBytes + entryBytes > MAX_INDEX_BYTES) {
    36→      // Truncate snippet to fit within budget
    37→      const remaining = MAX_INDEX_BYTES - totalBytes - (entryBytes - snippet.length) - 10
    38→      if (remaining < 20) break
    39→      snippet = snippet.slice(0, remaining)
    40→      entry.snippet = snippet
    41→    }
    42→    totalBytes += JSON.stringify(entry).length
    43→    entries.push(entry)
    44→  }
    45→
    46→  return entries
    47→}
    48→
    49→function transcriptUrl(m: ManifestEntry): string {
    50→  return transcriptUrlFromRoot(m)
    51→}
    52→
    53→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    54→// Search page renderer
    55→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    56→
    57→export function renderSearchPage(index: SearchIndexEntry[], t?: Translations): string {
    58→  // Escape </script> to prevent script-injection via manifest-controlled content
    59→  const indexJson = JSON.stringify(index).replace(/<\//g, '<\\/')
    60→  const nav = renderNav('search', 0, t)
    61→  const title = t?.search.title ?? 'Search'
    62→  const placeholder = t?.search.placeholder ?? 'Search sessions, messages, and content...'
    63→
    64→  const extraHead = `<script>
    65→const SEARCH_INDEX = ${indexJson};
    66→</script>`
    67→
    68→  const content = `
    69→<div class="page-header">
    70→  <h1>${escHtml(title)}</h1>
    71→  <p class="subtitle">Full-text search across all imported sessions</p>
    72→</div>
    73→
    74→<input
    75→  type="search"
    76→  id="search-input"
    77→  class="search-box"
    78→  placeholder="${escHtml(placeholder)}"
    79→  autofocus
    80→>
    81→<div id="search-count"></div>
    82→<div id="search-results" class="search-results"></div>
    83→
    84→<script>
    85→(function() {
    86→  const input = document.getElementById('search-input');
    87→  const resultsEl = document.getElementById('search-results');
    88→  const countEl = document.getElementById('search-count');
    89→
    90→  function highlight(text, query) {
    91→    if (!query) return escHtml(text);
    92→    const escaped = escHtml(text);
    93→    const escapedQ = escHtml(query);
    94→    const lower = escaped.toLowerCase();
    95→    const lowerQ = escapedQ.toLowerCase();
    96→    if (!lowerQ) return escaped;
    97→    let result = '';
    98→    let i = 0;
    99→    while (i < escaped.length) {
   100→      const idx = lower.indexOf(lowerQ, i);
   101→      if (idx === -1) { result += escaped.slice(i); break; }
   102→      result += escaped.slice(i, idx) + '<mark>' + escaped.slice(idx, idx + lowerQ.length) + '</mark>';
   103→      i = idx + lowerQ.length;
   104→    }
   105→    return result;
   106→  }
   107→
   108→  function escHtml(s) {
   109→    return s.replace(/&/g,'&amp;').replace(/</g,'&lt;').replace(/>/g,'&gt;').replace(/"/g,'&quot;');
   110→  }
   111→
   112→  function renderResults(query) {
   113→    const q = (query || '').toLowerCase().trim();
   114→    if (!q) {
   115→      resultsEl.innerHTML = '';
   116→      countEl.textContent = SEARCH_INDEX.length + ' session(s) indexed';
   117→      return;
   118→    }
   119→    const matches = SEARCH_INDEX.filter(function(e) {
   120→      return (e.title || '').toLowerCase().includes(q) ||
   121→             (e.snippet || '').toLowerCase().includes(q) ||
   122→             (e.client || '').toLowerCase().includes(q);
   123→    });
   124→    countEl.textContent = matches.length + ' result(s) for "' + escHtml(q) + '"';
   125→    resultsEl.innerHTML = matches.map(function(e) {
   126→      return '<div class="search-result">' +
   127→        '<div class="search-result-title"><a href="' + escHtml(e.url) + '">' + highlight(e.title, query) + '</a></div>' +
   128→        '<div class="search-result-meta"><span class="badge badge-' + escHtml(e.client) + '">' + escHtml(e.client) + '</span> &nbsp; ' + escHtml(e.date) + '</div>' +
   129→        '<div class="search-result-snippet">' + highlight(e.snippet, query) + '</div>' +
   130→        '</div>';
   131→    }).join('');
   132→  }
   133→
   134→  renderResults('');
   135→  input.addEventListener('input', function() { renderResults(input.value); });
   136→})();
   137→</script>`
   138→
   139→  return htmlShell(title, content, nav, extraHead)
   140→}
   141→

Show output payload 
1→/**
     2→ * CLI adapters for `memorytree report build` and `memorytree report serve`.
     3→ */
     4→
     5→import { createServer } from 'node:http'
     6→import { createReadStream, existsSync, statSync } from 'node:fs'
     7→import { extname, join, resolve, sep } from 'node:path'
     8→
     9→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    10→// Build command
    11→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    12→
    13→export interface CmdReportBuildOptions {
    14→  root: string
    15→  output: string
    16→  noAi: boolean
    17→  model: string
    18→}
    19→
    20→export async function cmdReportBuild(opts: CmdReportBuildOptions): Promise<number> {
    21→  const { buildReport } = await import('../report/build.js')
    22→  try {
    23→    await buildReport({
    24→      root: resolve(opts.root),
    25→      output: resolve(opts.output),
    26→      noAi: opts.noAi,
    27→      model: opts.model,
    28→    })
    29→    console.log(`Report generated at: ${resolve(opts.output)}`)
    30→    console.log(`Open: ${join(resolve(opts.output), 'index.html')}`)
    31→    return 0
    32→  } catch (err: unknown) {
    33→    console.error(`Report build failed: ${String(err)}`)
    34→    return 1
    35→  }
    36→}
    37→
    38→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    39→// Serve command
    40→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    41→
    42→export interface CmdReportServeOptions {
    43→  dir: string
    44→  port: number
    45→}
    46→
    47→export function cmdReportServe(opts: CmdReportServeOptions): number {
    48→  const root = resolve(opts.dir)
    49→  const port = opts.port
    50→
    51→  const MIME: Record<string, string> = {
    52→    '.html': 'text/html; charset=utf-8',
    53→    '.css': 'text/css',
    54→    '.js': 'application/javascript',
    55→    '.json': 'application/json',
    56→    '.svg': 'image/svg+xml',
    57→    '.ico': 'image/x-icon',
    58→  }
    59→
    60→  const server = createServer((req, res) => {
    61→    let urlPath: string
    62→    try {
    63→      urlPath = decodeURIComponent((req.url ?? '/').split('?')[0] ?? '/')
    64→    } catch {
    65→      res.writeHead(400, { 'Content-Type': 'text/plain' })
    66→      res.end('Bad request')
    67→      return
    68→    }
    69→
    70→    let filePath = join(root, urlPath)
    71→
    72→    // Containment check: reject paths that escape the serve root
    73→    // Use sep (\ on Windows, / on Unix) so the check works cross-platform.
    74→    if (!filePath.startsWith(root + sep) && filePath !== root) {
    75→      res.writeHead(403, { 'Content-Type': 'text/plain' })
    76→      res.end('Forbidden')
    77→      return
    78→    }
    79→
    80→    // Serve index.html for directory requests
    81→    if (existsSync(filePath) && statSync(filePath).isDirectory()) {
    82→      filePath = join(filePath, 'index.html')
    83→    }
    84→
    85→    if (!existsSync(filePath)) {
    86→      res.writeHead(404, { 'Content-Type': 'text/plain' })
    87→      res.end('Not found')
    88→      return
    89→    }
    90→
    91→    const ext = extname(filePath)
    92→    const contentType = MIME[ext] ?? 'application/octet-stream'
    93→    res.writeHead(200, { 'Content-Type': contentType })
    94→    const stream = createReadStream(filePath)
    95→    stream.on('error', () => { res.destroy() })
    96→    stream.pipe(res)
    97→  })
    98→
    99→  server.on('error', (err: NodeJS.ErrnoException) => {
   100→    if (err.code === 'EADDRINUSE') {
   101→      console.error(`Error: port ${port} is already in use.`)
   102→      process.exit(1)
   103→    }
   104→    console.error(`Server error: ${String(err)}`)
   105→    process.exit(1)
   106→  })
   107→
   108→  server.listen(port, () => {
   109→    console.log(`Serving ${root}`)
   110→    console.log(`Open: http://localhost:${port}/`)
   111→    console.log('Press Ctrl+C to stop.')
   112→  })
   113→
   114→  return 0
   115→}
   116→

Show output payload 
1→/**
     2→ * AI summary generation with SHA256-based caching.
     3→ * Falls back gracefully when API key is absent or SDK is missing.
     4→ */
     5→
     6→import { existsSync, mkdirSync, readFileSync, writeFileSync } from 'node:fs'
     7→import { join } from 'node:path'
     8→
     9→import type { SummaryCache } from '../types/report.js'
    10→
    11→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    12→// Types
    13→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    14→
    15→export interface SummaryMessage {
    16→  role: string
    17→  text: string
    18→}
    19→
    20→export interface SummaryOptions {
    21→  cacheDir: string
    22→  noAi: boolean
    23→  model: string
    24→}
    25→
    26→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    27→// Semaphore (max 5 concurrent AI calls)
    28→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    29→
    30→let activeCalls = 0
    31→const MAX_CONCURRENT = 5
    32→
    33→async function withSemaphore<T>(fn: () => Promise<T>): Promise<T> {
    34→  while (activeCalls >= MAX_CONCURRENT) {
    35→    await new Promise<void>(resolve => setTimeout(resolve, 100))
    36→  }
    37→  activeCalls++
    38→  try {
    39→    return await fn()
    40→  } finally {
    41→    activeCalls--
    42→  }
    43→}
    44→
    45→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    46→// Main entry point
    47→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    48→
    49→export async function getSummary(
    50→  sha256: string,
    51→  messages: SummaryMessage[],
    52→  options: SummaryOptions,
    53→): Promise<string> {
    54→  // Check cache
    55→  const cached = readCache(sha256, options.cacheDir)
    56→  if (cached !== null) {
    57→    return cached
    58→  }
    59→
    60→  if (options.noAi) return ''

  "file_path": "C:\\Users\\ai\\.codex\\skills\\memorytree-workflow\\src\\report\\render\\search.ts",
  "old_string": "export function buildSearchIndex(\n  manifests: ManifestEntry[],\n  getSnippet: (m: ManifestEntry) => string,\n): SearchIndexEntry[] {\n  const entries: SearchIndexEntry[] = []\n  let totalBytes = 0\n\n  for (const m of manifests) {\n    let snippet = getSnippet(m).slice(0, SNIPPET_LEN)\n    const entry: SearchIndexEntry = {\n      url: transcriptUrl(m),\n      title: m.title || m.session_id,\n      client: m.client,\n      date: m.started_at.slice(0, 10),\n      snippet,\n    }",
  "new_string": "export function buildSearchIndex(\n  manifests: ManifestEntry[],\n  getSnippet: (m: ManifestEntry) => string,\n): SearchIndexEntry[] {\n  const entries: SearchIndexEntry[] = []\n  let totalBytes = 0\n\n  for (const m of manifests) {\n    let snippet = getSnippet(m).slice(0, SNIPPET_LEN)\n    const project = m.project || lastPathSegment(m.cwd) || 'unknown'\n    const entry: SearchIndexEntry = {\n      url: transcriptUrl(m),\n      title: m.title || m.session_id,\n      client: m.client,\n      project,\n      date: m.started_at.slice(0, 10),\n      snippet,\n    }"
}

  "old_string": "export function renderSearchPage(index: SearchIndexEntry[], t?: Translations): string {\n  // Escape </script> to prevent script-injection via manifest-controlled content\n  const indexJson = JSON.stringify(index).replace(/<\\//g, '<\\\\/')\n  const nav = renderNav('search', 0, t)\n  const title = t?.search.title ?? 'Search'\n  const placeholder = t?.search.placeholder ?? 'Search sessions, messages, and content...'\n\n  const extraHead = `<script>\nconst SEARCH_INDEX = ${indexJson};\n</script>`\n\n  const content = `\n<div class=\"page-header\">\n  <h1>${escHtml(title)}</h1>\n  <p class=\"subtitle\">Full-text search across all imported sessions</p>\n</div>\n\n<input\n  type=\"search\"\n  id=\"search-input\"\n  class=\"search-box\"\n  placeholder=\"${escHtml(placeholder)}\"\n  autofocus\n>\n<div id=\"search-count\"></div>\n<div id=\"search-results\" class=\"search-results\"></div>\n\n<script>\n(function() {\n  const input = document.getElementById('search-input');\n  const resultsEl = document.getElementById('search-results');\n  const countEl = document.getElementById('search-count');\n\n  function highlight(text, query) {\n    if (!query) return escHtml(text);\n    const escaped = escHtml(text);\n    const escapedQ = escHtml(query);\n    const lower = escaped.toLowerCase();\n    const lowerQ = escapedQ.toLowerCase();\n    if (!lowerQ) return escaped;\n    let result = '';\n    let i = 0;\n    while (i < escaped.length) {\n      const idx = lower.indexOf(lowerQ, i);\n      if (idx === -1) { result += escaped.slice(i); break; }\n      result += escaped.slice(i, idx) + '<mark>' + escaped.slice(idx, idx + lowerQ.length) + '</mark>';\n      i = idx + lowerQ.length;\n    }\n    return result;\n  }\n\n  function escHtml(s) {\n    return s.replace(/&/g,'&amp;').replace(/</g,'&lt;').replace(/>/g,'&gt;').replace(/\"/g,'&quot;');\n  }\n\n  function renderResults(query) {\n    const q = (query || '').toLowerCase().trim();\n    if (!q) {\n      resultsEl.innerHTML = '';\n      countEl.textContent = SEARCH_INDEX.length + ' session(s) indexed';\n      return;\n    }\n    const matches = SEARCH_INDEX.filter(function(e) {\n      return (e.title || '').toLowerCase().includes(q) ||\n             (e.snippet || '').toLowerCase().includes(q) ||\n             (e.client || '').toLowerCase().includes(q);\n    });\n    countEl.textContent = matches.length + ' result(s) for \"' + escHtml(q) + '\"';\n    resultsEl.innerHTML = matches.map(function(e) {\n      return '<div class=\"search-result\">' +\n        '<div class=\"search-result-title\"><a href=\"' + escHtml(e.url) + '\">' + highlight(e.title, query) + '</a></div>' +\n        '<div class=\"search-result-meta\"><span class=\"badge badge-' + escHtml(e.client) + '\">' + escHtml(e.client) + '</span> &nbsp; ' + escHtml(e.date) + '</div>' +\n        '<div class=\"search-result-snippet\">' + highlight(e.snippet, query) + '</div>' +\n        '</div>';\n    }).join('');\n  }\n\n  renderResults('');\n  input.addEventListener('input', function() { renderResults(input.value); });\n})();\n</script>`\n\n  return htmlShell(title, content, nav, extraHead)\n}",
  "new_string": "export function renderSearchPage(index: SearchIndexEntry[], t?: Translations): string {\n  // Escape </script> to prevent script-injection via manifest-controlled content\n  const indexJson = JSON.stringify(index).replace(/<\\//g, '<\\\\/')\n  const nav = renderNav('search', 0, t)\n  const title = t?.search.title ?? 'Search'\n  const placeholder = t?.search.placeholder ?? 'Search sessions, messages, and content...'\n\n  // Build unique client and project lists for filter dropdowns\n  const clients = [...new Set(index.map(e => e.client).filter(Boolean))].sort()\n  const projects = [...new Set(index.map(e => e.project).filter(Boolean))].sort()\n\n  const clientOptions = clients\n    .map(c => `<option value=\"${escHtml(c)}\">${escHtml(c)}</option>`)\n    .join('')\n  const projectOptions = projects\n    .map(p => `<option value=\"${escHtml(p)}\">${escHtml(p)}</option>`)\n    .join('')\n\n  const extraHead = `<script>\nconst SEARCH_INDEX = ${indexJson};\n</script>`\n\n  const content = `\n<div class=\"page-header\">\n  <h1>${escHtml(title)}</h1>\n  <p class=\"subtitle\">Full-text search across all imported sessions</p>\n</div>\n\n<div style=\"display:flex;gap:0.5rem;margin-bottom:0.75rem;flex-wrap:wrap\">\n  <select id=\"filter-client\" class=\"search-filter-select\">\n    <option value=\"\">All Clients</option>\n    ${clientOptions}\n  </select>\n  <select id=\"filter-project\" class=\"search-filter-select\">\n    <option value=\"\">All Projects</option>\n    ${projectOptions}\n  </select>\n</div>\n<input\n  type=\"search\"\n  id=\"search-input\"\n  class=\"search-box\"\n  placeholder=\"${escHtml(placeholder)}\"\n  autofocus\n>\n<div id=\"search-count\"></div>\n<div id=\"search-results\" class=\"search-results\"></div>\n\n<script>\n(function() {\n  var input = document.getElementById('search-input');\n  var resultsEl = document.getElementById('search-results');\n  var countEl = document.getElementById('search-count');\n  var clientSel = document.getElementById('filter-client');\n  var projectSel = document.getElementById('filter-project');\n\n  function highlight(text, query) {\n    if (!query) return escHtml(text);\n    var escaped = escHtml(text);\n    var escapedQ = escHtml(query);\n    var lower = escaped.toLowerCase();\n    var lowerQ = escapedQ.toLowerCase();\n    if (!lowerQ) return escaped;\n    var result = '';\n    var i = 0;\n    while (i < escaped.length) {\n      var idx = lower.indexOf(lowerQ, i);\n      if (idx === -1) { result += escaped.slice(i); break; }\n      result += escaped.slice(i, idx) + '<mark>' + escaped.slice(idx, idx + lowerQ.length) + '</mark>';\n      i = idx + lowerQ.length;\n    }\n    return result;\n  }\n\n  function escHtml(s) {\n    return String(s).replace(/&/g,'&amp;').replace(/</g,'&lt;').replace(/>/g,'&gt;').replace(/\"/g,'&quot;');\n  }\n\n  function renderResults() {\n    var query = (input.value || '');\n    var q = query.toLowerCase().trim();\n    var filterClient = clientSel ? clientSel.value : '';\n    var filterProject = projectSel ? projectSel.value : '';\n    var filtered = SEARCH_INDEX.filter(function(e) {\n      if (filterClient && e.client !== filterClient) return false;\n      if (filterProject && e.project !== filterProject) return false;\n      if (!q) return true;\n      return (e.title || '').toLowerCase().includes(q) ||\n             (e.snippet || '').toLowerCase().includes(q);\n    });\n    if (!q && !filterClient && !filterProject) {\n      resultsEl.innerHTML = '';\n      countEl.textContent = SEARCH_INDEX.length + ' session(s) indexed';\n      return;\n    }\n    countEl.textContent = filtered.length + ' result(s)' + (q ? ' for \"' + escHtml(q) + '\"' : '');\n    resultsEl.innerHTML = filtered.map(function(e) {\n      return '<div class=\"search-result\">' +\n        '<div class=\"search-result-title\"><a href=\"' + escHtml(e.url) + '\">' + highlight(e.title, query) + '</a></div>' +\n        '<div class=\"search-result-meta\"><span class=\"badge badge-' + escHtml(e.client) + '\">' + escHtml(e.client) + '</span> &nbsp; ' + escHtml(e.date) + '</div>' +\n        '<div class=\"search-result-snippet\">' + highlight(e.snippet, query) + '</div>' +\n        '</div>';\n    }).join('');\n  }\n\n  renderResults();\n  input.addEventListener('input', renderResults);\n  if (clientSel) clientSel.addEventListener('change', renderResults);\n  if (projectSel) projectSel.addEventListener('change', renderResults);\n})();\n</script>`\n\n  return htmlShell(title, content, nav, extraHead)\n}"
}

This session is being continued from a previous conversation that ran out of context. The summary below covers the earlier portion of the conversation. Summary: 1. Primary Request and Intent: - User asked whether to s...

Expand full message

This session is being continued from a previous conversation that ran out of context. The summary below covers the earlier portion of the conversation.

Summary:
1. Primary Request and Intent:
- User asked whether to submit Issues or PR for Phase 1 work → decided PR
- User requested creating new feat/report-v2 branch, committing all changes, creating PR
- User asked to review Phase 2 plan for bugs/issues before implementing
- User accepted best-practice recommendations and said "开始" (start) to begin Phase 2 implementation
- Phase 2 implementation is currently in progress

2. Key Technical Concepts:
- NodeNext ESM TypeScript with strict mode, noUncheckedIndexedAccess
- Git stash recovery via dropped stash SHA (git show <sha> + git stash apply <sha>)
- TOC generation: h2+h3 headings, duplicate-safe slugs with -N suffix, injectHeadingIds() post-processor
- Callout syntax: > [!note] → styled div with color-coded border, per-type emoji icon
- Mermaid CDN + SRI: hasMermaidBlocks() scan → conditional CDN injection; <pre class="mermaid"> rendering
- View Transitions API: Progressive enhancement, ~5 lines CSS + JS, cross-page fade animation
- Sidebar icon mode: 768–1024px CSS only, emoji icons, title tooltip, nav-label spans hidden
- Sidebar collapse: localStorage mt-sidebar, body.sidebar-collapsed CSS class
- Reader Mode: body.reader-mode class hides sidebar/breadcrumb/meta, localStorage mt-reader
- RSS 2.0: XML escaping with xmlEsc(), RFC 2822 dates, 100-item cap, graceful baseUrl omission
- Search filtering: project field added to SearchIndexEntry, client + project <select> dropdowns, combined AND filter
- report_base_url config: New field for RSS <link> and OG og:url, empty = graceful skip
- HtmlShellOptions: New options object for htmlShell(), backward-compatible with legacy string arg

3. Files and Code Sections:

- src/types/report.ts — Added project: string to SearchIndexEntry; added reportBaseUrl?: string to BuildReportOptions

     export interface SearchIndexEntry {
       url: string; title: string; client: string
       project: string  // NEW
       date: string; snippet: string
     }
     // BuildReportOptions:
     reportBaseUrl?: string  // NEW

- src/types/transcript.ts — Added optional tags to ManifestEntry

     tags?: string[]  // AI-extracted keyword tags, optional for backward compat

- src/heartbeat/config.ts — Added report_base_url constant, Config field, defaultConfig, parseRaw, saveConfig

     const DEFAULT_REPORT_BASE_URL = ''
     // Config interface:
     readonly report_base_url: string
     // saveConfig:
     `report_base_url = ${tomlString(cfg.report_base_url ?? DEFAULT_REPORT_BASE_URL)}`
     // parseRaw:
     let reportBaseUrl = raw['report_base_url']
     if (typeof reportBaseUrl !== 'string') reportBaseUrl = DEFAULT_REPORT_BASE_URL

- src/heartbeat/heartbeat.ts — Pass reportBaseUrl to buildReport()

- src/report/render/toc.ts — NEW FILE. Full TOC extraction and rendering

- src/report/render/markdown.ts — Rewrote to add callouts, blockquotes, mermaid

     export function hasMermaidBlocks(md: string): boolean
     export const MERMAID_CDN_SCRIPT = `<script type="module">
     import mermaid from 'https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/npm/mermaid@11/dist/mermaid.esm.min.mjs';
     mermaid.initialize({ startOnLoad: true, theme: ... });
     </script>`
     // mermaid blocks → <pre class="mermaid">{escaped content}</pre>
     // > [!note/tip/warning/...] → <div class="callout callout-{type}">
     // Regular > lines → <blockquote>
     const CALLOUT_ICONS: Record<string, string> = { note:'📝', tip:'💡', ... }

- src/report/render/css.ts — Major additions at end of CSS string:
- Sidebar icon mode (768–1024px): --sidebar-width: 52px, hide .nav-label
- Sidebar collapse: body.sidebar-collapsed rules
- Breadcrumb: .breadcrumb, .breadcrumb-item, .breadcrumb-sep
- TOC: .toc, .toc-toggle, .toc-list, .toc-arrow rotation
- Callouts: .callout, .callout-{type} with dark/light theme variants
- Mermaid: pre.mermaid centered block
- Reader Mode: body.reader-mode hides sidebar/topbar/breadcrumb/meta
- View Transitions: ::view-transition-old/new, vt-fade-out/in keyframes

- src/report/render/layout.ts — Multiple changes:
- SIDEBAR_INLINE_JS: Added sidebar collapse restore on load, collapse toggle, TOC toggle, Reader Mode toggle, View Transitions API handler
- Nav icons: changed to emoji 📊💬📁🕸️🎯✅📚🗄️🔍
- Nav links: added title attr + <span class="nav-label"> wrapping text
- Sidebar: added sidebar-collapse-btn button («)

- src/report/render/rss.ts — NEW FILE

     export function renderRssFeed(
       manifests: ManifestEntry[],
       summaries: Record<string, string>,
       baseUrl: string,  // '' = omit links gracefully

- src/report/build.ts — Added reportBaseUrl variable, renderRssFeed import, writes feed.xml

     import { renderRssFeed } from './render/rss.js'
     const reportBaseUrl = options.reportBaseUrl ?? ''
     writeFileSync(join(output, 'feed.xml'), renderRssFeed(manifests, summaries, reportBaseUrl), 'utf-8')

- src/report/render/search.ts — In progress at summary time:
- Added project to entries in buildSearchIndex()
- Added lastPathSegment() helper
- Rewrote renderSearchPage() with client/project <select> dropdowns
- Filter logic: filterClient && e.client !== filterClient → skip; same for project; combined AND

     function lastPathSegment(p: string): string {
       return p.split(/[/\\]/).filter(Boolean).at(-1) ?? ''
     }
     // renderSearchPage now has:
     var filterClient = clientSel ? clientSel.value : '';
     var filterProject = projectSel ? projectSel.value : '';

- Test files created:
- tests/report/render/toc.test.ts — 12 tests: extractToc, renderToc, injectHeadingIds
- tests/report/render/breadcrumb.test.ts — 7 tests
- tests/report/render/rss.test.ts — 9 tests: XML validity, baseUrl behavior, escaping, 100-item limit, RFC2822

4. Errors and Fixes:
- Stash lost during branch creation: Switched to main while report v1 files didn't exist there → stash pop caused deleted by us conflicts → did git reset --hard HEAD which reset the feat/report-v2 branch and git stash drop had already removed the stash. Fixed by discovering the stash object SHA (93945f39764750a6f11112ebb7faa283a32d4b03) was still in git object store → git stash apply <SHA> on the correct base branch recovered all changes.
- Branch naming conflict: The existing branch fix/codex-parser-duplicates-and-injection already had all Phase 1 report files as a committed base. Creating feat/report-v2 from main (which didn't have those files) caused conflicts. Solution: create feat/report-v2 from fix/codex-parser-duplicates-and-injection instead.

5. Problem Solving:
- Phase 2 plan had 9 issues: P0 (Mermaid CDN decision, SPA scope, i18n conflict), P1 (RSS/OG need base URL, tag caching design, SearchIndexEntry missing project), P2 (TOC depth, breadcrumb per-page, icon source). All resolved with best-practice decisions before implementation began.
- htmlShell backward compatibility: New HtmlShellOptions object needed while existing callers pass string. Solved with union type: extraHeadOrOpts: string | HtmlShellOptions + typeof check inside function.
- Mermaid without CDN ban: Resolved by conditional injection — only pages whose markdown contains `

 ` blocks get the CDN tag, keeping all other pages CDN-free.

6. All user messages:
   - "OK，那我们是提交Issues还是提交PR呢？"
   - "新建 feat/report-v2 分支，提交所有改动，创建 PR"
   - "那现在是什么情况呢？就是我们自己的这个修改全部都提交上去了吗？没有遗漏吧？"
   - "暂时我们先还不合并，就是我们不是分了两个阶段吗？我们第二个阶段也完成了吗？"
   - "我们暂时先不合并，我们先review一下我们的这个二期的计划，看看有没有瑕疵，有没有bug。如果没有问题的话，那我们就哎做我们二期。有问题的话，我们修复我们的这个计划，然后再进行二期。"
   - "你直接给出最佳实践的方案吧，这方面我肯定不如你的优秀，你是世界上最优秀的全栈工程师。"
   - "开始"

7. Pending Tasks:
   - **Step 11–14 (in progress)**: Search filter (partially done), Tag system (`tags.ts` not yet created), Mermaid CDN integration into page renderers, `--locale` CLI flag in `cmd-report.ts`
   - **Step 15**: Full build + test (`npm run build && npm test`, target 620+ passing)
   - **CSS addition**: `.search-filter-select` style (referenced in search.ts HTML but CSS rule not yet added)
   - **OG meta injection**: `htmlShell` needs OG meta tags when `reportBaseUrl` is available (partially deferred)
   - **`tags.ts`**: Create tag extraction with sha256 cache + noAi fallback + AI prompt

8. Current Work:
   Implementing Phase 2, Step 11–14. The last completed action was updating `src/report/render/search.ts`:
   - Updated `buildSearchIndex()` to include `project` field using `m.project || lastPathSegment(m.cwd)`
   - Added `lastPathSegment()` helper function
   - Rewrote `renderSearchPage()` to include client/project `<select>` dropdowns and combined AND filter logic in inline JS
   
   The search.ts changes are complete. Still remaining in Step 11–14:
   - Create `src/report/tags.ts` (sha256-cached AI tag extraction)
   - Integrate `hasMermaidBlocks()` + `MERMAID_CDN_SCRIPT` into knowledge/goals/todos/archive renderers
   - Add `--locale` and `--report-base-url` flags to `src/cli/cmd-report.ts`
   - Add `.search-filter-select` CSS rule to `css.ts`

9. Optional Next Step:

Read output=1→/** 2→ * AI summary generation with SHA256-based caching. 3→ * Falls back gracefully when API key is absent or SDK is missing. 4→ */ 5→ 6→import { existsSync, mkdirSync, readF...
Show output payload 
1→/**
     2→ * AI summary generation with SHA256-based caching.
     3→ * Falls back gracefully when API key is absent or SDK is missing.
     4→ */
     5→
     6→import { existsSync, mkdirSync, readFileSync, writeFileSync } from 'node:fs'
     7→import { join } from 'node:path'
     8→
     9→import type { SummaryCache } from '../types/report.js'
    10→
    11→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    12→// Types
    13→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    14→
    15→export interface SummaryMessage {
    16→  role: string
    17→  text: string
    18→}
    19→
    20→export interface SummaryOptions {
    21→  cacheDir: string
    22→  noAi: boolean
    23→  model: string
    24→}
    25→
    26→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    27→// Semaphore (max 5 concurrent AI calls)
    28→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    29→
    30→let activeCalls = 0
    31→const MAX_CONCURRENT = 5
    32→
    33→async function withSemaphore<T>(fn: () => Promise<T>): Promise<T> {
    34→  while (activeCalls >= MAX_CONCURRENT) {
    35→    await new Promise<void>(resolve => setTimeout(resolve, 100))
    36→  }
    37→  activeCalls++
    38→  try {
    39→    return await fn()
    40→  } finally {
    41→    activeCalls--
    42→  }
    43→}
    44→
    45→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    46→// Main entry point
    47→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    48→
    49→export async function getSummary(
    50→  sha256: string,
    51→  messages: SummaryMessage[],
    52→  options: SummaryOptions,
    53→): Promise<string> {
    54→  // Check cache
    55→  const cached = readCache(sha256, options.cacheDir)
    56→  if (cached !== null) {
    57→    return cached
    58→  }
    59→
    60→  if (options.noAi) return ''

Read output=60→ if (options.noAi) return '' 61→ 62→ const apiKey = process.env['ANTHROPIC_API_KEY'] 63→ if (!apiKey) return '' 64→ 65→ return withSemaphore(() => callApi(sha256, messages, o...
Show output payload 
60→  if (options.noAi) return ''
    61→
    62→  const apiKey = process.env['ANTHROPIC_API_KEY']
    63→  if (!apiKey) return ''
    64→
    65→  return withSemaphore(() => callApi(sha256, messages, options))
    66→}
    67→
    68→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    69→// API call
    70→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    71→
    72→async function callApi(
    73→  sha256: string,
    74→  messages: SummaryMessage[],
    75→  options: SummaryOptions,
    76→): Promise<string> {
    77→  try {
    78→    // Dynamic import so the build never fails if @anthropic-ai/sdk is absent
    79→    const { default: Anthropic } = await (import('@anthropic-ai/sdk') as Promise<{ default: typeof import('@anthropic-ai/sdk').default }>)
    80→
    81→    const client = new Anthropic()
    82→    const prompt = buildSummaryPrompt(messages)
    83→
    84→    const response = await client.messages.create({
    85→      model: options.model,
    86→      max_tokens: 200,
    87→      system:
    88→        'Summarize in 2-3 sentences. What was accomplished? What problems were solved? Be specific. Use past tense.',
    89→      messages: [{ role: 'user', content: prompt }],
    90→    })
    91→
    92→    const block = response.content[0]
    93→    const text = block && block.type === 'text' ? block.text : ''
    94→    if (text) {
    95→      writeCache(sha256, text, options.cacheDir)
    96→    }
    97→    return text
    98→  } catch (err: unknown) {
    99→    // MODULE_NOT_FOUND or any API error — never crash the build
   100→    const code = (err as NodeJS.ErrnoException).code
   101→    if (code !== 'MODULE_NOT_FOUND') {
   102→      console.warn(`[summarize] API call failed: ${String(err)}`)
   103→    }
   104→    return ''
   105→  }
   106→}
   107→
   108→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   109→// Prompt construction
   110→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   111→
   112→/** First 30 messages, each truncated to 500 chars. */
   113→export function buildSummaryPrompt(messages: SummaryMessage[]): string {
   114→  const slice = messages.slice(0, 30)
   115→  const lines = slice.map(m => {
   116→    const text = m.text.slice(0, 500)
   117→    return `${m.role.toUpperCase()}: ${text}`
   118→  })
   119→  return lines.join('\n\n')
   120→}
   121→
   122→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   123→// Cache read / write
   124→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   125→
   126→export function readCache(sha256: string, cacheDir: string): string | null {
   127→  const path = cachePath(sha256, cacheDir)
   128→  if (!existsSync(path)) return null
   129→  try {
   130→    const raw = readFileSync(path, 'utf-8')
   131→    const entry = JSON.parse(raw) as SummaryCache
   132→    return entry.summary ?? null
   133→  } catch {
   134→    return null
   135→  }
   136→}
   137→
   138→export function writeCache(sha256: string, summary: string, cacheDir: string): void {
   139→  try {
   140→    mkdirSync(cacheDir, { recursive: true })
   141→    const entry: SummaryCache = {
   142→      sha256,
   143→      summary,
   144→      generated_at: new Date().toISOString(),
   145→    }
   146→    writeFileSync(cachePath(sha256, cacheDir), JSON.stringify(entry, null, 2) + '\n', 'utf-8')
   147→  } catch {
   148→    // Never crash on cache write failure
   149→  }
   150→}
   151→
   152→function cachePath(sha256: string, cacheDir: string): string {
   153→  return join(cacheDir, `${sha256}.json`)
   154→}
   155→

Tool Result #814 Read toolu_01XSNzfyhdT4ZD6npM2P3x7A 15:40:18
Read output=1→/** 2→ * CLI adapters for `memorytree report build` and `memorytree report serve`. 3→ */ 4→ 5→import { createServer } from 'node:http' 6→import { createReadStream, existsSync,...
Show output payload 
1→/**
     2→ * CLI adapters for `memorytree report build` and `memorytree report serve`.
     3→ */
     4→
     5→import { createServer } from 'node:http'
     6→import { createReadStream, existsSync, statSync } from 'node:fs'
     7→import { extname, join, resolve, sep } from 'node:path'
     8→
     9→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    10→// Build command
    11→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    12→
    13→export interface CmdReportBuildOptions {
    14→  root: string
    15→  output: string
    16→  noAi: boolean
    17→  model: string
    18→}
    19→
    20→export async function cmdReportBuild(opts: CmdReportBuildOptions): Promise<number> {
    21→  const { buildReport } = await import('../report/build.js')
    22→  try {
    23→    await buildReport({
    24→      root: resolve(opts.root),
    25→      output: resolve(opts.output),
    26→      noAi: opts.noAi,
    27→      model: opts.model,
    28→    })
    29→    console.log(`Report generated at: ${resolve(opts.output)}`)
    30→    console.log(`Open: ${join(resolve(opts.output), 'index.html')}`)
    31→    return 0
    32→  } catch (err: unknown) {
    33→    console.error(`Report build failed: ${String(err)}`)
    34→    return 1
    35→  }
    36→}
    37→
    38→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    39→// Serve command
    40→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    41→
    42→export interface CmdReportServeOptions {
    43→  dir: string
    44→  port: number
    45→}
    46→
    47→export function cmdReportServe(opts: CmdReportServeOptions): number {
    48→  const root = resolve(opts.dir)
    49→  const port = opts.port
    50→
    51→  const MIME: Record<string, string> = {
    52→    '.html': 'text/html; charset=utf-8',
    53→    '.css': 'text/css',
    54→    '.js': 'application/javascript',
    55→    '.json': 'application/json',
    56→    '.svg': 'image/svg+xml',
    57→    '.ico': 'image/x-icon',
    58→  }
    59→
    60→  const server = createServer((req, res) => {
    61→    let urlPath: string
    62→    try {
    63→      urlPath = decodeURIComponent((req.url ?? '/').split('?')[0] ?? '/')
    64→    } catch {
    65→      res.writeHead(400, { 'Content-Type': 'text/plain' })
    66→      res.end('Bad request')
    67→      return
    68→    }
    69→
    70→    let filePath = join(root, urlPath)
    71→
    72→    // Containment check: reject paths that escape the serve root
    73→    // Use sep (\ on Windows, / on Unix) so the check works cross-platform.
    74→    if (!filePath.startsWith(root + sep) && filePath !== root) {
    75→      res.writeHead(403, { 'Content-Type': 'text/plain' })
    76→      res.end('Forbidden')
    77→      return
    78→    }
    79→
    80→    // Serve index.html for directory requests
    81→    if (existsSync(filePath) && statSync(filePath).isDirectory()) {
    82→      filePath = join(filePath, 'index.html')
    83→    }
    84→
    85→    if (!existsSync(filePath)) {
    86→      res.writeHead(404, { 'Content-Type': 'text/plain' })
    87→      res.end('Not found')
    88→      return
    89→    }
    90→
    91→    const ext = extname(filePath)
    92→    const contentType = MIME[ext] ?? 'application/octet-stream'
    93→    res.writeHead(200, { 'Content-Type': contentType })
    94→    const stream = createReadStream(filePath)
    95→    stream.on('error', () => { res.destroy() })
    96→    stream.pipe(res)
    97→  })
    98→
    99→  server.on('error', (err: NodeJS.ErrnoException) => {
   100→    if (err.code === 'EADDRINUSE') {
   101→      console.error(`Error: port ${port} is already in use.`)
   102→      process.exit(1)
   103→    }
   104→    console.error(`Server error: ${String(err)}`)
   105→    process.exit(1)
   106→  })
   107→
   108→  server.listen(port, () => {
   109→    console.log(`Serving ${root}`)
   110→    console.log(`Open: http://localhost:${port}/`)
   111→    console.log('Press Ctrl+C to stop.')
   112→  })
   113→
   114→  return 0
   115→}
   116→
