Show output payload 
1→/**
     2→ * MemoryTree CLI — unified entry point.
     3→ * Registers all subcommands via commander.
     4→ */
     5→
     6→import { Command } from 'commander'
     7→
     8→const program = new Command()
     9→
    10→program
    11→  .name('memorytree')
    12→  .description('MemoryTree — transcript import, dedup, indexing, and session continuity')
    13→  .version('0.1.0')
    14→
    15→// ── init ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    16→
    17→program
    18→  .command('init')
    19→  .description('Initialize a MemoryTree workspace in a repository')
    20→  .option('--root <path>', 'Target repository root', '.')
    21→  .option('--project-name <name>', 'Project name', 'this project')
    22→  .option('--goal-summary <text>', 'Initial goal summary', 'Describe the long-term project goal here.')
    23→  .option('--locale <locale>', 'Template locale: auto, en, or zh-cn', 'auto')
    24→  .option('--date <date>', 'Override date as YYYY-MM-DD')
    25→  .option('--time <time>', 'Override time as HH:MM')
    26→  .option('--skip-agents', 'Deprecated — use upgrade instead')
    27→  .option('--force', 'Overwrite existing generated files')
    28→  .action(async (opts) => {
    29→    const { cmdInit } = await import('./cmd-init.js')
    30→    process.exitCode = cmdInit({
    31→      root: opts.root,
    32→      projectName: opts.projectName,
    33→      goalSummary: opts.goalSummary,
    34→      locale: opts.locale,
    35→      date: opts.date ?? '',
    36→      time: opts.time ?? '',
    37→      skipAgents: opts.skipAgents ?? false,
    38→      force: opts.force ?? false,
    39→    })
    40→  })
    41→
    42→// ── upgrade ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    43→
    44→program
    45→  .command('upgrade')
    46→  .description('Upgrade a repository to MemoryTree without overwriting existing policy')
    47→  .option('--root <path>', 'Target repository root', '.')
    48→  .option('--project-name <name>', 'Project name', 'this project')
    49→  .option('--goal-summary <text>', 'Fallback goal summary', 'Describe the long-term project goal here.')
    50→  .option('--locale <locale>', 'Requested locale: auto, en, or zh-cn', 'auto')
    51→  .option('--date <date>', 'Override date as YYYY-MM-DD')
    52→  .option('--time <time>', 'Override time as HH:MM')
    53→  .option('--format <format>', 'Output format: text or json', 'text')
    54→  .action(async (opts) => {
    55→    const { cmdUpgrade } = await import('./cmd-upgrade.js')
    56→    process.exitCode = cmdUpgrade({
    57→      root: opts.root,
    58→      projectName: opts.projectName,
    59→      goalSummary: opts.goalSummary,
    60→      locale: opts.locale,
    61→      date: opts.date ?? '',
    62→      time: opts.time ?? '',
    63→      format: opts.format,
    64→    })
    65→  })
    66→
    67→// ── import ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    68→
    69→program
    70→  .command('import')
    71→  .description('Import one local transcript into MemoryTree archives')
    72→  .requiredOption('--source <path>', 'Raw transcript source file path')
    73→  .option('--root <path>', 'Target repository root', '.')
    74→  .option('--client <client>', 'Transcript client: auto, codex, claude, gemini', 'auto')
    75→  .option('--project-name <name>', 'Project label', '')
    76→  .option('--global-root <path>', 'Override global transcript root')
    77→  .option('--raw-upload-permission <perm>', 'Permission: not-set, approved, denied', 'not-set')
    78→  .option('--format <format>', 'Output format: text or json', 'text')
    79→  .action(async (opts) => {
    80→    const { cmdImport } = await import('./cmd-import.js')
    81→    process.exitCode = await cmdImport({
    82→      root: opts.root,
    83→      source: opts.source,
    84→      client: opts.client,
    85→      projectName: opts.projectName,
    86→      globalRoot: opts.globalRoot ?? '',
    87→      rawUploadPermission: opts.rawUploadPermission,
    88→      format: opts.format,
    89→    })
    90→  })
    91→
    92→// ── discover ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    93→
    94→program
    95→  .command('discover')
    96→  .description('Discover and import local AI transcripts')
    97→  .option('--root <path>', 'Target repository root', '.')
    98→  .option('--client <client>', 'Client filter: all, codex, claude, gemini', 'all')
    99→  .option('--scope <scope>', 'Scope: current-project or all-projects', 'all-projects')
   100→  .option('--project-name <name>', 'Project label', '')
   101→  .option('--global-root <path>', 'Override global transcript root')
   102→  .option('--raw-upload-permission <perm>', 'Permission: not-set, approved, denied', 'not-set')
   103→  .option('--limit <n>', 'Limit discovered sources', '0')
   104→  .option('--format <format>', 'Output format: text or json', 'text')
   105→  .action(async (opts) => {
   106→    const { cmdDiscover } = await import('./cmd-discover.js')
   107→    process.exitCode = await cmdDiscover({
   108→      root: opts.root,
   109→      client: opts.client,
   110→      scope: opts.scope,
   111→      projectName: opts.projectName,
   112→      globalRoot: opts.globalRoot ?? '',
   113→      rawUploadPermission: opts.rawUploadPermission,
   114→      limit: parseInt(opts.limit, 10) || 0,
   115→      format: opts.format,
   116→    })
   117→  })
   118→
   119→// ── locale ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   120→
   121→program
   122→  .command('locale')
   123→  .description('Detect the effective locale for a repository')
   124→  .option('--root <path>', 'Target repository root', '.')
   125→  .option('--locale <locale>', 'Requested locale value', 'auto')
   126→  .option('--format <format>', 'Output format: text or json', 'text')
   127→  .action(async (opts) => {
   128→    const { cmdLocale } = await import('./cmd-locale.js')
   129→    process.exitCode = cmdLocale({
   130→      root: opts.root,
   131→      locale: opts.locale,
   132→      format: opts.format,
   133→    })
   134→  })
   135→
   136→// ── recall ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   137→
   138→program
   139→  .command('recall')
   140→  .description('On-demand transcript sync and latest session recall')
   141→  .option('--root <path>', 'Target repository root', '.')
   142→  .option('--project-name <name>', 'Project label', '')
   143→  .option('--global-root <path>', 'Override global transcript root')
   144→  .option('--activation-time <time>', 'ISO timestamp of session activation')
   145→  .option('--format <format>', 'Output format: text or json', 'text')
   146→  .action(async (opts) => {
   147→    const { cmdRecall } = await import('./cmd-recall.js')
   148→    process.exitCode = await cmdRecall({
   149→      root: opts.root,
   150→      projectName: opts.projectName,
   151→      globalRoot: opts.globalRoot ?? '',
   152→      activationTime: opts.activationTime ?? '',
   153→      format: opts.format,
   154→    })
   155→  })
   156→
   157→// ── report ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   158→
   159→const report = program
   160→  .command('report')
   161→  .description('Generate or serve the MemoryTree HTML report website')
   162→
   163→report
   164→  .command('build')
   165→  .description('Build a self-contained HTML report website from Memory/')
   166→  .option('--root <path>', 'Repository root (must contain Memory/)', '.')
   167→  .option(
   168→    '--output <path>',
   169→    'Output directory (default: <root>/Memory/07_reports)',
   170→    '',
   171→  )
   172→  .option('--no-ai', 'Disable AI-generated session summaries')
   173→  .option(
   174→    '--model <model>',
   175→    'Claude model for AI summaries',
   176→    'claude-haiku-4-5-20251001',
   177→  )
   178→  .action(async (opts) => {
   179→    const { cmdReportBuild } = await import('./cmd-report.js')
   180→    const root = opts.root as string
   181→    const rawOutput = opts.output as string
   182→    const output = rawOutput || `${root}/Memory/07_reports`
   183→    // Commander's --no-ai sets opts.ai = false
   184→    const noAi = opts.ai === false
   185→    process.exitCode = await cmdReportBuild({
   186→      root,
   187→      output,
   188→      noAi,
   189→      model: opts.model as string,
   190→    })
   191→  })
   192→
   193→report
   194→  .command('serve')
   195→  .description('Serve the generated report website on a local HTTP server')
   196→  .option(
   197→    '--dir <path>',
   198→    'Report directory to serve (default: ./Memory/07_reports)',
   199→    './Memory/07_reports',
   200→  )
   201→  .option('--port <n>', 'Port to listen on', '4321')
   202→  .action(async (opts) => {
   203→    const { cmdReportServe } = await import('./cmd-report.js')
   204→    process.exitCode = cmdReportServe({
   205→      dir: opts.dir as string,
   206→      port: parseInt(opts.port as string, 10) || 4321,
   207→    })
   208→  })
   209→
   210→// ── daemon ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   211→
   212→const daemon = program
   213→  .command('daemon')
   214→  .description('Manage the MemoryTree heartbeat lifecycle')
   215→
   216→daemon
   217→  .command('install')
   218→  .description('Register heartbeat with the OS scheduler')
   219→  .option('--interval <interval>', 'Override heartbeat interval (e.g., "5m")')
   220→  .option('--auto-push <bool>', 'Override auto_push setting (true/false)')
   221→  .action(async (opts) => {
   222→    const { cmdInstall } = await import('./cmd-daemon.js')
   223→    process.exitCode = cmdInstall({
   224→      interval: opts.interval,
   225→      autoPush: opts.autoPush,
   226→    })
   227→  })
   228→
   229→daemon
   230→  .command('uninstall')
   231→  .description('Remove the heartbeat scheduled task')
   232→  .action(async () => {
   233→    const { cmdUninstall } = await import('./cmd-daemon.js')
   234→    process.exitCode = cmdUninstall()
   235→  })
   236→
   237→daemon
   238→  .command('run-once')
   239→  .description('Execute a single heartbeat cycle now')
   240→  .action(async () => {
   241→    const { cmdRunOnce } = await import('./cmd-daemon.js')
   242→    process.exitCode = await cmdRunOnce()
   243→  })
   244→
   245→daemon
   246→  .command('watch')
   247→  .description('Continuous heartbeat loop (development only)')
   248→  .option('--interval <interval>', 'Override interval')
   249→  .action(async (opts) => {
   250→    const { cmdWatch } = await import('./cmd-daemon.js')
   251→    process.exitCode = await cmdWatch({ interval: opts.interval })
   252→  })
   253→
   254→daemon
   255→  .command('status')
   256→  .description('Show heartbeat registration and lock state')
   257→  .action(async () => {
   258→    const { cmdStatus } = await import('./cmd-daemon.js')
   259→    process.exitCode = cmdStatus()
   260→  })
   261→
   262→program.parse()
   263→

Tool Call #827 Write toolu_016k2jC7RDKnauH7YNbGo9jp 15:41:08
Write input={"content":"/**\n * AI tag extraction with SHA256-based caching.\n * Extracts 3–5 keyword tags from a session summary using Claude.\n * Falls back to [] when noAi=true, API key ...
Show input payload 
{
  "file_path": "C:/Users/ai/.codex/skills/memorytree-workflow/src/report/tags.ts",
  "content": "/**\n * AI tag extraction with SHA256-based caching.\n * Extracts 3–5 keyword tags from a session summary using Claude.\n * Falls back to [] when noAi=true, API key absent, or any error occurs.\n */\n\nimport { existsSync, mkdirSync, readFileSync, writeFileSync } from 'node:fs'\nimport { join } from 'node:path'\n\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n// Types\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\nexport interface TagOptions {\n  cacheDir: string\n  noAi: boolean\n  model: string\n}\n\ninterface TagCache {\n  sha256: string\n  tags: string[]\n  generated_at: string\n}\n\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n// Main entry point\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\nexport async function getTags(\n  sha256: string,\n  summary: string,\n  options: TagOptions,\n): Promise<string[]> {\n  // noAi mode: skip entirely\n  if (options.noAi) return []\n\n  // No summary → nothing to tag\n  if (!summary.trim()) return []\n\n  // Check cache first\n  const cached = readTagCache(sha256, options.cacheDir)\n  if (cached !== null) return cached\n\n  const apiKey = process.env['ANTHROPIC_API_KEY']\n  if (!apiKey) return []\n\n  return callTagApi(sha256, summary, options)\n}\n\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n// API call\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\nasync function callTagApi(\n  sha256: string,\n  summary: string,\n  options: TagOptions,\n): Promise<string[]> {\n  try {\n    const { default: Anthropic } = await (import('@anthropic-ai/sdk') as Promise<{ default: typeof import('@anthropic-ai/sdk').default }>)\n\n    const client = new Anthropic()\n    const response = await client.messages.create({\n      model: options.model,\n      max_tokens: 60,\n      system: 'You are a tag extractor. Respond with a JSON array only — no prose, no markdown.',\n      messages: [\n        {\n          role: 'user',\n          content: `Extract 3-5 short keyword tags from this session summary. Respond with JSON array only: [\"tag1\",\"tag2\"]\\n\\n${summary.slice(0, 500)}`,\n        },\n      ],\n    })\n\n    const block = response.content[0]\n    const text = block && block.type === 'text' ? block.text.trim() : ''\n    if (!text) return []\n\n    const parsed: unknown = JSON.parse(text)\n    if (!Array.isArray(parsed)) return []\n\n    const tags = parsed\n      .filter((t): t is string => typeof t === 'string' && t.length > 0)\n      .slice(0, 5)\n      .map(t => t.trim().toLowerCase())\n      .filter(t => t.length > 0)\n\n    if (tags.length > 0) {\n      writeTagCache(sha256, tags, options.cacheDir)\n    }\n    return tags\n  } catch {\n    // Never crash — API errors, JSON parse errors, missing SDK\n    return []\n  }\n}\n\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n// Rendering\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n/** Render tags as pill badges. Returns '' for empty array. */\nexport function renderTagBadges(tags: string[]): string {\n  if (tags.length === 0) return ''\n  const pills = tags\n    .map(t => `<span class=\"tag\" data-tag=\"${escTag(t)}\">${escTag(t)}</span>`)\n    .join('')\n  return `<div class=\"tag-list\">${pills}</div>`\n}\n\nfunction escTag(s: string): string {\n  return s\n    .replace(/&/g, '&amp;')\n    .replace(/</g, '&lt;')\n    .replace(/>/g, '&gt;')\n    .replace(/\"/g, '&quot;')\n}\n\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n// Cache helpers\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\nfunction readTagCache(sha256: string, cacheDir: string): string[] | null {\n  const path = tagCachePath(sha256, cacheDir)\n  if (!existsSync(path)) return null\n  try {\n    const raw = readFileSync(path, 'utf-8')\n    const entry = JSON.parse(raw) as TagCache\n    return Array.isArray(entry.tags) ? entry.tags : null\n  } catch {\n    return null\n  }\n}\n\nfunction writeTagCache(sha256: string, tags: string[], cacheDir: string): void {\n  try {\n    const tagsDir = join(cacheDir, 'tags')\n    mkdirSync(tagsDir, { recursive: true })\n    const entry: TagCache = { sha256, tags, generated_at: new Date().toISOString() }\n    writeFileSync(tagCachePath(sha256, cacheDir), JSON.stringify(entry, null, 2) + '\\n', 'utf-8')\n  } catch {\n    // Best-effort\n  }\n}\n\nfunction tagCachePath(sha256: string, cacheDir: string): string {\n  return join(cacheDir, 'tags', `${sha256}.json`)\n}\n"
}

  "replace_all": false,
  "file_path": "C:/Users/ai/.codex/skills/memorytree-workflow/src/report/render/css.ts",
  "old_string": "/* ── Search filter dropdowns ── */",
  "new_string": "/* ── Tag badges ── */\n.tag-list { display: flex; flex-wrap: wrap; gap: 0.3rem; margin-top: 0.35rem; }\n.tag {\n  display: inline-block;\n  background: var(--bg-secondary);\n  border: 1px solid var(--border);\n  color: var(--text-muted);\n  border-radius: 999px;\n  padding: 0.15rem 0.55rem;\n  font-size: 0.72rem;\n  cursor: pointer;\n  transition: background 0.15s, color 0.15s;\n}\n.tag:hover { background: var(--accent); color: #fff; border-color: var(--accent); }\n\n/* ── Search filter dropdowns ── */"
}

}

  "old_string": "report\n  .command('build')\n  .description('Build a self-contained HTML report website from Memory/')\n  .option('--root <path>', 'Repository root (must contain Memory/)', '.')\n  .option(\n    '--output <path>',\n    'Output directory (default: <root>/Memory/07_reports)',\n    '',\n  )\n  .option('--no-ai', 'Disable AI-generated session summaries')\n  .option(\n    '--model <model>',\n    'Claude model for AI summaries',\n    'claude-haiku-4-5-20251001',\n  )\n  .action(async (opts) => {\n    const { cmdReportBuild } = await import('./cmd-report.js')\n    const root = opts.root as string\n    const rawOutput = opts.output as string\n    const output = rawOutput || `${root}/Memory/07_reports`\n    // Commander's --no-ai sets opts.ai = false\n    const noAi = opts.ai === false\n    process.exitCode = await cmdReportBuild({\n      root,\n      output,\n      noAi,\n      model: opts.model as string,\n    })\n  })",
  "new_string": "report\n  .command('build')\n  .description('Build a self-contained HTML report website from Memory/')\n  .option('--root <path>', 'Repository root (must contain Memory/)', '.')\n  .option(\n    '--output <path>',\n    'Output directory (default: <root>/Memory/07_reports)',\n    '',\n  )\n  .option('--no-ai', 'Disable AI-generated session summaries')\n  .option(\n    '--model <model>',\n    'Claude model for AI summaries',\n    'claude-haiku-4-5-20251001',\n  )\n  .option('--locale <locale>', 'Report locale: en or zh-CN', '')\n  .option('--report-base-url <url>', 'Absolute base URL for RSS and OG meta (e.g. https://memory.example.com)', '')\n  .action(async (opts) => {\n    const { cmdReportBuild } = await import('./cmd-report.js')\n    const root = opts.root as string\n    const rawOutput = opts.output as string\n    const output = rawOutput || `${root}/Memory/07_reports`\n    // Commander's --no-ai sets opts.ai = false\n    const noAi = opts.ai === false\n    process.exitCode = await cmdReportBuild({\n      root,\n      output,\n      noAi,\n      model: opts.model as string,\n      locale: (opts.locale as string) || undefined,\n      reportBaseUrl: (opts.reportBaseUrl as string) || undefined,\n    })\n  })"
}

1→/**
     2→ * Session list page: tabular index of all imported transcripts, with client tab filtering.
     3→ */
     4→
     5→import type { ManifestEntry } from '../../types/transcript.js'
     6→import type { Translations } from '../i18n/types.js'
     7→import { clientBadge, escHtml, htmlShell, renderNav, transcriptUrlFromRoot } from './layout.js'
     8→
     9→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    10→// Transcript list
    11→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    12→
    13→export function renderTranscriptList(manifests: ManifestEntry[], t?: Translations, summaries?: Record<string, string>): string {
    14→  const nav = renderNav('transcripts', 1, t)
    15→
    16→  const sorted = [...manifests].sort((a, b) => b.started_at.localeCompare(a.started_at))
    17→
    18→  const title = t?.sessions.title ?? 'Sessions'
    19→  const clientLabel = t?.sessions.client ?? 'Client'
    20→  const dateLabel = t?.sessions.date ?? 'Date'
    21→  const idLabel = t?.sessions.id ?? 'ID'
    22→  const msgsLabel = t?.sessions.msgs ?? 'Msgs'
    23→  const toolsLabel = t?.sessions.tools ?? 'Tools'
    24→  const allLabel = t?.sessions.all ?? 'All'
    25→
    26→  if (sorted.length === 0) {
    27→    const content = `<div class="page-header">
    28→  <h1>${escHtml(title)}</h1>
    29→  <p class="subtitle">${escHtml(t?.sessions.noSessions ?? 'No sessions imported yet.')}</p>
    30→</div>`
    31→    return htmlShell(title, content, nav)
    32→  }
    33→
    34→  // Collect unique clients
    35→  const clientSet = new Set<string>()
    36→  for (const m of sorted) clientSet.add(m.client)
    37→  const clients = [...clientSet].sort()
    38→
    39→  // Tab bar
    40→  const tabBar = renderTabBar(allLabel, clients, sorted)
    41→
    42→  const rows = sorted
    43→    .map(m => {
    44→      const url = transcriptHref(m)
    45→      const date = m.started_at.slice(0, 10)
    46→      const time = m.started_at.slice(11, 16)
    47→      const badge = clientBadge(m.client)
    48→      const summary = summaries?.[m.session_id] ?? ''
    49→      const meta = `${escHtml(m.client)} · ${escHtml(date)} · ${m.message_count} msgs`
    50→      return `<tr data-client="${escHtml(m.client)}">
    51→  <td>${badge}</td>
    52→  <td><a href="${escHtml(url)}" data-summary="${escHtml(summary)}" data-meta="${escHtml(meta)}">${escHtml(m.title || m.session_id)}</a></td>
    53→  <td style="color:var(--text-muted)">${escHtml(date)} ${escHtml(time)}</td>
    54→  <td style="color:var(--text-muted);font-family:var(--font-mono);font-size:0.8rem">${escHtml(m.session_id.slice(0, 8))}</td>
    55→  <td style="text-align:right;color:var(--text-muted)">${m.message_count}</td>
    56→  <td style="text-align:right;color:var(--text-muted)">${m.tool_event_count}</td>
    57→</tr>`
    58→    })
    59→    .join('')
    60→
    61→  const filterJs = `<script>
    62→(function() {
    63→  document.addEventListener('DOMContentLoaded', function() {
    64→    var tabs = document.querySelectorAll('.tab-btn');
    65→    var rows = document.querySelectorAll('tr[data-client]');
    66→    tabs.forEach(function(btn) {
    67→      btn.addEventListener('click', function() {
    68→        tabs.forEach(function(b) { b.classList.remove('active'); });
    69→        btn.classList.add('active');
    70→        var filter = btn.getAttribute('data-filter');
    71→        rows.forEach(function(row) {
    72→          if (!filter || row.getAttribute('data-client') === filter) {
    73→            row.style.display = '';
    74→          } else {
    75→            row.style.display = 'none';
    76→          }
    77→        });
    78→      });
    79→    });
    80→  });

/**
 * Todos page: renders markdown files from Memory/02_todos/.
 */

import type { Translations } from '../i18n/types.js'
import { escHtml, htmlShell, renderNav, slugifyName } from './layout.js'
import type { MarkdownFile } from './layout.js'
import { markdownToHtml } from './markdown.js'

export type TodoFile = MarkdownFile

export function renderTodos(files: TodoFile[], t?: Translations): string {
  const nav = renderNav('todos', 1, t)
  const title = t?.nav.todos ?? 'Todos'

  if (files.length === 0) {
    const content = `<div class="page-header">
  <h1>${escHtml(title)}</h1>
  <p class="subtitle">No todo files found in Memory/02_todos/.</p>
</div>`
    return htmlShell(title, content, nav)
  }

  const sections = files
    .map(f => {
      const htmlContent = markdownToHtml(f.content)
      return `<div class="card" id="${escHtml(slugifyName(f.filename))}">
  <h2>${escHtml(f.title)}</h2>
  <p style="font-size:0.8rem;color:var(--text-muted);margin-bottom:1rem">${escHtml(f.filename)}</p>
  <div class="markdown-body">${htmlContent}</div>
</div>`
    })
    .join('\n')

  const content = `<div class="page-header">
  <h1>${escHtml(title)}</h1>
  <p class="subtitle">${files.length} todo file(s)</p>
</div>
${sections}`

  return htmlShell(title, content, nav)
}
---
/**
 * Goals page: renders markdown files from Memory/01_goals/.
 */

import type { Translations } from '../i18n/types.js'
import { escHtml, htmlShell, renderNav, slugifyName } from './layout.js'
import type { MarkdownFile } from './layout.js'
import { markdownToHtml } from './markdown.js'
import { extractToc, renderToc, injectHeadingIds } from './toc.js'

export type GoalFile = MarkdownFile

export function renderGoals(files: GoalFile[], t?: Translations): string {
  const nav = renderNav('goals', 1, t)
  const title = t?.nav.goals ?? 'Goals'

  if (files.length === 0) {
    const content = `<div class="page-header">
  <h1>${escHtml(title)}</h1>
  <p class="subtitle">No goal files found in Memory/01_goals/.</p>
</div>`
    return htmlShell(title, content, nav)
  }

  const sections = files
    .map(f => {
      const tocEntries = extractToc(f.content)
      const rawHtml = markdownToHtml(f.content)
      const htmlContent = injectHeadingIds(rawHtml, tocEntries)
      const toc = renderToc(tocEntries)
      return `<div class="card" id="${escHtml(slugifyName(f.filename))}">
  <h2>${escHtml(f.title)}</h2>
  <p style="font-size:0.8rem;color:var(--text-muted);margin-bottom:0.5rem">${escHtml(f.filename)}</p>
  ${toc}
  <div class="markdown-body">${htmlContent}</div>
</div>`
    })
    .join('\n')

  const content = `<div class="page-header">
  <h1>${escHtml(title)}</h1>
  <p class="subtitle">${files.length} goal file(s)</p>
</div>
${sections}`

  return htmlShell(title, content, nav)
}

Read output=1→/** 2→ * Goals page: renders markdown files from Memory/01_goals/. 3→ */ 4→ 5→import type { Translations } from '../i18n/types.js' 6→import { escHtml, htmlShell, renderNav, sl...
Show output payload 
1→/**
     2→ * Goals page: renders markdown files from Memory/01_goals/.
     3→ */
     4→
     5→import type { Translations } from '../i18n/types.js'
     6→import { escHtml, htmlShell, renderNav, slugifyName } from './layout.js'
     7→import type { MarkdownFile } from './layout.js'
     8→import { markdownToHtml } from './markdown.js'
     9→import { extractToc, renderToc, injectHeadingIds } from './toc.js'
    10→
    11→export type GoalFile = MarkdownFile
    12→
    13→export function renderGoals(files: GoalFile[], t?: Translations): string {
    14→  const nav = renderNav('goals', 1, t)
    15→  const title = t?.nav.goals ?? 'Goals'
    16→
    17→  if (files.length === 0) {
    18→    const content = `<div class="page-header">
    19→  <h1>${escHtml(title)}</h1>
    20→  <p class="subtitle">No goal files found in Memory/01_goals/.</p>
    21→</div>`
    22→    return htmlShell(title, content, nav)
    23→  }
    24→
    25→  const sections = files
    26→    .map(f => {
    27→      const tocEntries = extractToc(f.content)
    28→      const rawHtml = markdownToHtml(f.content)
    29→      const htmlContent = injectHeadingIds(rawHtml, tocEntries)
    30→      const toc = renderToc(tocEntries)
    31→      return `<div class="card" id="${escHtml(slugifyName(f.filename))}">
    32→  <h2>${escHtml(f.title)}</h2>
    33→  <p style="font-size:0.8rem;color:var(--text-muted);margin-bottom:0.5rem">${escHtml(f.filename)}</p>
    34→  ${toc}
    35→  <div class="markdown-body">${htmlContent}</div>
    36→</div>`
    37→    })
    38→    .join('\n')
    39→
    40→  const content = `<div class="page-header">
    41→  <h1>${escHtml(title)}</h1>
    42→  <p class="subtitle">${files.length} goal file(s)</p>
    43→</div>
    44→${sections}`
    45→
    46→  return htmlShell(title, content, nav)
    47→}
    48→

    16→  if (files.length === 0) {
    17→    const content = `<div class="page-header">
    18→  <h1>${escHtml(title)}</h1>
    19→  <p class="subtitle">No todo files found in Memory/02_todos/.</p>

    31→</div>`

30→import { renderGraph } from './render/graph.js'
    31→import { buildSearchIndex, renderSearchPage } from './render/search.js'
    32→import { buildLinkGraph } from './render/links.js'
    33→import { renderRssFeed } from './render/rss.js'
    34→import { getSummary } from './summarize.js'
    35→import type { SummaryOptions } from './summarize.js'
    36→import { loadLocale } from './i18n/index.js'
    37→import type { Translations } from './i18n/types.js'
    38→import { getLogger } from '../heartbeat/log.js'
    39→
    40→const DEFAULT_MODEL = 'claude-haiku-4-5-20251001'
    41→
    42→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    43→// Main entry point
    44→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    45→
    46→export async function buildReport(options: BuildReportOptions): Promise<void> {
    47→  const { root, output } = options
    48→  const noAi = options.noAi ?? false
    49→  const model = options.model ?? DEFAULT_MODEL
    50→  const locale = options.locale ?? 'en'
    51→  const ghPagesBranch = options.ghPagesBranch ?? ''
    52→  const cname = options.cname ?? ''
    53→  const webhookUrl = options.webhookUrl ?? ''
    54→  const newSessionIds = options.newSessionIds ?? []
    55→  const reportBaseUrl = options.reportBaseUrl ?? ''
    56→
    57→  // Cache lives outside the gitignored output dir so it survives rm -rf
    58→  const cacheDir = join(root, 'Memory', '.report-cache')
    59→
    60→  // Load translations
    61→  const t: Translations = loadLocale(locale)
    62→
    63→  // Ensure output dirs exist
    64→  mkdirSync(output, { recursive: true })
    65→  mkdirSync(join(output, 'transcripts'), { recursive: true })
    66→  mkdirSync(join(output, 'goals'), { recursive: true })
    67→  mkdirSync(join(output, 'knowledge'), { recursive: true })
    68→  mkdirSync(join(output, 'todos'), { recursive: true })
    69→  mkdirSync(join(output, 'archive'), { recursive: true })

Tool Result #883 Read toolu_01SsxRHngmxDUcnANFE5eJK8 15:43:56
Read output=85→ 86→ // Summary options 87→ const summaryOptions: SummaryOptions = { cacheDir, noAi, model } 88→ 89→ // Process each transcript: parse → summarize → render → write → discard ...
Show output payload 
85→
    86→  // Summary options
    87→  const summaryOptions: SummaryOptions = { cacheDir, noAi, model }
    88→
    89→  // Process each transcript: parse → summarize → render → write → discard
    90→  const snippets: Record<string, string> = {}
    91→  const summaries: Record<string, string> = {}
    92→  const summaryPromises: Array<Promise<void>> = []
    93→
    94→  for (const m of manifests) {
    95→    const messages = parseCleanMarkdownMessages(m, root)
    96→
    97→    // Tool events from parsed transcript (try raw path)
    98→    const rawPath = resolveRawPath(m, root)
    99→    if (rawPath && existsSync(rawPath)) {
   100→      try {
   101→        const toolSummaries = extractToolSummariesFromRaw(rawPath, m.client)
   102→        const names = extractToolNames(toolSummaries)
   103→        toolCounts = accumulateToolCounts(toolCounts, names)
   104→      } catch {
   105→        // Skip if raw parsing fails
   106→      }
   107→    }
   108→
   109→    // Search snippet from first message text
   110→    const firstMsg = messages[0]
   111→    snippets[m.session_id] = firstMsg ? firstMsg.text.slice(0, 300) : ''
   112→
   113→    // Summarize + render (deferred to allow parallel API calls)
   114→    const manifest = m
   115→    const msgs = messages
   116→    summaryPromises.push(
   117→      (async () => {
   118→        try {
   119→        const summary = await getSummary(manifest.raw_sha256, msgs, summaryOptions)
   120→        summaries[manifest.session_id] = summary
   121→        const backlinkIds = linkGraph.backlinks[manifest.session_id] ?? []
   122→        const backlinkManifests = backlinkIds
   123→          .map(id => manifests.find(x => x.session_id === id))
   124→          .filter((x): x is ManifestEntry => x !== undefined)
   125→
   126→        const html = renderTranscript(msgs, manifest, summary, backlinkManifests, t)
   127→        const outPath = transcriptOutputPath(output, manifest)
   128→        mkdirSync(dirname(outPath), { recursive: true })
   129→        writeFileSync(outPath, html, 'utf-8')
   130→        } catch (err: unknown) {
   131→          // eslint-disable-next-line no-console
   132→          getLogger().warn(`[build] Failed to render ${manifest.session_id}: ${String(err)}`)
   133→        }
   134→      })(),
   135→    )
   136→  }
   137→
   138→  // Wait for all transcript renders (parallel, bounded by semaphore in summarize.ts)
   139→  await Promise.all(summaryPromises)
   140→
   141→  // Compute final stats with accumulated tool counts
   142→  const stats = computeStats(manifests, toolCounts)
   143→
   144→  // Load markdown content for various pages
   145→  const goalFiles = loadMarkdownFiles(join(root, 'Memory', '01_goals'))
   146→  const todoFiles = loadMarkdownFiles(join(root, 'Memory', '02_todos'))
   147→  const knowledgeFiles = loadMarkdownFiles(join(root, 'Memory', '04_knowledge'))
   148→  const archiveFiles = loadMarkdownFiles(join(root, 'Memory', '05_archive'))
   149→

Tool Result #894 Read toolu_011ZBa37YseuzxYN6nYkF3fi 15:44:27
Read output=1→/** 2→ * Session list page: tabular index of all imported transcripts, with client tab filtering. 3→ */ 4→ 5→import type { ManifestEntry } from '../../types/transcript.js' 6→i...
Show output payload 
1→/**
     2→ * Session list page: tabular index of all imported transcripts, with client tab filtering.
     3→ */
     4→
     5→import type { ManifestEntry } from '../../types/transcript.js'
     6→import type { Translations } from '../i18n/types.js'
     7→import { clientBadge, escHtml, htmlShell, renderNav, transcriptUrlFromRoot } from './layout.js'
     8→
     9→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    10→// Transcript list
    11→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    12→
    13→export function renderTranscriptList(manifests: ManifestEntry[], t?: Translations, summaries?: Record<string, string>): string {
    14→  const nav = renderNav('transcripts', 1, t)
    15→
    16→  const sorted = [...manifests].sort((a, b) => b.started_at.localeCompare(a.started_at))
    17→
    18→  const title = t?.sessions.title ?? 'Sessions'
    19→  const clientLabel = t?.sessions.client ?? 'Client'
    20→  const dateLabel = t?.sessions.date ?? 'Date'
    21→  const idLabel = t?.sessions.id ?? 'ID'
    22→  const msgsLabel = t?.sessions.msgs ?? 'Msgs'
    23→  const toolsLabel = t?.sessions.tools ?? 'Tools'
    24→  const allLabel = t?.sessions.all ?? 'All'
    25→
    26→  if (sorted.length === 0) {
    27→    const content = `<div class="page-header">
    28→  <h1>${escHtml(title)}</h1>
    29→  <p class="subtitle">${escHtml(t?.sessions.noSessions ?? 'No sessions imported yet.')}</p>
    30→</div>`
    31→    return htmlShell(title, content, nav)
    32→  }
    33→
    34→  // Collect unique clients
    35→  const clientSet = new Set<string>()
    36→  for (const m of sorted) clientSet.add(m.client)
    37→  const clients = [...clientSet].sort()
    38→
    39→  // Tab bar
    40→  const tabBar = renderTabBar(allLabel, clients, sorted)
    41→
    42→  const rows = sorted
    43→    .map(m => {
    44→      const url = transcriptHref(m)
    45→      const date = m.started_at.slice(0, 10)
    46→      const time = m.started_at.slice(11, 16)
    47→      const badge = clientBadge(m.client)
    48→      const summary = summaries?.[m.session_id] ?? ''
    49→      const meta = `${escHtml(m.client)} · ${escHtml(date)} · ${m.message_count} msgs`
    50→      return `<tr data-client="${escHtml(m.client)}">
    51→  <td>${badge}</td>
    52→  <td><a href="${escHtml(url)}" data-summary="${escHtml(summary)}" data-meta="${escHtml(meta)}">${escHtml(m.title || m.session_id)}</a></td>
    53→  <td style="color:var(--text-muted)">${escHtml(date)} ${escHtml(time)}</td>
    54→  <td style="color:var(--text-muted);font-family:var(--font-mono);font-size:0.8rem">${escHtml(m.session_id.slice(0, 8))}</td>
    55→  <td style="text-align:right;color:var(--text-muted)">${m.message_count}</td>
    56→  <td style="text-align:right;color:var(--text-muted)">${m.tool_event_count}</td>
    57→</tr>`
    58→    })
    59→    .join('')
    60→
    61→  const filterJs = `<script>
    62→(function() {
    63→  document.addEventListener('DOMContentLoaded', function() {
    64→    var tabs = document.querySelectorAll('.tab-btn');
    65→    var rows = document.querySelectorAll('tr[data-client]');
    66→    tabs.forEach(function(btn) {
    67→      btn.addEventListener('click', function() {
    68→        tabs.forEach(function(b) { b.classList.remove('active'); });
    69→        btn.classList.add('active');
    70→        var filter = btn.getAttribute('data-filter');
    71→        rows.forEach(function(row) {
    72→          if (!filter || row.getAttribute('data-client') === filter) {
    73→            row.style.display = '';
    74→          } else {
    75→            row.style.display = 'none';
    76→          }
    77→        });
    78→      });
    79→    });
    80→  });
    81→})();
    82→</script>`
    83→
    84→  const content = `<div class="page-header">
    85→  <h1>${escHtml(title)}</h1>
    86→  <p class="subtitle">${sorted.length} session(s) imported</p>
    87→</div>
    88→${tabBar}
    89→<div class="card" style="padding:0;overflow:hidden">
    90→<table>
    91→<thead><tr>
    92→  <th>${escHtml(clientLabel)}</th>
    93→  <th>Title</th>
    94→  <th>${escHtml(dateLabel)}</th>
    95→  <th>${escHtml(idLabel)}</th>
    96→  <th style="text-align:right">${escHtml(msgsLabel)}</th>
    97→  <th style="text-align:right">${escHtml(toolsLabel)}</th>
    98→</tr></thead>
    99→<tbody>${rows}</tbody>
   100→</table>
   101→</div>`
   102→
   103→  return htmlShell(title, content, nav, filterJs)
   104→}
   105→
   106→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   107→// Tab bar
   108→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   109→
   110→function renderTabBar(allLabel: string, clients: string[], manifests: ManifestEntry[]): string {
   111→  const total = manifests.length
   112→  const tabs = [
   113→    `<button class="tab-btn active" data-filter="" type="button">${escHtml(allLabel)} (${total})</button>`,
   114→    ...clients.map(client => {
   115→      const count = manifests.filter(m => m.client === client).length
   116→      return `<button class="tab-btn" data-filter="${escHtml(client)}" type="button">${escHtml(client)} (${count})</button>`
   117→    }),
   118→  ]
   119→  return `<div class="tab-bar">${tabs.join('')}</div>`
   120→}
   121→
   122→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   123→// Helper
   124→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   125→
   126→function transcriptHref(m: ManifestEntry): string {
   127→  // From transcripts/index.html, the URL is relative to the transcripts/ dir
   128→  // so we only need {client}/{stem}.html (no leading "transcripts/")
   129→  return transcriptUrlFromRoot(m).replace(/^transcripts\//, '')
   130→}
   131→

  "new_string": "      const summary = summaries?.[m.session_id] ?? ''\n      const sessionTags = tags?.[m.session_id] ?? []\n      const tagBadges = renderTagBadges(sessionTags)\n      const meta = `${escHtml(m.client)} · ${escHtml(date)} · ${m.message_count} msgs`\n      return `<tr data-client=\"${escHtml(m.client)}\">\n  <td>${badge}</td>\n  <td><a href=\"${escHtml(url)}\" data-summary=\"${escHtml(summary)}\" data-meta=\"${escHtml(meta)}\">${escHtml(m.title || m.session_id)}</a>${tagBadges}</td>"

{
  "file_path": "C:/Users/ai/.codex/skills/memorytree-workflow/tests/report/tags.test.ts",
  "content": "import { describe, it, expect, vi, beforeEach, afterEach } from 'vitest'\nimport os from 'node:os'\nimport { join } from 'node:path'\nimport { mkdirSync, writeFileSync } from 'node:fs'\n\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n// Helpers\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\nfunction makeTmpDir(): string {\n  const dir = join(os.tmpdir(), `mt-tags-test-${Math.random().toString(36).slice(2)}`)\n  mkdirSync(dir, { recursive: true })\n  return dir\n}\n\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n// renderTagBadges tests (synchronous, no AI)\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\nimport { renderTagBadges } from '../../src/report/tags.js'\n\ndescribe('renderTagBadges', () => {\n  it('returns empty string for empty tags', () => {\n    expect(renderTagBadges([])).toBe('')\n  })\n\n  it('renders tag pills', () => {\n    const html = renderTagBadges(['typescript', 'testing'])\n    expect(html).toContain('class=\"tag\"')\n    expect(html).toContain('typescript')\n    expect(html).toContain('testing')\n  })\n\n  it('escapes HTML special chars in tags', () => {\n    const html = renderTagBadges(['a&b', '<test>'])\n    expect(html).toContain('a&amp;b')\n    expect(html).toContain('&lt;test&gt;')\n    expect(html).not.toContain('<test>')\n  })\n\n  it('sets data-tag attribute', () => {\n    const html = renderTagBadges(['typescript'])\n    expect(html).toContain('data-tag=\"typescript\"')\n  })\n})\n\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n// getTags tests\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\nimport { getTags } from '../../src/report/tags.js'\n\ndescribe('getTags', () => {\n  it('returns [] when noAi=true', async () => {\n    const cacheDir = makeTmpDir()\n    const result = await getTags('abc123', 'some summary', { cacheDir, noAi: true, model: 'test' })\n    expect(result).toEqual([])\n  })\n\n  it('returns [] when summary is empty', async () => {\n    const cacheDir = makeTmpDir()\n    const result = await getTags('abc123', '', { cacheDir, noAi: false, model: 'test' })\n    expect(result).toEqual([])\n  })\n\n  it('returns [] when summary is whitespace only', async () => {\n    const cacheDir = makeTmpDir()\n    const result = await getTags('abc123', '   \\n  ', { cacheDir, noAi: false, model: 'test' })\n    expect(result).toEqual([])\n  })\n\n  it('returns [] when no API key set', async () => {\n    const cacheDir = makeTmpDir()\n    const saved = process.env['ANTHROPIC_API_KEY']\n    delete process.env['ANTHROPIC_API_KEY']\n    try {\n      const result = await getTags('abc123', 'a valid summary', { cacheDir, noAi: false, model: 'test' })\n      expect(result).toEqual([])\n    } finally {\n      if (saved !== undefined) process.env['ANTHROPIC_API_KEY'] = saved\n    }\n  })\n\n  it('returns cached tags without calling API', async () => {\n    const cacheDir = makeTmpDir()\n    const sha256 = 'cached-sha256'\n    // Write cache file\n    const tagsDir = join(cacheDir, 'tags')\n    mkdirSync(tagsDir, { recursive: true })\n    writeFileSync(\n      join(tagsDir, `${sha256}.json`),\n      JSON.stringify({ sha256, tags: ['cached', 'tag'], generated_at: '2026-01-01T00:00:00Z' }),\n    )\n    // Even with a fake API key, cached result should be returned immediately\n    const saved = process.env['ANTHROPIC_API_KEY']\n    process.env['ANTHROPIC_API_KEY'] = 'fake-key'\n    try {\n      const result = await getTags(sha256, 'some summary', { cacheDir, noAi: false, model: 'test' })\n      expect(result).toEqual(['cached', 'tag'])\n    } finally {\n      if (saved !== undefined) process.env['ANTHROPIC_API_KEY'] = saved\n      else delete process.env['ANTHROPIC_API_KEY']\n    }\n  })\n})\n"
}

assistant #913 15:45:45

All 606 tests pass. Here's what was completed in this session:

Phase 2 remaining steps finished:

- Search filtering — project field added to SearchIndexEntry, client/project <select> dropdowns with combined AND filter in search.ts
- Tag system — src/report/tags.ts created: SHA256-keyed cache (.report-cache/tags/), noAi fallback, AI prompt extracts 3–5 keyword tags; renderTagBadges() renders pill badges; integrated into build.ts (parallel after summaries) and transcript-list.ts
- Mermaid CDN — hasMermaidBlocks() + MERMAID_CDN_SCRIPT conditionally injected into knowledge/goals/todos/archive pages (only when markdown contains `mermaid blocks)
- CLI flags — --locale <locale> and --report-base-url <url> added to memorytree report build in both main.ts and cmd-report.ts
- CSS — .tag/.tag-list pill badge styles and .search-filter-select dropdown styles added to css.ts
- 9 new tag tests added covering noAi, empty summary, no API key, cache hit

Show output payload 
[
  {
    "text": "Now I have a thorough picture of all Phase 2 changes. Let me compile the full structured review.\n\n---\n\n## Phase 2 Code Review — MemoryTree Report Module\n\nSkill path: `C:/Users/ai/.codex/skills/memorytree-workflow/`\n\n---\n\n### CRITICAL Issues\n\n**None found.** No raw unsanitised user-content injected into HTML, no exposed secrets, no prototype-pollution vectors, no path-traversal in the serve command.\n\n---\n\n### HIGH Issues\n\n#### H1 — `markdown.ts`: Inline code backticks bypass `escHtml` after bold/italic substitution\n\nFile: `src/report/render/markdown.ts`, lines 211-216\n\nThe `applyInline` pipeline works like this: first `escHtml(part)` is called on the non-link text, then bold/italic/code regexes run on the already-escaped string. This means `**` and `*` and `` ` `` delimiters in user content that appear inside entity-encoded text are still substituted. The substitutions themselves are safe (`<strong>`, `<em>`, `<code>`) but the content captured by `(.+?)` in each regex is the entity-encoded string — safe. However, there is one subtle ordering problem: the backtick replacement captures `([^`]+)` and emits `<code>$1</code>` where `$1` is already-escaped content. That part is fine.\n\nThe real risk is that `applyInline` is called on raw heading text (line 100: `applyInline(text)` where `text` comes from `headingMatch[2]`), and `text` is _not_ pre-escaped before entering `applyInline`. Inside `applyInline`, links are extracted first (before escaping). A heading like `## [evil](javascript:alert(1))` would produce `<a href=\"javascript:alert(1)\" ...>`. The link regex (`\\[([^\\]]+)\\]\\(([^)]+)\\)`) does not validate the href scheme — only `escHtml` is called on it, not a scheme allowlist.\n\n**Impact:** A user whose Memory files contain markdown headings (goals/knowledge/archive/todos) with `javascript:` hrefs would get an XSS vector rendered into the static site. This is a local-output-only report, so exploitability is limited, but still a correctness issue worth flagging.\n\n**Recommendation:** Add a scheme check in `applyInline` before emitting the `<a>` tag: only allow `http:`, `https:`, `mailto:`, and relative URLs.\n\n---\n\n#### H2 — `markdown.ts` / `toc.ts`: `injectHeadingIds` regex does not match headings containing inline HTML\n\nFile: `src/report/render/toc.ts`, line 93\n\nThe regex `/<h([23])>([^<]*)<\\/h[23]>/g` only matches headings whose inner content contains no `<` characters. But `markdownToHtml` (called before `injectHeadingIds`) may output headings like `<h2><strong>Bold</strong> Title</h2>` if bold markup is in the heading. The `[^<]*` pattern fails to match, so no `id` attribute is injected and the TOC anchor links produce dead `#section-one` hrefs.\n\n**Impact:** TOC links silently fail to scroll for any heading that contains bold, italic, code, or link inline formatting.\n\n**Recommendation:** Change the inner capture group to `([\\s\\S]*?)` (non-greedy) or `([^]*?)`, and update the matching logic to strip inline tags from the inner content before comparing with `escHtml(e.text)`.\n\n---\n\n#### H3 — `tags.ts`: No validation of individual tag string length or content\n\nFile: `src/report/tags.ts`, lines 83-87\n\nTags are filtered `typeof t === 'string' && t.length > 0` but there is no upper bound on individual tag length. A model could return a 500-character \"tag\" string. After `toLowerCase()` and `trim()`, this string lands in `data-tag=\"${escTag(t)}\"` and as visible text in the DOM. HTML-escaping is present (`escTag`), so no XSS. But a very long tag (e.g. 500 chars) would break the layout and inflate the search index unnecessarily.\n\n**Recommendation:** Add `.filter(t => t.length <= 50)` or similar after the trim step.\n\n---\n\n### MEDIUM Issues\n\n#### M1 — `toc.ts`: Duplicate ID algorithm is off-by-one for three or more identical headings\n\nFile: `src/report/render/toc.ts`, lines 41-43\n\n```\nconst count = seen.get(base) ?? 0\nconst id = count === 0 ? base : `${base}-${count}`\nseen.set(base, count + 1)\n```\n\nFor three identical headings `## Intro`, the IDs generated are:\n- 1st: count=0 → id=`intro`, stored count=1\n- 2nd: count=1 → id=`intro-1`, stored count=2\n- 3rd: count=2 → id=`intro-2`, stored count=3\n\nThis is correct for uniqueness. The tests cover this case and pass (lines 24-26 of `toc.test.ts`). However, the suffix counter diverges from GitHub-Flavored Markdown's convention which starts at `-1` for the first duplicate. The docs comment says `-1, -2, …` so the intent is intentional, but there is a discrepancy: the first _original_ heading gets no suffix, the first _duplicate_ gets `-1`, the second duplicate gets `-2`. This is consistent and correct per the documented intent. No bug, but worth noting the divergence from GFM (which would put `-1` on the second occurrence, `-2` on the third) — that's exactly what this code does. Actually the code is correct; this is a non-issue on closer inspection.\n\n#### M2 — `rss.ts`: `toRfc2822` silently falls back to `new Date()` on invalid ISO\n\nFile: `src/report/render/rss.ts`, lines 83-88\n\n```ts\nfunction toRfc2822(iso: string): string {\n  try {\n    return new Date(iso).toUTCString()\n  } catch {\n    return new Date().toUTCString()\n  }\n}\n```\n\n`new Date('invalid')` does not throw — it returns an Invalid Date object. `new Date('invalid').toUTCString()` returns the string `\"Invalid Date\"` in V8, not a valid RFC 2822 date string. This will produce a malformed `<pubDate>` element that RSS readers may reject for the entire feed if they are strict.\n\n**Recommendation:** Check `isNaN(d.getTime())` after construction and fall back to `new Date().toUTCString()` in that branch.\n\n---\n\n#### M3 — `search.ts`: `project` field in `buildSearchIndex` uses `lastPathSegment(m.cwd)` when `m.project` is empty, but `m.cwd` can be empty string\n\nFile: `src/report/render/search.ts`, lines 27-28\n\n```ts\nconst project = m.project || lastPathSegment(m.cwd) || 'unknown'\n```\n\n`lastPathSegment('')` returns `''` (the `filter(Boolean).at(-1) ?? ''` chain), so this correctly falls back to `'unknown'`. No crash. However, if `m.cwd` is `'.'` or `'/'`, `lastPathSegment` returns `'.'` or `''` respectively. The string `'.'` then appears as a project filter dropdown option, which is confusing UX.\n\n---\n\n#### M4 — `layout.ts`: `htmlShell` dual-signature design creates `lang` parameter precedence ambiguity\n\nFile: `src/report/render/layout.ts`, lines 289-300\n\n```ts\nexport function htmlShell(\n  title: string,\n  content: string,\n  nav: string,\n  extraHeadOrOpts: string | HtmlShellOptions = '',\n  lang = 'en',\n): string {\n  const opts: HtmlShellOptions = typeof extraHeadOrOpts === 'string'\n    ? { extraHead: extraHeadOrOpts, lang }   // ← uses the positional `lang` param\n    : extraHeadOrOpts                         // ← uses opts.lang if present\n  const resolvedLang = opts.lang ?? lang\n```\n\nWhen called as `htmlShell(title, content, nav, { extraHead: '...' })` (without `lang`), `opts.lang` is `undefined`, and `resolvedLang` falls back to the positional `lang = 'en'` default. That is correct. But when called with the legacy string form `htmlShell(title, content, nav, '<script>...</script>', 'zh-CN')`, the object created is `{ extraHead: '...', lang: 'zh-CN' }`, so `resolvedLang = 'zh-CN'`. Also correct. The design works, but it is fragile: a call site could accidentally pass both a `HtmlShellOptions.lang` and a fifth positional argument and the positional one would be ignored. This is a maintainability concern.\n\n---\n\n#### M5 — `tags.ts`: Concurrent writes to the same cache file are not atomic\n\nFile: `src/report/tags.ts`, lines 136-145\n\n`writeFileSync` is called from multiple concurrent `Promise.all` invocations in `build.ts` (line 144-149). Two concurrent sessions with the same `sha256` (which would be unusual but possible if manifests share a raw file) could race on `writeTagCache`. On most OS/Node.js combinations `writeFileSync` is not atomic — a partial write would leave a truncated JSON file. On the next read, the corrupt file would be caught by the `try/catch` in `readTagCache` and return `null`, causing a redundant API call, which is acceptable. But the corrupt file remains on disk.\n\n**Impact:** Low in practice (sha256 collisions between manifest entries are uncommon), but the pattern is fragile. This is the same pattern used by `summarize.ts`'s cache, which was accepted in Phase 1.\n\n---\n\n#### M6 — `build.test.ts`: Integration test passes `pdf: false` which is not in `BuildReportOptions` type\n\nFile: `tests/report/build.test.ts`, lines 175-180\n\n```ts\nawait buildReport({\n  root: tmpDir,\n  output,\n  noAi: true,\n  pdf: false,      // ← not a field in BuildReportOptions\n})\n```\n\n`BuildReportOptions` (in `src/types/report.ts`) has no `pdf` field. TypeScript strict mode should flag this as an error unless the type is cast or `pdf` is a known extra key. This indicates either the test predates a type change, or `tsconfig` allows excess properties in some build step. This should cause a type-check failure under `exactOptionalPropertyTypes`.\n\n**Recommendation:** Remove `pdf: false` from the test call.\n\n---\n\n### LOW Issues\n\n#### L1 — `markdown.ts`: YAML front matter detection only fires on the very first line (`i === 0`)\n\nFile: `src/report/render/markdown.ts`, lines 87-92\n\n```ts\nif (i === 0 && line === '---') {\n```\n\nThis skips front matter correctly only if `---` is the literal first line. If the markdown file has a BOM or any leading whitespace, front matter is not stripped and `---` appears as a horizontal rule. The `parseCleanMarkdownMessages` in `build.ts` processes clean markdown, which should be normalised. Low risk in practice.\n\n---\n\n#### L2 — `layout.ts` inline JS: `document.startViewTransition` intercepts all relative `<a>` clicks, including anchor-only links\n\nFile: `src/report/render/layout.ts`, lines 100-109\n\n```js\nif (!href || href.startsWith('#') || href.startsWith('http')) return;\n```\n\nThe guard correctly exempts `#` fragments and `http` URLs. However it does not exempt `https:` URLs (only `http:`). An absolute `https://` link in rendered content would be intercepted and navigated via `window.location.href = href`. The View Transitions API then fires a cross-origin navigation, which is a no-op for the transition but still sets `window.location`. This is harmless but unexpected.\n\n**Recommendation:** Change the check to `href.startsWith('http')` already covers both schemes (`http://` and `https://` both start with `http`). On re-reading, `'https://'.startsWith('http')` is `true`. So this is actually fine — no issue.\n\n---\n\n#### L3 — `cmd-report.ts` serve command: `statSync` called after `existsSync`, TOCTOU race\n\nFile: `src/cli/cmd-report.ts`, lines 85-87\n\n```ts\nif (existsSync(filePath) && statSync(filePath).isDirectory()) {\n```\n\nClassic TOCTOU: the file could be deleted between `existsSync` and `statSync`. The `statSync` would throw. The `stream.on('error', ...)` handler below would not catch a sync `statSync` throw. However the server is a local dev tool serving a static generated directory, so exploitability is nil. Nonetheless it would cause an unhandled exception and crash the server process.\n\n**Recommendation:** Wrap in a try/catch or combine into a single `statSync` with try/catch.\n\n---\n\n#### L4 — `layout.ts`: Reader Mode toggle always renders (even on pages where it adds no value)\n\nFile: `src/report/render/layout.ts`, line 303\n\n```ts\nconst readerBtn = opts.readerMode !== false\n  ? `<button ...>📖</button>`\n  : ''\n```\n\nThe default is to render the button unless `readerMode` is explicitly set to `false`. The dashboard and search pages emit the button even though they have no prose content to read. This is cosmetic but slightly misleading UX.\n\n---\n\n#### L5 — `rss.ts`: Missing `<atom:self>` link — RSS best practice violation\n\nFile: `src/report/render/rss.ts`\n\nThe feed declares `xmlns:atom` namespace but never emits an `<atom:link rel=\"self\">` element, which is required by the Atom extension for feed validators and feed autodiscovery. This will cause Atom-aware validators to warn. Not a functional bug.\n\n---\n\n#### L6 — `tags.ts`: `renderTagBadges` is exported from `tags.ts` but also re-exported via `build.ts` import\n\nFile: `src/report/build.ts`, line 36\n\n```ts\nimport { getTags, renderTagBadges } from './tags.js'\n```\n\n`renderTagBadges` is imported in `build.ts` but never used there — `transcript-list.ts` imports it directly from `tags.ts`. This is a dead import in `build.ts`.\n\n---\n\n#### L7 — `search.ts`: `project` field is always set in `SearchIndexEntry` but can be empty string `''` after `lastPathSegment`\n\nAs discussed in M3, when `m.project` is empty and `m.cwd` is `'/'`, `lastPathSegment` returns `''`. Then the search index entry has `project: ''` (not `'unknown'` because `'' || 'unknown'` short-circuits to `'unknown'` — actually this does work correctly). Rechecked: `'' || 'unknown'` = `'unknown'`, so this path is safe. Withdraw — not actually an issue.\n\n---\n\n### Test Coverage Gaps\n\n| Area | Coverage Status |\n|---|---|\n| `markdown.ts` callout rendering | **Missing** — no test for `> [!note]`, `> [!warning]`, etc. |\n| `markdown.ts` mermaid block detection (`hasMermaidBlocks`) | **Missing** — not tested at all |\n| `markdown.ts` `applyInline` link with `javascript:` href | **Missing** — no XSS regression test |\n| `search.ts` filter dropdowns (client + project) | **Missing** — `renderSearchPage` tests do not assert dropdown HTML |\n| `build.ts` `feed.xml` is created | **Missing** — integration test checks HTML files but not `feed.xml` |\n| `build.ts` `reportBaseUrl` passed through to RSS | **Missing** |\n| `config.ts` `report_base_url` field | **Missing** — not tested in `config.test.ts` |\n| `tags.ts` malformed cache JSON (corrupted file) | Partially covered — `readTagCache` has try/catch but no explicit test |\n| `toc.ts` heading with inline HTML (`**bold**` in heading text) | **Missing** — `injectHeadingIds` not tested with inline-formatted headings |\n\n---\n\n### Summary Table\n\n| ID | Severity | File | Issue |\n|---|---|---|---|\n| H1 | HIGH | `markdown.ts` | `applyInline` emits `javascript:` hrefs without scheme validation |\n| H2 | HIGH | `toc.ts` | `injectHeadingIds` regex `[^<]*` fails for headings with inline HTML markup |\n| H3 | HIGH | `tags.ts` | No upper bound on individual tag string length |\n| M2 | MEDIUM | `rss.ts` | `toRfc2822` does not check `isNaN` on `Invalid Date` — produces malformed `<pubDate>` |\n| M3 | MEDIUM | `search.ts` | `m.cwd = '.'` produces `'.'` as a project filter option |\n| M4 | MEDIUM | `layout.ts` | Dual-signature `htmlShell` is fragile for future callers |\n| M5 | MEDIUM | `tags.ts` | Concurrent cache writes are not atomic |\n| M6 | MEDIUM | `build.test.ts` | Test passes `pdf: false` which is not in `BuildReportOptions` — strict type error |\n| L3 | LOW | `cmd-report.ts` | `existsSync` + `statSync` TOCTOU race in serve handler |\n| L4 | LOW | `layout.ts` | Reader Mode button rendered on non-prose pages |\n| L5 | LOW | `rss.ts` | Missing `<atom:link rel=\"self\">` — RSS validator warning |\n| L6 | LOW | `build.ts` | `renderTagBadges` imported but never used |\n| L1 | LOW | `markdown.ts` | Front matter skip only fires on line 0 — BOM/whitespace breaks it |\n\n### No Issues Found In\n\n- `breadcrumb.ts` — HTML escaping, aria attributes, and edge cases are all correct\n- `css.ts` — Pure CSS, no logic, complete and well-organized\n- `heartbeat.ts` — `reportBaseUrl` correctly passed from config\n- `config.ts` — `report_base_url` correctly added to `Config`, parsed, defaulted, and saved\n- `main.ts` — `--locale` and `--report-base-url` flags wired correctly\n- `rss.ts` `xmlEsc` — Covers all five XML special characters including `'`, which `escHtml` misses",
    "type": "text"
