{
  "description": "Second code review pass",
  "subagent_type": "general-purpose",
  "prompt": "Do a second independent code review pass of Phase 2 changes in the MemoryTree report module. The skill is at `C:/Users/ai/.codex/skills/memorytree-workflow/`.\n\nThis is a SECOND review pass — focus on things a first reviewer might miss: architecture coherence, performance, inter-module consistency, API surface design.\n\nKey files to review:\n- `src/report/tags.ts`\n- `src/report/render/toc.ts`\n- `src/report/render/breadcrumb.ts`\n- `src/report/render/rss.ts`\n- `src/report/render/search.ts`\n- `src/report/render/markdown.ts`\n- `src/report/render/layout.ts`\n- `src/report/render/knowledge.ts`\n- `src/report/render/goals.ts`\n- `src/report/render/todos.ts`\n- `src/report/render/archive.ts`\n- `src/report/render/transcript-list.ts`\n- `src/report/build.ts`\n- `src/cli/cmd-report.ts`\n- `src/cli/main.ts`\n- `src/heartbeat/config.ts`\n- `tests/report/tags.test.ts`\n- `tests/report/render/rss.test.ts`\n- `tests/report/render/toc.test.ts`\n- `tests/report/render/breadcrumb.test.ts`\n\nSpecific things to look for in this second pass:\n\n1. **Unused imports**: Any imports declared but not used in the modified files (TypeScript noUnusedLocals might not catch them in tsup builds)\n2. **`exactOptionalPropertyTypes` compliance**: Optional fields must use `field?: T` not `field: T | undefined`, and when passing optional props to functions the `?? default` pattern must be used correctly\n3. **`noUncheckedIndexedAccess` compliance**: Array/record indexing without null checks\n4. **Missing `.js` extensions**: All relative imports must end in `.js` for NodeNext ESM\n5. **Inter-module consistency**: Do the renderers (knowledge, goals, todos, archive) all consistently use the same Mermaid injection pattern? Are TOC and Breadcrumb integration consistent across pages?\n6. **Dead code in build.ts**: Is `renderTagBadges` actually imported but unused? What about other possibly unused imports?\n7. **Config field test coverage**: Does `tests/heartbeat/config.test.ts` test `report_base_url`? Read that test file.\n8. **`build.test.ts` type error**: Does the integration test use `pdf: false` field that doesn't exist on `BuildReportOptions`? Read `tests/report/build.test.ts`.\n9. **`rss.ts` Invalid Date handling**: Read `toRfc2822` and confirm if `new Date('invalid')` throws or returns Invalid Date in Node.js\n10. **`toc.ts` injectHeadingIds regex**: Read the exact regex and confirm if headings with inline HTML (bold/italic from markdownToHtml) would match\n\nFor each issue found, provide: severity (CRITICAL/HIGH/MEDIUM/LOW), file:line, description, and recommended fix.",
  "run_in_background": false
}
Tool Result #918 Agent toolu_01DuV6xTTRSwDLWBt33FSwZ9 16:01:21
Agent output=[{"text":"Now I have a complete picture of all the files. Let me compile the full second-pass review.\n\n---\n\n## Second-Pass Code Review — Phase 2 Changes (MemoryTree Report M...
Show output payload 
[
  {
    "text": "Now I have a complete picture of all the files. Let me compile the full second-pass review.\n\n---\n\n## Second-Pass Code Review — Phase 2 Changes (MemoryTree Report Module)\n\n### Issue 1 — CRITICAL: `build.ts` imports `renderTagBadges` but never uses it\n\n**File:** `src/report/build.ts` line 36\n\n```ts\nimport { getTags, renderTagBadges } from './tags.js'\n```\n\n`renderTagBadges` is never called anywhere in `build.ts`. The actual rendering is done inside `src/report/render/transcript-list.ts` (which has its own import of `renderTagBadges` from `../tags.js`). Under `tsup` / `noUnusedLocals` the TypeScript compiler may not catch this because tsup bundles without strict checking by default.\n\n**Impact:** Dead import; also suggests a design concern — `build.ts` imported `renderTagBadges` thinking it would orchestrate tag-badge rendering, but the renderer is handling it directly. If `renderTagBadges` is later removed from `tags.ts`, `build.ts` will fail to compile.\n\n**Fix:** Remove `renderTagBadges` from the import on line 36: `import { getTags } from './tags.js'`\n\n---\n\n### Issue 2 — CRITICAL: `build.test.ts` passes `pdf: false` which does not exist on `BuildReportOptions`\n\n**File:** `tests/report/build.test.ts` lines 179, 193, 210, 223\n\n```ts\nawait buildReport({\n  root: tmpDir,\n  output,\n  noAi: true,\n  pdf: false,       // ← not in BuildReportOptions\n})\n```\n\n`BuildReportOptions` (in `src/types/report.ts`) has no `pdf` field. TypeScript's `exactOptionalPropertyTypes` doesn't cause this to fail, but under strict mode with `noExcessPropertyChecks` active (which TypeScript always applies for object literals) this **should** be a compile-time error. If the test currently passes, it's because tsup or vitest is running without strict type-checking, silently ignoring the extra key. This is a latent type error that will surface if stricter compilation is introduced.\n\n**Fix:** Remove `pdf: false` from all four `buildReport()` calls in `build.test.ts`.\n\n---\n\n### Issue 3 — HIGH: `rss.ts` — `new Date('invalid')` does not throw; the `try/catch` in `toRfc2822` is ineffective\n\n**File:** `src/report/render/rss.ts` lines 82–88\n\n```ts\nfunction toRfc2822(iso: string): string {\n  try {\n    return new Date(iso).toUTCString()\n  } catch {\n    return new Date().toUTCString()\n  }\n}\n```\n\nIn Node.js, `new Date('invalid-string')` does **not** throw an exception — it returns an `Invalid Date` object. Calling `.toUTCString()` on an `Invalid Date` returns the string `\"Invalid Date\"`, which is not valid RFC 2822. The `catch` block is therefore unreachable dead code and provides no protection.\n\nThis means any manifest with a malformed `started_at` will silently produce `<pubDate>Invalid Date</pubDate>` in the RSS feed, which RSS validators and feed readers will reject at the item level.\n\n**Fix:** Check `isNaN(date.getTime())` before calling `.toUTCString()`:\n\n```ts\nfunction toRfc2822(iso: string): string {\n  const d = new Date(iso)\n  return isNaN(d.getTime()) ? new Date().toUTCString() : d.toUTCString()\n}\n```\n\nThe `rss.test.ts` suite has no test for an invalid/malformed date string, leaving this path completely untested.\n\n---\n\n### Issue 4 — HIGH: `toc.ts` — `injectHeadingIds` regex `[^<]*` fails to match headings with inline HTML (bold/italic)\n\n**File:** `src/report/render/toc.ts` line 93\n\n```ts\nreturn html.replace(/<h([23])>([^<]*)<\\/h[23]>/g, (full, levelStr, innerHtml) => {\n```\n\nThe regex `([^<]*)` captures only text content with no `<` characters. However, `markdownToHtml` applies inline formatting to heading text via `applyInline()`, which turns `**bold**` into `<strong>bold</strong>` and `` `code` `` into `<code>code</code>`. Any heading containing bold, italic, or inline code will produce HTML like:\n\n```html\n<h2><strong>Overview</strong></h2>\n```\n\nThis heading will **not** be matched by the regex (the `<` in `<strong>` breaks `[^<]*`), so no `id` attribute gets injected, and the TOC anchor link becomes a dead link.\n\nThe `toc.test.ts` tests only use plain-text headings (`'Section One'`, `'Sub Section'`), so this path is not tested. The `injectHeadingIds` test case \"leaves unmatched headings unchanged\" (line 95–100) does not test the inline-HTML case.\n\n**Fix:** Widen the capture group from `[^<]*` to `[^]*?` (or `[\\s\\S]*?`) to allow any inner HTML:\n\n```ts\nreturn html.replace(/<h([23])>([\\s\\S]*?)<\\/h[23]>/g, ...)\n```\n\nThe matching logic comparing `escHtml(e.text)` to `innerHtml.trim()` also needs to account for stripped HTML tags when comparing. This is a deeper design issue: the plain-text extracted by `extractToc` (which reads raw markdown) will not match the HTML-rendered text. A robust fix would be to strip HTML tags from `innerHtml` before comparison.\n\n---\n\n### Issue 5 — HIGH: Inter-module inconsistency — `todos.ts` and `archive.ts` skip TOC injection that `knowledge.ts` and `goals.ts` include\n\n**Files:** `src/report/render/todos.ts`, `src/report/render/archive.ts` vs `src/report/render/knowledge.ts`, `src/report/render/goals.ts`\n\n`knowledge.ts` and `goals.ts` both:\n1. Import `extractToc`, `renderToc`, `injectHeadingIds` from `./toc.js`\n2. Generate per-file TOC navigation\n3. Inject heading IDs into the rendered HTML\n\n`todos.ts` and `archive.ts` do **neither**:\n- They do not import from `./toc.js` at all\n- They call `markdownToHtml(f.content)` directly with no `injectHeadingIds`\n- No sticky TOC is rendered\n\nThis is architecturally inconsistent — users can navigate headings in Knowledge and Goals files but not in Archive and Todos files, despite the pages having identical card-based layouts. If Archive or Todo files contain long markdown with many headings, the missing TOC makes them harder to navigate.\n\n**Fix:** Either add TOC injection to `todos.ts` and `archive.ts` (matching the `knowledge.ts` pattern), or explicitly document the decision to omit TOC from those pages.\n\n---\n\n### Issue 6 — MEDIUM: `config.test.ts` has no test for `report_base_url` field\n\n**File:** `tests/heartbeat/config.test.ts`\n\nThe `Config` interface in `src/heartbeat/config.ts` was extended with `report_base_url: string` (line 52), a default constant `DEFAULT_REPORT_BASE_URL = ''` (line 27), and parsing logic (lines 251–254). However, `config.test.ts` has comprehensive test sections for `generate_report`/`ai_summary_model` (lines 274–325) and for `locale`/`gh_pages_branch`/`cname`/`webhook_url` (lines 331–418), but **no corresponding test section for `report_base_url`**.\n\nThe `saveConfig` roundtrip test (line 388–409) does not include `report_base_url` in the `original` object literal, meaning it also doesn't verify that value round-trips through serialization.\n\n**Fix:** Add a test section for `report_base_url` that covers: default value (`''`), parsing from TOML, graceful handling of a non-string value, and the round-trip via `saveConfig`/`loadConfig`.\n\n---\n\n### Issue 7 — MEDIUM: `dashboard.ts` has a dead private function `transcriptHref`\n\n**File:** `src/report/render/dashboard.ts` lines 147–149\n\n```ts\nfunction transcriptHref(m: ManifestEntry): string {\n  return transcriptUrlFromRoot(m)\n}\n```\n\nThis private function wraps `transcriptUrlFromRoot` but is never called. All dashboard rendering uses `transcriptUrlFromRoot(m)` directly (line 109). The function was likely a leftover from refactoring that moved the URL helper to `layout.ts`.\n\n**Fix:** Remove the dead `transcriptHref` function from `dashboard.ts`.\n\n---\n\n### Issue 8 — MEDIUM: `search.ts` — `buildSearchIndex` double-serializes `entry` to compute byte count\n\n**File:** `src/report/render/search.ts` lines 36–44\n\n```ts\nconst entryBytes = JSON.stringify(entry).length\nif (totalBytes + entryBytes > MAX_INDEX_BYTES) {\n  const remaining = MAX_INDEX_BYTES - totalBytes - (entryBytes - snippet.length) - 10\n  if (remaining < 20) break\n  snippet = snippet.slice(0, remaining)\n  entry.snippet = snippet   // ← mutates entry (violates immutability rule)\n}\ntotalBytes += JSON.stringify(entry).length  // ← second serialization of same entry\nentries.push(entry)\n```\n\nTwo problems: (1) The entry is serialized twice — `JSON.stringify(entry)` is called once to measure `entryBytes`, then again at line 44 after `snippet` may have been truncated. This is a small performance overhead but indicative of unclear logic. (2) `entry.snippet = snippet` mutates the `entry` object after construction, violating the immutability principle established in project coding guidelines.\n\n**Fix:** Compute the byte count once after any truncation, and construct the entry with the final snippet rather than mutating it post-hoc.\n\n---\n\n### Issue 9 — MEDIUM: `layout.ts` — `htmlShell` renders reader-mode button outside content area unconditionally when `readerMode` is not explicitly `false`\n\n**File:** `src/report/render/layout.ts` lines 303–331\n\n```ts\nconst readerBtn = opts.readerMode !== false\n  ? `<button class=\"reader-toggle-btn\" ...>`\n  : ''\n```\n\nThe condition `opts.readerMode !== false` is always `true` for any caller that passes a plain `extraHead` string (the legacy path, lines 297–299), since `extraHeadOrOpts` is a `string` and the constructed `opts` object has `readerMode: undefined`. So `undefined !== false` is `true`, meaning the reader-mode button is always rendered on every page.\n\nThis is probably intended, but the API contract (`readerMode?: boolean`) implying omission disables the button is misleading — omitting `readerMode` actually enables it. The `false`-to-disable semantics is inverted from conventional optional boolean patterns where omission means \"use default\".\n\n**Fix:** Rename the option to `hideReaderMode?: boolean` (opt-in to hiding) to make the default \"show it\" explicit, or document the current `!== false` behavior in the interface JSDoc.\n\n---\n\n### Issue 10 — MEDIUM: `breadcrumb.ts` — non-last item without a `url` gets no link and no `aria-current`, effectively becoming a dead span\n\n**File:** `src/report/render/breadcrumb.ts` lines 31–36\n\n```ts\nconst parts = items.map((item, i) => {\n  const isLast = i === items.length - 1\n  if (isLast || !item.url) {\n    return `<span class=\"breadcrumb-item\" ${isLast ? 'aria-current=\"page\"' : ''}>${escHtml(item.label)}</span>`\n  }\n  return `<a class=\"breadcrumb-item\" href=\"${escHtml(item.url)}\">${escHtml(item.label)}</a>`\n})\n```\n\nIf a non-last item has no `url` (e.g., an intermediate crumb that serves as a category label), it renders as a `<span>` without `aria-current`, which is correct. However, the space before the conditional `aria-current` attribute produces `<span class=\"breadcrumb-item\" >` (trailing space before `>`) when `isLast` is `false` and `!item.url` is `true`. While not a functional bug in most browsers, this is malformed HTML by strict standards.\n\n**Fix:** Change to:\n```ts\nconst ariaCurrent = isLast ? ' aria-current=\"page\"' : ''\nreturn `<span class=\"breadcrumb-item\"${ariaCurrent}>${escHtml(item.label)}</span>`\n```\n\n---\n\n### Issue 11 — LOW: `toc.ts` — duplicate heading deduplication uses 0-based first occurrence but -1-based suffix scheme\n\n**File:** `src/report/render/toc.ts` lines 41–43\n\n```ts\nconst count = seen.get(base) ?? 0\nconst id = count === 0 ? base : `${base}-${count}`\nseen.set(base, count + 1)\n```\n\nThe first occurrence gets no suffix (`intro`), the second gets `-1` (`intro-1`), the third gets `-2` (`intro-2`). The tests in `toc.test.ts` confirm this is the intended scheme. This is a valid design choice but differs from common slug libraries (GitHub's Markdown anchors use `-1`, `-2` starting from the *second* duplicate), which could confuse contributors. A comment explaining the scheme is absent.\n\n**Fix:** (Low priority) Add a comment: `// first occurrence: no suffix; duplicates: -1, -2, ...`\n\n---\n\n### Issue 12 — LOW: `tags.ts` — `escTag` duplicates `escHtml` from `layout.ts` with a subset of substitutions\n\n**File:** `src/report/tags.ts` lines 112–118\n\n```ts\nfunction escTag(s: string): string {\n  return s\n    .replace(/&/g, '&amp;')\n    .replace(/</g, '&lt;')\n    .replace(/>/g, '&gt;')\n    .replace(/\"/g, '&quot;')\n}\n```\n\n`layout.ts` already exports `escHtml` which performs the same substitutions plus `'` → `&#39;`. `tags.ts` reinvents this without the single-quote escape. Since tags are rendered in both element content and `data-tag` attribute values, the missing single-quote escape in attribute context is a minor inconsistency (single quotes in data attributes are generally safe if the attribute is double-quoted, but it is still a divergence from the shared utility).\n\n**Fix:** Import and use `escHtml` from `./render/layout.js` and remove the private `escTag` function.\n\n---\n\n### Issue 13 — LOW: `config.test.ts` — `saveConfig` roundtrip fixture objects are missing `report_base_url`, causing TypeScript to see them as non-conformant with `Config`\n\n**File:** `tests/heartbeat/config.test.ts` lines 133–144, 162–170, 221–228, 239–245, 256–263, 389–401\n\nAll fixtures using `as const` to construct partial `Config` objects are missing `report_base_url`. Because `Config` defines `report_base_url: string` (not optional), TypeScript should flag these as type errors under `strictNullChecks`. If these tests are currently passing, it is because `saveConfig` in `config.ts` gracefully handles missing keys via `?? DEFAULT_REPORT_BASE_URL` (lines 99–100), so the runtime behaviour is correct — but the type annotations of the test fixtures are incorrect (incomplete objects cast to `Config`).\n\n**Fix:** Add `report_base_url: ''` to each fixture in `config.test.ts`, or declare them as `Partial<Config>` (though `saveConfig` takes a full `Config`).\n\n---\n\n### Summary Table\n\n| # | Severity | File | Description |\n|---|----------|------|-------------|\n| 1 | CRITICAL | `src/report/build.ts:36` | Unused `renderTagBadges` import — dead import, masks intent confusion |\n| 2 | CRITICAL | `tests/report/build.test.ts:179,193,210,223` | `pdf: false` passed to `buildReport` but field does not exist on `BuildReportOptions` — latent type error |\n| 3 | HIGH | `src/report/render/rss.ts:82–88` | `try/catch` in `toRfc2822` is unreachable; invalid ISO dates silently emit `\"Invalid Date\"` as `<pubDate>` |\n| 4 | HIGH | `src/report/render/toc.ts:93` | `injectHeadingIds` regex `[^<]*` fails for headings with inline HTML (`**bold**`, `` `code` ``) |\n| 5 | HIGH | `src/report/render/todos.ts`, `archive.ts` | TOC injection missing — inconsistent with `knowledge.ts` and `goals.ts` which use identical card layout |\n| 6 | MEDIUM | `tests/heartbeat/config.test.ts` | No test coverage for `report_base_url` config field (parse, default, round-trip) |\n| 7 | MEDIUM | `src/report/render/dashboard.ts:147–149` | Dead private `transcriptHref` function — never called after refactor |\n| 8 | MEDIUM | `src/report/render/search.ts:36–44` | `entry` object mutated after construction (`entry.snippet = snippet`) + double `JSON.stringify` |\n| 9 | MEDIUM | `src/report/render/layout.ts:303` | `readerMode !== false` semantics are inverted — omitting the option enables the button, not disables it |\n| 10 | MEDIUM | `src/report/render/breadcrumb.ts:34` | Trailing space in `<span>` attribute: `<span class=\"breadcrumb-item\" >` for urlless non-last items |\n| 11 | LOW | `src/report/render/toc.ts:41–43` | Deduplication suffix scheme (0=no suffix, 1=`-1`) is undocumented |\n| 12 | LOW | `src/report/tags.ts:112–118` | `escTag` duplicates `escHtml` from `layout.ts`, missing single-quote escape |\n| 13 | LOW | `tests/heartbeat/config.test.ts` (multiple fixtures) | Fixture objects missing `report_base_url` field — technically non-conformant with `Config` type |\n\n### Architecture-Level Observations\n\n**TOC/Breadcrumb integration** — `knowledge.ts` and `goals.ts` are the only two renderers that integrate TOC. `breadcrumb.ts` and `toc.ts` were both introduced in Phase 2 but neither is wired into `transcript.ts`, `archive.ts`, or `todos.ts`. There is no breadcrumb rendered on any page in the codebase at all — `htmlShell` accepts `opts.breadcrumb` but no caller currently passes one. The `breadcrumb.ts` module and its test are entirely functional but the integration bridge in `build.ts` was never completed.\n\n**Mermaid injection pattern** — All four content renderers (`knowledge`, `goals`, `todos`, `archive`) implement the `hasMermaid ? { extraHead: MERMAID_CDN_SCRIPT } : {}` pattern correctly and consistently. This is well-designed.\n\n**`noUncheckedIndexedAccess` compliance** — The codebase consistently uses `?? ''` or null-checks after array/record indexing (e.g., `match[1] ?? ''`, `lines[i] ?? ''`, `starts[i]`). No raw unchecked index access was found in the reviewed files.\n\n**`exactOptionalPropertyTypes` compliance** — `BuildReportOptions` uses `field?: T` correctly. `HtmlShellOptions` optional fields are handled with `?? default` at use sites. No `field: T | undefined` anti-pattern was found.\n\n**`.js` extension compliance** — All relative imports in the reviewed files correctly use `.js` extensions for NodeNext ESM compatibility.",
    "type": "text"
  },

两次 review 都找到了问题。以下是合并后的清单，按严重度排序：

---

### CRITICAL — 必须修复

| # | 文件 | 问题 |
|---|------|------|
| C1 | src/report/build.ts:36 | renderTagBadges 导入但从未使用（dead import） |
| C2 | tests/report/build.test.ts | pdf: false 字段传给 buildReport() 但 BuildReportOptions 里不存在该字段 — latent type error |

### HIGH — 功能 bug

| # | 文件 | 问题 |
|---|------|------|
| H1 | src/report/render/rss.ts | toRfc2822 的 try/catch 永远不会触发；new Date('invalid') 不 throw，会静默输出 "Invalid Date" 作为 <pubDate> |
| H2 | src/report/render/toc.ts | injectHeadingIds 正则 [^<]* 对含内联 HTML（bold, ` code ）的标题失效，TOC 锚点死链 |
| H3 | src/report/render/todos.ts, archive.ts | 没有 TOC 注入，与 knowledge.ts/goals.ts` 风格不一致 |

### MEDIUM — 质量问题

| # | 文件 | 问题 |
|---|------|------|
| M1 | src/report/render/search.ts | entry.snippet = snippet 在构造后 mutate 对象，且 JSON.stringify 调了两次 |
| M2 | src/report/render/breadcrumb.ts:34 | 无 URL 的非末项生成 <span class="breadcrumb-item" > — 多余空格 |
| M3 | src/report/tags.ts | 单个 tag 无长度上限，未做 t.length <= 50 过滤 |
| M4 | src/report/render/dashboard.ts | 死函数 transcriptHref 从未被调用 |
| M5 | tests/heartbeat/config.test.ts | report_base_url 无测试覆盖（parse/default/roundtrip） |

### LOW — 小问题

| # | 文件 | 问题 |
|---|------|------|
| L1 | src/report/tags.ts | escTag 重复实现了 layout.ts 的 escHtml，且少了单引号转义 |
| L2 | tests/heartbeat/config.test.ts | fixture 对象缺 report_base_url 字段，类型不完整 |

---

两次 review 在 H1（RSS Invalid Date）、H2（TOC regex）、C1（dead import）、C2（pdf 字段）上完全一致，确认这些都是真实 bug。

要我现在全部修复（CRITICAL + HIGH + MEDIUM），然后再提交 PR 吗？

Tool Result #932 Read toolu_01EE58ECYnMYWWiv1ss8SJzy 16:26:06
Read output=1→/** 2→ * Table of contents extraction and rendering. 3→ * Extracts h2/h3 headings from raw markdown and generates a sticky TOC nav. 4→ */ 5→ 6→import { escHtml } from './layou...
Show output payload 
1→/**
     2→ * Table of contents extraction and rendering.
     3→ * Extracts h2/h3 headings from raw markdown and generates a sticky TOC nav.
     4→ */
     5→
     6→import { escHtml } from './layout.js'
     7→
     8→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
     9→// Types
    10→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    11→
    12→export interface TocEntry {
    13→  level: 2 | 3
    14→  text: string
    15→  /** URL-safe anchor id, duplicate-free (suffixed -1, -2, …) */
    16→  id: string
    17→}
    18→
    19→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    20→// Extract TOC from raw markdown
    21→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    22→
    23→/**
    24→ * Parse h2 and h3 headings from markdown source.
    25→ * Duplicate heading text gets a numeric suffix: `-1`, `-2`, …
    26→ */
    27→export function extractToc(md: string): TocEntry[] {
    28→  const entries: TocEntry[] = []
    29→  const seen = new Map<string, number>()
    30→
    31→  for (const line of md.split('\n')) {
    32→    const h2 = line.match(/^## (.+)$/)
    33→    const h3 = line.match(/^### (.+)$/)
    34→    const match = h2 ?? h3
    35→    if (!match) continue
    36→
    37→    const level: 2 | 3 = h2 ? 2 : 3
    38→    const text = (match[1] ?? '').trim()
    39→    const base = slugifyHeading(text)
    40→
    41→    const count = seen.get(base) ?? 0
    42→    const id = count === 0 ? base : `${base}-${count}`
    43→    seen.set(base, count + 1)
    44→
    45→    entries.push({ level, text, id })
    46→  }
    47→
    48→  return entries
    49→}
    50→
    51→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    52→// Render TOC HTML
    53→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    54→
    55→/**
    56→ * Render a collapsible TOC nav from extracted entries.
    57→ * Returns empty string when entries is empty.
    58→ */
    59→export function renderToc(entries: TocEntry[]): string {
    60→  if (entries.length === 0) return ''
    61→
    62→  const items = entries
    63→    .map(e => {
    64→      const indent = e.level === 3 ? ' style="padding-left:1.25rem"' : ''
    65→      return `<li${indent}><a href="#${escHtml(e.id)}">${escHtml(e.text)}</a></li>`
    66→    })
    67→    .join('\n      ')
    68→
    69→  return `<nav class="toc" id="toc">
    70→  <button class="toc-toggle" id="toc-toggle" type="button" aria-expanded="true">
    71→    Contents <span class="toc-arrow">▾</span>
    72→  </button>
    73→  <ul class="toc-list" id="toc-list">
    74→    ${items}
    75→  </ul>
    76→</nav>`
    77→}
    78→
    79→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    80→// Heading anchor injection
    81→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    82→
    83→/**
    84→ * Post-process rendered HTML to add id attributes to heading tags so
    85→ * TOC anchor links resolve correctly.
    86→ * Only modifies <h2> and <h3> tags without existing id attributes.
    87→ */
    88→export function injectHeadingIds(html: string, entries: TocEntry[]): string {
    89→  if (entries.length === 0) return html
    90→
    91→  // Process entries in order so duplicates match correctly
    92→  const remaining = [...entries]
    93→  return html.replace(/<h([23])>([^<]*)<\/h[23]>/g, (full, levelStr, innerHtml) => {
    94→    const level = parseInt(levelStr, 10) as 2 | 3
    95→    const idx = remaining.findIndex(e => e.level === level && escHtml(e.text) === innerHtml.trim())
    96→    if (idx === -1) return full
    97→    const entry = remaining.splice(idx, 1)[0]!
    98→    return `<h${level} id="${escHtml(entry.id)}">${innerHtml}</h${level}>`
    99→  })
   100→}
   101→
   102→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   103→// Internal helpers
   104→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   105→
   106→function slugifyHeading(text: string): string {
   107→  return text
   108→    .toLowerCase()
   109→    .replace(/[^\w\s-]/g, '')   // remove non-word chars except hyphen
   110→    .trim()
   111→    .replace(/[\s_]+/g, '-')   // spaces/underscores → hyphen
   112→    .replace(/-+/g, '-')       // collapse multiple hyphens
   113→}
   114→

1→import { describe, it, expect, beforeEach, afterEach, vi } from 'vitest'

     3→import { join } from 'node:path'

     6→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
     7→// Mock homedir so all path helpers point to a temp directory
     8→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
     9→
    10→let tmpDir: string
    11→
    12→vi.mock('node:os', async () => {

    14→  return {
    15→    ...actual,
    16→    homedir: () => tmpDir,
    17→  }
    18→})
    19→
    20→// Import after mock setup so configPath() / memorytreeRoot() use the mock
    21→import {
    22→  loadConfig,
    23→  saveConfig,
    24→  intervalToSeconds,
    25→  registerProject,
    26→  configPath,
    27→  memorytreeRoot,
    28→} from '../../src/heartbeat/config.js'
    29→
    30→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    31→// Setup / Teardown
    32→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    33→
    34→beforeEach(() => {
    35→  tmpDir = mkdtempSync(join(tmpdir(), 'cfg-test-'))
    36→})
    37→
    38→afterEach(() => {
    39→  rmSync(tmpDir, { recursive: true, force: true })
    40→})
    41→
    42→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    43→// configPath / memorytreeRoot
    44→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    45→
    46→describe('configPath / memorytreeRoot', () => {

    48→    const root = memorytreeRoot()
    49→    expect(root).toContain('.memorytree')
    50→    expect(root.startsWith(tmpDir)).toBe(true)

Tool Result #934 Read toolu_01BVkMRfPgGcJ1PTxRfB2g78 16:26:06
Read output=1→/** 2→ * Dashboard page renderer: stat cards + SVG charts + recent sessions. 3→ */ 4→ 5→import type { ManifestEntry } from '../../types/transcript.js' 6→import type { ReportSt...
Show output payload 
1→/**
     2→ * Dashboard page renderer: stat cards + SVG charts + recent sessions.
     3→ */
     4→
     5→import type { ManifestEntry } from '../../types/transcript.js'
     6→import type { ReportStats } from '../../types/report.js'
     7→import type { Translations } from '../i18n/types.js'
     8→import { clientBadge, escHtml, htmlShell, renderNav, transcriptUrlFromRoot } from './layout.js'
     9→import { renderHeatmap, renderClientDoughnut, renderWeeklyLine, renderToolBar } from './charts.js'
    10→
    11→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    12→// Dashboard
    13→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    14→
    15→export function renderDashboard(stats: ReportStats, manifests: ManifestEntry[], t?: Translations): string {
    16→  const nav = renderNav('dashboard', 0, t)
    17→  const content = [
    18→    renderPageHeader(stats, t),
    19→    renderStatsCards(stats, t),
    20→    renderCharts(stats),
    21→    renderRecentSessions(manifests, t),
    22→  ].join('\n')
    23→
    24→  const lang = t ? detectLang(t) : 'en'
    25→  return htmlShell(t?.dashboard.title ?? 'Dashboard', content, nav, '', lang)
    26→}
    27→
    28→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    29→// Internal renderers
    30→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    31→
    32→function renderPageHeader(stats: ReportStats, t?: Translations): string {
    33→  const from = stats.dateRange.from.slice(0, 10) || '—'
    34→  const to = stats.dateRange.to.slice(0, 10) || '—'
    35→  const title = t?.dashboard.title ?? 'Memory Dashboard'
    36→  const subtitle = (t?.dashboard.subtitle ?? 'Activity from {from} to {to}')
    37→    .replace('{from}', from)
    38→    .replace('{to}', to)
    39→  return `<div class="page-header">
    40→  <h1>${escHtml(title)}</h1>
    41→  <p class="subtitle">${escHtml(subtitle)}</p>
    42→</div>`
    43→}
    44→
    45→function renderStatsCards(stats: ReportStats, t?: Translations): string {
    46→  const cards = [
    47→    { value: fmtNum(stats.totalSessions), label: t?.dashboard.sessions ?? 'Sessions' },
    48→    { value: fmtNum(stats.totalMessages), label: t?.dashboard.messages ?? 'Messages' },
    49→    { value: fmtNum(stats.totalToolEvents), label: t?.dashboard.toolEvents ?? 'Tool Events' },
    50→    { value: fmtNum(stats.activeDays), label: t?.dashboard.activeDays ?? 'Active Days' },
    51→  ]
    52→
    53→  const html = cards
    54→    .map(
    55→      c => `<div class="card">
    56→  <div class="card-title">${escHtml(c.label)}</div>
    57→  <div class="stat-value">${c.value}</div>
    58→</div>`,
    59→    )
    60→    .join('')
    61→
    62→  return `<div class="stats-grid">${html}</div>`
    63→}
    64→
    65→function renderCharts(stats: ReportStats): string {
    66→  const heatmap = renderHeatmap(stats.dayBuckets)
    67→  const doughnut = renderClientDoughnut(stats.clientCounts)
    68→  const line = renderWeeklyLine(stats.weekBuckets)
    69→  const bar = renderToolBar(stats.toolCounts)
    70→
    71→  return `<div class="chart-grid">
    72→  <div class="chart-card full-width">
    73→    <div class="chart-title">Activity (last 365 days)</div>
    74→    ${heatmap}
    75→  </div>
    76→  <div class="chart-card">
    77→    <div class="chart-title">Client Distribution</div>
    78→    ${doughnut}
    79→  </div>
    80→  <div class="chart-card">
    81→    <div class="chart-title">Messages / Week (last 52 weeks)</div>
    82→    ${line}
    83→  </div>
    84→  <div class="chart-card full-width">
    85→    <div class="chart-title">Top 10 Tools</div>
    86→    ${bar}
    87→  </div>
    88→</div>`
    89→}
    90→
    91→function renderRecentSessions(manifests: ManifestEntry[], t?: Translations): string {
    92→  const recent = [...manifests]
    93→    .sort((a, b) => b.started_at.localeCompare(a.started_at))
    94→    .slice(0, 10)
    95→
    96→  const heading = t?.dashboard.recentSessions ?? 'Recent Sessions'
    97→  const clientLabel = t?.sessions.client ?? 'Client'
    98→  const titleLabel = 'Title'
    99→  const dateLabel = t?.sessions.date ?? 'Date'
   100→  const msgsLabel = t?.sessions.msgs ?? 'Msgs'
   101→  const toolsLabel = t?.sessions.tools ?? 'Tools'
   102→
   103→  if (recent.length === 0) {
   104→    return `<div class="card"><p style="color:var(--text-muted)">${escHtml(t?.sessions.noSessions ?? 'No sessions imported yet.')}</p></div>`
   105→  }
   106→
   107→  const rows = recent
   108→    .map(m => {
   109→      const url = transcriptUrlFromRoot(m)
   110→      const date = m.started_at.slice(0, 10)
   111→      const badge = clientBadge(m.client)
   112→      const msgs = m.message_count
   113→      const tools = m.tool_event_count
   114→      const summary = ''
   115→      const meta = `${escHtml(m.client)} · ${escHtml(date)} · ${msgs} msgs`
   116→      return `<tr>
   117→  <td>${badge}</td>
   118→  <td><a href="${escHtml(url)}" data-summary="${escHtml(summary)}" data-meta="${escHtml(meta)}">${escHtml(m.title || m.session_id)}</a></td>
   119→  <td style="color:var(--text-muted)">${escHtml(date)}</td>
   120→  <td style="color:var(--text-muted);text-align:right">${msgs}</td>
   121→  <td style="color:var(--text-muted);text-align:right">${tools}</td>
   122→</tr>`
   123→    })
   124→    .join('')
   125→
   126→  return `<h2>${escHtml(heading)}</h2>
   127→<div class="card" style="padding:0;overflow:hidden">
   128→<table>
   129→<thead><tr>
   130→  <th>${escHtml(clientLabel)}</th><th>Title</th><th>${escHtml(dateLabel)}</th>
   131→  <th style="text-align:right">${escHtml(msgsLabel)}</th>
   132→  <th style="text-align:right">${escHtml(toolsLabel)}</th>
   133→</tr></thead>
   134→<tbody>${rows}</tbody>
   135→</table>
   136→</div>`
   137→}
   138→
   139→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   140→// Helpers
   141→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   142→
   143→function fmtNum(n: number): string {
   144→  return n.toLocaleString('en-US')
   145→}
   146→
   147→function transcriptHref(m: ManifestEntry): string {
   148→  return transcriptUrlFromRoot(m)
   149→}
   150→
   151→function detectLang(t: Translations): string {
   152→  // Heuristic: check if nav.dashboard is Chinese
   153→  return /[\u4e00-\u9fff]/.test(t.nav.dashboard) ? 'zh-CN' : 'en'
   154→}
   155→

Read output=1→import { describe, it, expect, beforeEach, afterEach } from 'vitest' 2→import { 3→ mkdtempSync, 4→ mkdirSync, 5→ writeFileSync, 6→ readFileSync, 7→ rmSync, 8→ existsSync, 9→ r...
Show output payload 
1→import { describe, it, expect, beforeEach, afterEach } from 'vitest'
     2→import {
     3→  mkdtempSync,
     4→  mkdirSync,
     5→  writeFileSync,
     6→  readFileSync,
     7→  rmSync,
     8→  existsSync,
     9→  readdirSync,
    10→} from 'node:fs'
    11→import { join } from 'node:path'
    12→import { tmpdir } from 'node:os'
    13→import { buildReport, ensureGitignore, parseMessagesFromMarkdown } from '../../src/report/build.js'
    14→
    15→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    16→// Setup
    17→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    18→
    19→let tmpDir: string
    20→
    21→beforeEach(() => {
    22→  tmpDir = mkdtempSync(join(tmpdir(), 'build-test-'))
    23→  delete process.env['ANTHROPIC_API_KEY']
    24→})
    25→
    26→afterEach(() => {
    27→  rmSync(tmpDir, { recursive: true, force: true })
    28→  delete process.env['ANTHROPIC_API_KEY']
    29→})
    30→
    31→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    32→// ensureGitignore
    33→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    34→
    35→describe('ensureGitignore', () => {
    36→  it('creates .gitignore with entry if missing', () => {
    37→    ensureGitignore(tmpDir, 'Memory/07_reports/')
    38→    const path = join(tmpDir, '.gitignore')
    39→    expect(existsSync(path)).toBe(true)
    40→    const content = readFileSync(path, 'utf-8')
    41→    expect(content).toContain('Memory/07_reports/')
    42→  })
    43→
    44→  it('appends to existing .gitignore', () => {
    45→    const path = join(tmpDir, '.gitignore')
    46→    writeFileSync(path, 'node_modules/\ndist/\n', 'utf-8')
    47→    ensureGitignore(tmpDir, 'Memory/07_reports/')
    48→    const content = readFileSync(path, 'utf-8')
    49→    expect(content).toContain('node_modules/')
    50→    expect(content).toContain('Memory/07_reports/')
    51→  })
    52→
    53→  it('does not add duplicate entry', () => {
    54→    const path = join(tmpDir, '.gitignore')
    55→    writeFileSync(path, 'Memory/07_reports/\n', 'utf-8')
    56→    ensureGitignore(tmpDir, 'Memory/07_reports/')
    57→    const content = readFileSync(path, 'utf-8')
    58→    const count = (content.match(/Memory\/07_reports\//g) ?? []).length
    59→    expect(count).toBe(1)
    60→  })
    61→})
    62→
    63→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    64→// parseMessagesFromMarkdown
    65→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    66→
    67→describe('parseMessagesFromMarkdown', () => {
    68→  it('parses messages from clean markdown format', () => {
    69→    const md = `---
    70→client: codex
    71→---
    72→
    73→# Test Session
    74→
    75→## Messages
    76→
    77→### 1. user
    78→- Timestamp: \`2026-03-10T10:00:00Z\`
    79→
    80→Hello, how are you?
    81→
    82→### 2. assistant
    83→- Timestamp: \`2026-03-10T10:00:05Z\`
    84→
    85→I'm doing well, thank you!
    86→`
    87→    const messages = parseMessagesFromMarkdown(md)
    88→    expect(messages).toHaveLength(2)
    89→    expect(messages[0]!.role).toBe('user')
    90→    expect(messages[0]!.text).toContain('Hello')
    91→    expect(messages[1]!.role).toBe('assistant')
    92→    expect(messages[1]!.text).toContain("I'm doing well")
    93→  })
    94→
    95→  it('returns empty array when no ## Messages section', () => {
    96→    const md = '# Title\n\nSome content.'
    97→    expect(parseMessagesFromMarkdown(md)).toEqual([])
    98→  })
    99→
   100→  it('parses timestamps', () => {
   101→    const md = `\n## Messages\n\n### 1. user\n- Timestamp: \`2026-03-10T10:00:00Z\`\n\nHello`
   102→    const messages = parseMessagesFromMarkdown(md)
   103→    expect(messages[0]!.timestamp).toBe('2026-03-10T10:00:00Z')
   104→  })
   105→})
   106→
   107→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   108→// buildReport integration test
   109→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   110→
   111→describe('buildReport', () => {
   112→  function scaffoldMemory(root: string): void {
   113→    // Create directory structure
   114→    const manifestsDir = join(root, 'Memory', '06_transcripts', 'manifests', 'codex', '2026', '03')
   115→    const cleanDir = join(root, 'Memory', '06_transcripts', 'clean', 'codex', '2026', '03')
   116→    mkdirSync(manifestsDir, { recursive: true })
   117→    mkdirSync(cleanDir, { recursive: true })
   118→
   119→    const stem = 'test-session__deadbeef'
   120→    const manifest = {
   121→      client: 'codex',
   122→      project: 'test',
   123→      session_id: 'test-session-001',
   124→      raw_sha256: 'deadbeef' + '0'.repeat(56),
   125→      title: 'Integration Test Session',
   126→      started_at: '2026-03-10T10:00:00Z',
   127→      imported_at: '2026-03-10T10:01:00Z',
   128→      cwd: '/home/user/project',
   129→      branch: 'main',
   130→      raw_source_path: '/src/session.jsonl',
   131→      raw_upload_permission: 'not-set',
   132→      global_raw_path: '',
   133→      global_clean_path: '',
   134→      global_manifest_path: '',
   135→      repo_raw_path: '',
   136→      repo_clean_path: `Memory/06_transcripts/clean/codex/2026/03/${stem}.md`,
   137→      repo_manifest_path: `Memory/06_transcripts/manifests/codex/2026/03/${stem}.json`,
   138→      message_count: 2,
   139→      tool_event_count: 0,
   140→      cleaning_mode: 'deterministic-code',
   141→      repo_mirror_enabled: true,
   142→    }
   143→
   144→    writeFileSync(
   145→      join(manifestsDir, `${stem}.json`),
   146→      JSON.stringify(manifest, null, 2),
   147→      'utf-8',
   148→    )
   149→
   150→    const cleanMd = `---
   151→client: codex
   152→---
   153→
   154→# Integration Test Session
   155→
   156→## Messages
   157→
   158→### 1. user
   159→- Timestamp: \`2026-03-10T10:00:00Z\`
   160→
   161→Build the report system.
   162→
   163→### 2. assistant
   164→- Timestamp: \`2026-03-10T10:00:05Z\`
   165→
   166→I'll help with that!
   167→`
   168→    writeFileSync(join(cleanDir, `${stem}.md`), cleanMd, 'utf-8')
   169→  }
   170→
   171→  it('creates all expected HTML files', async () => {
   172→    scaffoldMemory(tmpDir)
   173→    const output = join(tmpDir, 'Memory', '07_reports')
   174→
   175→    await buildReport({
   176→      root: tmpDir,
   177→      output,
   178→      noAi: true,
   179→      pdf: false,
   180→    })
   181→
   182→    expect(existsSync(join(output, 'index.html'))).toBe(true)
   183→    expect(existsSync(join(output, 'transcripts', 'index.html'))).toBe(true)
   184→    expect(existsSync(join(output, 'goals', 'index.html'))).toBe(true)
   185→    expect(existsSync(join(output, 'knowledge', 'index.html'))).toBe(true)
   186→    expect(existsSync(join(output, 'search.html'))).toBe(true)
   187→  })
   188→
   189→  it('generates individual transcript HTML with SVG charts in dashboard', async () => {
   190→    scaffoldMemory(tmpDir)
   191→    const output = join(tmpDir, 'Memory', '07_reports')
   192→
   193→    await buildReport({ root: tmpDir, output, noAi: true, pdf: false })
   194→
   195→    // Dashboard should contain SVG
   196→    const dashHtml = readFileSync(join(output, 'index.html'), 'utf-8')
   197→    expect(dashHtml).toContain('<svg')
   198→
   199→    // Individual transcript should exist
   200→    const transcriptDir = join(output, 'transcripts', 'codex')
   201→    expect(existsSync(transcriptDir)).toBe(true)
   202→    const files = readdirSync(transcriptDir)
   203→    expect(files.some(f => f.endsWith('.html'))).toBe(true)
   204→  })
   205→
   206→  it('creates .gitignore entry for Memory/07_reports/', async () => {
   207→    scaffoldMemory(tmpDir)
   208→    const output = join(tmpDir, 'Memory', '07_reports')
   209→
   210→    await buildReport({ root: tmpDir, output, noAi: true, pdf: false })
   211→
   212→    const gitignore = readFileSync(join(tmpDir, '.gitignore'), 'utf-8')
   213→    expect(gitignore).toContain('Memory/07_reports/')
   214→  })
   215→
   216→  it('handles empty Memory directory gracefully', async () => {
   217→    // Create root but no transcripts
   218→    mkdirSync(join(tmpDir, 'Memory'), { recursive: true })
   219→    const output = join(tmpDir, 'Memory', '07_reports')
   220→
   221→    await expect(
   222→      buildReport({ root: tmpDir, output, noAi: true, pdf: false }),
   223→    ).resolves.toBeUndefined()
   224→
   225→    expect(existsSync(join(output, 'index.html'))).toBe(true)
   226→  })
   227→})
   228→

}

H2: 修复 toc.ts injectHeadingIds 正则 + 匹配逻辑：

Tool Call #947 Edit toolu_01NqLsW4NaamqXnEyvCprLjT 16:28:05
Edit input={"file_path":"C:/Users/ai/.codex/skills/memorytree-workflow/src/report/render/toc.ts","new_string":"export function injectHeadingIds(html: string, entries: TocEntry[]): string {...
Show input payload 
{
  "replace_all": false,
  "file_path": "C:/Users/ai/.codex/skills/memorytree-workflow/src/report/render/toc.ts",
  "old_string": "export function injectHeadingIds(html: string, entries: TocEntry[]): string {\n  if (entries.length === 0) return html\n\n  // Process entries in order so duplicates match correctly\n  const remaining = [...entries]\n  return html.replace(/<h([23])>([^<]*)<\\/h[23]>/g, (full, levelStr, innerHtml) => {\n    const level = parseInt(levelStr, 10) as 2 | 3\n    const idx = remaining.findIndex(e => e.level === level && escHtml(e.text) === innerHtml.trim())\n    if (idx === -1) return full\n    const entry = remaining.splice(idx, 1)[0]!\n    return `<h${level} id=\"${escHtml(entry.id)}\">${innerHtml}</h${level}>`\n  })\n}",
  "new_string": "export function injectHeadingIds(html: string, entries: TocEntry[]): string {\n  if (entries.length === 0) return html\n\n  // Process entries in order so duplicates match correctly\n  const remaining = [...entries]\n  // [\\s\\S]*? allows inline HTML inside headings (e.g. <strong>, <code>)\n  return html.replace(/<h([23])>([\\s\\S]*?)<\\/h[23]>/g, (full, levelStr, innerHtml) => {\n    const level = parseInt(levelStr, 10) as 2 | 3\n    // Strip HTML tags from rendered inner content to get plain text for matching\n    const innerText = (innerHtml as string).replace(/<[^>]+>/g, '').trim()\n    const idx = remaining.findIndex(e => {\n      if (e.level !== level) return false\n      // Compare plain text: strip markdown markers from entry text, strip HTML from inner\n      return stripInlineMarkdown(e.text) === innerText || escHtml(e.text) === (innerHtml as string).trim()\n    })\n    if (idx === -1) return full\n    const entry = remaining.splice(idx, 1)[0]!\n    return `<h${level} id=\"${escHtml(entry.id)}\">${innerHtml}</h${level}>`\n  })\n}"
}

  "replace_all": false,
  "file_path": "C:/Users/ai/.codex/skills/memorytree-workflow/src/report/render/toc.ts",
  "old_string": "function slugifyHeading(text: string): string {\n  return text\n    .toLowerCase()\n    .replace(/[^\\w\\s-]/g, '')   // remove non-word chars except hyphen\n    .trim()\n    .replace(/[\\s_]+/g, '-')   // spaces/underscores → hyphen\n    .replace(/-+/g, '-')       // collapse multiple hyphens\n}",
  "new_string": "function slugifyHeading(text: string): string {\n  return text\n    .toLowerCase()\n    .replace(/[^\\w\\s-]/g, '')   // remove non-word chars except hyphen\n    .trim()\n    .replace(/[\\s_]+/g, '-')   // spaces/underscores → hyphen\n    .replace(/-+/g, '-')       // collapse multiple hyphens\n}\n\n/**\n * Strip common markdown inline markers to get plain text.\n * Used to match TOC entries against rendered HTML heading content.\n */\nfunction stripInlineMarkdown(text: string): string {\n  return text\n    .replace(/\\*\\*([^*]+)\\*\\*/g, '$1')          // **bold**\n    .replace(/\\*([^*]+)\\*/g, '$1')               // *italic*\n    .replace(/`([^`]+)`/g, '$1')                 // `code`\n    .replace(/\\[([^\\]]+)\\]\\([^)]+\\)/g, '$1')    // [link](url) → text\n    .trim()\n}"
}

Edit input={"file_path":"C:/Users/ai/.codex/skills/memorytree-workflow/src/report/render/todos.ts","new_string":"/**\n * Todos page: renders markdown files from Memory/02_todos/.\n */\n\ni...

{
  "file_path": "C:/Users/ai/.codex/skills/memorytree-workflow/src/report/render/todos.ts",
  "new_string": "/**\n * Todos page: renders markdown files from Memory/02_todos/.\n */\n\nimport type { Translations } from '../i18n/types.js'\nimport { escHtml, htmlShell, renderNav, slugifyName } from './layout.js'\nimport type { MarkdownFile } from './layout.js'\nimport { hasMermaidBlocks, markdownToHtml, MERMAID_CDN_SCRIPT } from './markdown.js'\nimport { extractToc, renderToc, injectHeadingIds } from './toc.js'\n\nexport type TodoFile = MarkdownFile\n\nexport function renderTodos(files: TodoFile[], t?: Translations): string {\n  const nav = renderNav('todos', 1, t)\n  const title = t?.nav.todos ?? 'Todos'\n\n  if (files.length === 0) {\n    const content = `<div class=\"page-header\">\n  <h1>${escHtml(title)}</h1>\n  <p class=\"subtitle\">No todo files found in Memory/02_todos/.</p>\n</div>`\n    return htmlShell(title, content, nav)\n  }\n\n  const hasMermaid = files.some(f => hasMermaidBlocks(f.content))\n\n  const sections = files\n    .map(f => {\n      const tocEntries = extractToc(f.content)\n      const rawHtml = markdownToHtml(f.content)\n      const htmlContent = injectHeadingIds(rawHtml, tocEntries)\n      const toc = renderToc(tocEntries)\n      return `<div class=\"card\" id=\"${escHtml(slugifyName(f.filename))}\">\n  <h2>${escHtml(f.title)}</h2>\n  <p style=\"font-size:0.8rem;color:var(--text-muted);margin-bottom:1rem\">${escHtml(f.filename)}</p>\n  ${toc}\n  <div class=\"markdown-body\">${htmlContent}</div>\n</div>`\n    })\n    .join('\\n')\n\n  const content = `<div class=\"page-header\">\n  <h1>${escHtml(title)}</h1>\n  <p class=\"subtitle\">${files.length} todo file(s)</p>\n</div>\n${sections}`\n\n  return htmlShell(title, content, nav, hasMermaid ? { extraHead: MERMAID_CDN_SCRIPT } : {})\n}\n"
}

1→/**
     2→ * Todos page: renders markdown files from Memory/02_todos/.
     3→ */
     4→
     5→import type { Translations } from '../i18n/types.js'
     6→import { escHtml, htmlShell, renderNav, slugifyName } from './layout.js'
     7→import type { MarkdownFile } from './layout.js'
     8→import { hasMermaidBlocks, markdownToHtml, MERMAID_CDN_SCRIPT } from './markdown.js'
     9→
    10→export type TodoFile = MarkdownFile
    11→
    12→export function renderTodos(files: TodoFile[], t?: Translations): string {
    13→  const nav = renderNav('todos', 1, t)
    14→  const title = t?.nav.todos ?? 'Todos'
    15→
    16→  if (files.length === 0) {
    17→    const content = `<div class="page-header">
    18→  <h1>${escHtml(title)}</h1>
    19→  <p class="subtitle">No todo files found in Memory/02_todos/.</p>
    20→</div>`
    21→    return htmlShell(title, content, nav)
    22→  }
    23→
    24→  const sections = files
    25→    .map(f => {
    26→      const htmlContent = markdownToHtml(f.content)
    27→      return `<div class="card" id="${escHtml(slugifyName(f.filename))}">
    28→  <h2>${escHtml(f.title)}</h2>
    29→  <p style="font-size:0.8rem;color:var(--text-muted);margin-bottom:1rem">${escHtml(f.filename)}</p>
    30→  <div class="markdown-body">${htmlContent}</div>
    31→</div>`
    32→    })
    33→    .join('\n')
    34→
    35→  const content = `<div class="page-header">
    36→  <h1>${escHtml(title)}</h1>
    37→  <p class="subtitle">${files.length} todo file(s)</p>
    38→</div>
    39→${sections}`
    40→
    41→  const hasMermaid = files.some(f => hasMermaidBlocks(f.content))
    42→  return htmlShell(title, content, nav, hasMermaid ? { extraHead: MERMAID_CDN_SCRIPT } : {})
    43→}
    44→

{
  "replace_all": false,
  "file_path": "C:/Users/ai/.codex/skills/memorytree-workflow/src/report/render/search.ts",
  "old_string": "  for (const m of manifests) {\n    let snippet = getSnippet(m).slice(0, SNIPPET_LEN)\n    const project = m.project || lastPathSegment(m.cwd) || 'unknown'\n    const entry: SearchIndexEntry = {\n      url: transcriptUrl(m),\n      title: m.title || m.session_id,\n      client: m.client,\n      project,\n      date: m.started_at.slice(0, 10),\n      snippet,\n    }\n    const entryBytes = JSON.stringify(entry).length\n    if (totalBytes + entryBytes > MAX_INDEX_BYTES) {\n      // Truncate snippet to fit within budget\n      const remaining = MAX_INDEX_BYTES - totalBytes - (entryBytes - snippet.length) - 10\n      if (remaining < 20) break\n      snippet = snippet.slice(0, remaining)\n      entry.snippet = snippet\n    }\n    totalBytes += JSON.stringify(entry).length\n    entries.push(entry)\n  }",
  "new_string": "  for (const m of manifests) {\n    let snippet = getSnippet(m).slice(0, SNIPPET_LEN)\n    const project = m.project || lastPathSegment(m.cwd) || 'unknown'\n\n    // Check if truncating the snippet is needed to stay within budget\n    const baseBytes = JSON.stringify({\n      url: transcriptUrl(m),\n      title: m.title || m.session_id,\n      client: m.client,\n      project,\n      date: m.started_at.slice(0, 10),\n      snippet: '',\n    }).length\n    const budgetForSnippet = MAX_INDEX_BYTES - totalBytes - baseBytes - 10\n    if (budgetForSnippet < 20) break\n    if (baseBytes + snippet.length > MAX_INDEX_BYTES - totalBytes) {\n      snippet = snippet.slice(0, Math.max(0, budgetForSnippet))\n    }\n\n    const entry: SearchIndexEntry = {\n      url: transcriptUrl(m),\n      title: m.title || m.session_id,\n      client: m.client,\n      project,\n      date: m.started_at.slice(0, 10),\n      snippet,\n    }\n    totalBytes += JSON.stringify(entry).length\n    entries.push(entry)\n  }"
}

Tool Result #971 Read toolu_01DrckEdTe3nBBjTAyazX2za 16:30:07
Read output=1→/** 2→ * Breadcrumb navigation helper. 3→ * Renders an accessible <nav aria-label="breadcrumb"> from a list of items. 4→ */ 5→ 6→import { escHtml } from './layout.js' 7→ 8→// ...
Show output payload 
1→/**
     2→ * Breadcrumb navigation helper.
     3→ * Renders an accessible <nav aria-label="breadcrumb"> from a list of items.
     4→ */
     5→
     6→import { escHtml } from './layout.js'
     7→
     8→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
     9→// Types
    10→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    11→
    12→export interface BreadcrumbItem {
    13→  label: string
    14→  /** URL of this crumb. Omit for the current (last) item. */
    15→  url?: string
    16→}
    17→
    18→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    19→// Renderer
    20→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    21→
    22→/**
    23→ * Render a breadcrumb trail.
    24→ * - Items with a `url` are rendered as links.
    25→ * - The last item (current page) has no link and gets aria-current="page".
    26→ * - Returns empty string when items array is empty.
    27→ */
    28→export function renderBreadcrumb(items: BreadcrumbItem[]): string {
    29→  if (items.length === 0) return ''
    30→
    31→  const parts = items.map((item, i) => {
    32→    const isLast = i === items.length - 1
    33→    if (isLast || !item.url) {
    34→      return `<span class="breadcrumb-item" ${isLast ? 'aria-current="page"' : ''}>${escHtml(item.label)}</span>`
    35→    }
    36→    return `<a class="breadcrumb-item" href="${escHtml(item.url)}">${escHtml(item.label)}</a>`
    37→  })
    38→
    39→  return `<nav class="breadcrumb" aria-label="breadcrumb">${parts.join('<span class="breadcrumb-sep">›</span>')}</nav>`
    40→}
    41→

  "file_path": "C:/Users/ai/.codex/skills/memorytree-workflow/src/report/tags.ts",
  "old_string": "/** Render tags as pill badges. Returns '' for empty array. */\nexport function renderTagBadges(tags: string[]): string {\n  if (tags.length === 0) return ''\n  const pills = tags\n    .map(t => `<span class=\"tag\" data-tag=\"${escTag(t)}\">${escTag(t)}</span>`)\n    .join('')\n  return `<div class=\"tag-list\">${pills}</div>`\n}\n\nfunction escTag(s: string): string {\n  return s\n    .replace(/&/g, '&amp;')\n    .replace(/</g, '&lt;')\n    .replace(/>/g, '&gt;')\n    .replace(/\"/g, '&quot;')\n}",
  "new_string": "/** Render tags as pill badges. Returns '' for empty array. */\nexport function renderTagBadges(tags: string[]): string {\n  if (tags.length === 0) return ''\n  const pills = tags\n    .map(t => `<span class=\"tag\" data-tag=\"${escHtml(t)}\">${escHtml(t)}</span>`)\n    .join('')\n  return `<div class=\"tag-list\">${pills}</div>`\n}"
}

  "old_string": "  it('round-trips all 4 fields via saveConfig/loadConfig', () => {\n    const original = {\n      heartbeat_interval: '5m',\n      auto_push: true,\n      log_level: 'info',\n      watch_dirs: [],\n      projects: [],\n      generate_report: false,\n      ai_summary_model: 'claude-haiku-4-5-20251001',\n      locale: 'zh-CN',\n      gh_pages_branch: 'gh-pages',\n      cname: 'memory.example.com',\n      webhook_url: 'https://hooks.slack.com/services/test',\n    } as const\n\n    saveConfig(original)\n    const loaded = loadConfig()\n    expect(loaded.locale).toBe('zh-CN')\n    expect(loaded.gh_pages_branch).toBe('gh-pages')\n    expect(loaded.cname).toBe('memory.example.com')\n    expect(loaded.webhook_url).toBe('https://hooks.slack.com/services/test')\n  })\n\n  it('handles non-string gh_pages_branch gracefully', () => {\n    const path = configPath()\n    mkdirSync(join(tmpDir, '.memorytree'), { recursive: true })\n    writeFileSync(path, 'gh_pages_branch = 42\\n')\n    const cfg = loadConfig()\n    expect(cfg.gh_pages_branch).toBe('')\n  })\n})",
  "new_string": "  it('round-trips all 4 fields via saveConfig/loadConfig', () => {\n    const original = {\n      heartbeat_interval: '5m',\n      auto_push: true,\n      log_level: 'info',\n      watch_dirs: [],\n      projects: [],\n      generate_report: false,\n      ai_summary_model: 'claude-haiku-4-5-20251001',\n      locale: 'zh-CN',\n      gh_pages_branch: 'gh-pages',\n      cname: 'memory.example.com',\n      webhook_url: 'https://hooks.slack.com/services/test',\n      report_base_url: '',\n    } as const\n\n    saveConfig(original)\n    const loaded = loadConfig()\n    expect(loaded.locale).toBe('zh-CN')\n    expect(loaded.gh_pages_branch).toBe('gh-pages')\n    expect(loaded.cname).toBe('memory.example.com')\n    expect(loaded.webhook_url).toBe('https://hooks.slack.com/services/test')\n  })\n\n  it('handles non-string gh_pages_branch gracefully', () => {\n    const path = configPath()\n    mkdirSync(join(tmpDir, '.memorytree'), { recursive: true })\n    writeFileSync(path, 'gh_pages_branch = 42\\n')\n    const cfg = loadConfig()\n    expect(cfg.gh_pages_branch).toBe('')\n  })\n})\n\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n// New field: report_base_url\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\ndescribe('report_base_url field', () => {\n  it('defaults to empty string', () => {\n    const cfg = loadConfig()\n    expect(cfg.report_base_url).toBe('')\n  })\n\n  it('parses report_base_url from TOML', () => {\n    const path = configPath()\n    mkdirSync(join(tmpDir, '.memorytree'), { recursive: true })\n    writeFileSync(path, 'report_base_url = \"https://memory.example.com\"\\n')\n    const cfg = loadConfig()\n    expect(cfg.report_base_url).toBe('https://memory.example.com')\n  })\n\n  it('defaults on missing field', () => {\n    const path = configPath()\n    mkdirSync(join(tmpDir, '.memorytree'), { recursive: true })\n    writeFileSync(path, 'heartbeat_interval = \"5m\"\\n')\n    const cfg = loadConfig()\n    expect(cfg.report_base_url).toBe('')\n  })\n\n  it('defaults on non-string value', () => {\n    const path = configPath()\n    mkdirSync(join(tmpDir, '.memorytree'), { recursive: true })\n    writeFileSync(path, 'report_base_url = 123\\n')\n    const cfg = loadConfig()\n    expect(cfg.report_base_url).toBe('')\n  })\n\n  it('round-trips via saveConfig/loadConfig', () => {\n    const original = {\n      heartbeat_interval: '5m',\n      auto_push: false,\n      log_level: 'info',\n      watch_dirs: [],\n      projects: [],\n      generate_report: false,\n      ai_summary_model: 'claude-haiku-4-5-20251001',\n      locale: 'en',\n      gh_pages_branch: '',\n      cname: '',\n      webhook_url: '',\n      report_base_url: 'https://memory.example.com',\n    } as const\n\n    saveConfig(original)\n    const loaded = loadConfig()\n    expect(loaded.report_base_url).toBe('https://memory.example.com')\n  })\n})"
}

所有 Review 问题已修复完毕。汇总：

| # | 修复内容 |
|---|---------|
| C1 | build.ts 删除未使用的 renderTagBadges import |
| C2 | build.test.ts 删除 4 处不存在的 pdf: false 字段 |
| H1 | rss.ts toRfc2822 改用 isNaN(d.getTime()) 检测 Invalid Date |
| H2 | toc.ts injectHeadingIds 正则改为 [\s\S]*?，匹配逻辑加 stripInlineMarkdown |
| H3 | todos.ts / archive.ts 统一加入 TOC 注入（与 knowledge/goals 一致） |
| M1 | search.ts 消除 entry mutation，单次 JSON.stringify |
| M2 | breadcrumb.ts 修复非末项无 URL 时的多余空格 |
| M3+L1 | tags.ts 加 length <= 50 过滤，用 escHtml 替换私有 escTag |
| M4 | dashboard.ts 删除从未调用的死函数 transcriptHref |
| M5+L2 | config.test.ts 新增 report_base_url 5条测试 + 修复现有 fixture 缺字段 |

    22→
    23→export function renderTranscript(
    24→  messages: RenderedMessage[],

    26→  summary: string,

    33→    renderHeader(manifest, t),
    34→    summary ? renderSummaryCard(summary, t) : '',
    35→    backlinks.length > 0 ? renderBacklinks(backlinks, t) : '',
    36→    renderMessages(messages, t),
    37→  ]
    38→    .filter(Boolean)
    39→    .join('\n')
    40→
    41→  const title = manifest.title || manifest.session_id
    42→  return htmlShell(title, content, nav)
    43→}

    46→// Sub-renderers
    47→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

    50→  const badge = clientBadge(m.client)

280→
   281→export interface HtmlShellOptions {
   282→  extraHead?: string
   283→  lang?: string
   284→  breadcrumb?: string
   285→  /** Inject reader-mode toggle button into the page */
   286→  readerMode?: boolean
   287→}
   288→
   289→export function htmlShell(
   290→  title: string,
   291→  content: string,
   292→  nav: string,
   293→  extraHeadOrOpts: string | HtmlShellOptions = '',
   294→  lang = 'en',
   295→): string {
   296→  // Accept legacy string arg (extraHead) or new options object
   297→  const opts: HtmlShellOptions = typeof extraHeadOrOpts === 'string'
   298→    ? { extraHead: extraHeadOrOpts, lang }
   299→    : extraHeadOrOpts
   300→  const extraHead = opts.extraHead ?? ''
   301→  const resolvedLang = opts.lang ?? lang
   302→  const breadcrumb = opts.breadcrumb ?? ''
   303→  const readerBtn = opts.readerMode !== false
   304→    ? `<button class="reader-toggle-btn" id="reader-toggle" type="button" title="Toggle reader mode">📖</button>`
   305→    : ''
   306→
   307→  const topbar = `<header class="topbar" id="topbar">
   308→  <span class="topbar-brand">MemoryTree</span>
   309→  <div class="topbar-actions">
   310→    <button class="theme-toggle-btn" id="theme-toggle-mobile" type="button">☀</button>
   311→    <button class="hamburger" id="hamburger" type="button">☰</button>
   312→  </div>
   313→</header>`
   314→
   315→  return `<!DOCTYPE html>
   316→<html lang="${escHtml(resolvedLang)}" data-theme="dark">
   317→<head>
   318→<meta charset="UTF-8">
   319→<meta name="viewport" content="width=device-width, initial-scale=1.0">
   320→<title>${escHtml(title)} — MemoryTree</title>
   321→<style>${REPORT_CSS}</style>
   322→${extraHead}
   323→${SIDEBAR_INLINE_JS}
   324→</head>
   325→<body>
   326→${topbar}
   327→${nav}
   328→<div class="main-content">
   329→  <div class="container">
   330→${breadcrumb ? `${breadcrumb}\n` : ''}<div style="display:flex;justify-content:flex-end;margin-bottom:0.5rem">${readerBtn}</div>
   331→${content}
   332→  </div>
   333→</div>
   334→</body>
   335→</html>`
   336→}
   337→

{
  "replace_all": false,
  "file_path": "C:/Users/ai/.codex/skills/memorytree-workflow/src/report/render/transcript.ts",
  "old_string": "export function renderTranscript(\n  messages: RenderedMessage[],\n  manifest: ManifestEntry,\n  summary: string,\n  backlinks: ManifestEntry[],\n  t?: Translations,\n): string {\n  const nav = renderNav('transcripts', 2, t)\n\n  const content = [\n    renderHeader(manifest, t),\n    summary ? renderSummaryCard(summary, t) : '',\n    backlinks.length > 0 ? renderBacklinks(backlinks, t) : '',\n    renderMessages(messages, t),\n  ]\n    .filter(Boolean)\n    .join('\\n')\n\n  const title = manifest.title || manifest.session_id\n  return htmlShell(title, content, nav)\n}",
  "new_string": "export function renderTranscript(\n  messages: RenderedMessage[],\n  manifest: ManifestEntry,\n  summary: string,\n  backlinks: ManifestEntry[],\n  t?: Translations,\n  reportBaseUrl = '',\n): string {\n  const nav = renderNav('transcripts', 2, t)\n\n  const content = [\n    renderHeader(manifest, t),\n    summary ? renderSummaryCard(summary, t) : '',\n    backlinks.length > 0 ? renderBacklinks(backlinks, t) : '',\n    renderMessages(messages, t),\n  ]\n    .filter(Boolean)\n    .join('\\n')\n\n  const title = manifest.title || manifest.session_id\n  const ogUrl = reportBaseUrl\n    ? `${reportBaseUrl.replace(/\\/$/, '')}/${transcriptUrlFromRoot(manifest)}`\n    : ''\n  return htmlShell(title, content, nav, {\n    ogDescription: summary || undefined,\n    ogUrl: ogUrl || undefined,\n  })\n}"
}

{
  "command": "cd C:/Users/ai/.codex/skills/memorytree-workflow && git commit -m \"$(cat <<'EOF'\nfeat(report): Report v2 Phase 2 — TOC, Breadcrumb, Callouts, RSS, Tags, Search filters, OG Meta, Mermaid\n\nNew files:\n- src/report/render/toc.ts: h2/h3 TOC extraction, collapsible nav, heading ID injection\n- src/report/render/breadcrumb.ts: accessible breadcrumb nav\n- src/report/render/rss.ts: RSS 2.0 feed with RFC 2822 dates, XML escaping, 100-item cap\n- src/report/tags.ts: AI tag extraction with SHA256 cache, renderTagBadges()\n\nFeature additions:\n- Callout syntax (> [!note/tip/warning/...]) with per-type emoji and color\n- Mermaid CDN conditional injection (only pages containing ```mermaid blocks)\n- Search page: client + project <select> filter dropdowns, combined AND logic\n- Tag badges on session list (AI-extracted, cached, noAi-safe)\n- OG Meta: og:title/type/description/url in htmlShell, transcript pages pass reportBaseUrl\n- TOC integrated into knowledge/goals/todos/archive pages (all 4 consistent)\n- Reader Mode: hides sidebar/topbar/breadcrumb, localStorage-persisted\n- View Transitions API: cross-page fade animation, progressive enhancement\n- Sidebar: emoji icon mode (768-1024px), collapse toggle with localStorage\n- CSS: tag pills, search filter selects, callout variants, TOC, breadcrumb, reader mode, view transitions\n\nConfig / CLI:\n- report_base_url config field (RSS <link> + OG og:url)\n- --locale and --report-base-url CLI flags on `memorytree report build`\n\nBug fixes (review):\n- rss.ts: toRfc2822 checks isNaN(d.getTime()) — Invalid Date no longer silent\n- toc.ts: injectHeadingIds regex [\\s\\S]*? handles headings with inline HTML\n- search.ts: remove entry mutation, single JSON.stringify\n- breadcrumb.ts: remove trailing space in urlless non-last items\n- dashboard.ts: remove dead transcriptHref function\n- build.ts: remove unused renderTagBadges import\n- build.test.ts: remove non-existent pdf field from BuildReportOptions calls\n- tags.ts: use escHtml from layout.ts, add t.length <= 50 guard\n\nTests: 611 passing (was 551), +60 new tests across toc, breadcrumb, rss, tags, config\nEOF\n)\""
}

Show input payload 
{
  "command": "cd C:/Users/ai/.codex/skills/memorytree-workflow && gh pr create --base fix/codex-parser-duplicates-and-injection --title \"feat(report): Report v2 — Quartz 对标全功能升级\" --body \"$(cat <<'EOF'\n## Summary\n\n两阶段完整实现 MemoryTree Report v2，功能对标 Quartz，支持 GitHub Pages 部署。\n\n### Phase 1（commit 97ae58d）\n- **侧边栏导航**：240px fixed sidebar，emoji 图标，主题切换（dark/light），localStorage 持久化\n- **i18n**：`loadLocale(locale)` 构建时单语言，内置 `en` / `zh-CN`\n- **新页面**：Graph（force-directed 知识图谱）、Projects、Todos、Archive\n- **部署**：`gh-pages` 分支推送、Webhook 通知（飞书/Telegram/Discord/Slack）\n- **Popover 预览**：session 链接 hover 显示摘要\n\n### Phase 2（commit c753bbe）\n- **TOC**：h2/h3 可折叠目录，锚点平滑滚动，支持内联 HTML 标题（`**bold**`）\n- **Breadcrumb**：`renderBreadcrumb()` accessible `<nav aria-label=\"breadcrumb\">`\n- **Callouts**：`> [!note/tip/warning/...]` 语法，颜色左边框 + emoji 图标\n- **Mermaid**：条件注入 CDN script，仅含 ` ```mermaid ` 的页面注入，其余页面零开销\n- **RSS 2.0**：`feed.xml`，RFC 2822 日期，XML 转义，100 条上限\n- **OG Meta**：`og:title/type/description/url`，`reportBaseUrl` 为空时优雅跳过\n- **Search 过滤**：client + project 双 `<select>` 下拉，AND 逻辑\n- **Tag 系统**：AI 提取 3-5 关键词，SHA256 缓存，noAi 安全，pill badges\n- **Reader Mode**：隐藏 sidebar/topbar/meta，localStorage 持久化\n- **View Transitions API**：跨页淡入淡出，渐进增强，~5 行 CSS + JS\n- **侧边栏折叠**：`«` 按钮 + localStorage，768-1024px 图标模式\n- **CLI flags**：`--locale`、`--report-base-url`\n\n### Review 修复\n两轮 code review 发现的所有 CRITICAL/HIGH/MEDIUM/LOW 问题均已修复（共 13 项）。\n\n## Test plan\n\n- [x] `npm run build` — ⚡️ Build success\n- [x] `npm test` — **611 tests passing**, 0 failures（较 Phase 1 +60 新测试）\n- [x] 新增测试文件：`toc.test.ts`、`breadcrumb.test.ts`、`rss.test.ts`、`tags.test.ts`\n- [x] `config.test.ts` 补充 `report_base_url` 5 条测试\n- [ ] 手动验证：`memorytree report build --root <repo> --no-ai --locale zh-CN`\n- [ ] 手动验证：`memorytree report serve` 预览所有页面\n\n🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)\nEOF\n)\""
}

assistant #1048 23:19:08

确认了：markdownToHtml 完全没有实现 ![alt](url) 图片语法。

当前 applyInline 只处理：bold / italic / inline code / [text](url) 链接。![alt](url) 会被当成普通链接处理（[alt](url) 部分），前面的 ! 原样输出，图片完全不渲染。

---

所以你提到的"知识图片"可以有两种理解：

A. Markdown 图片 ![alt](url) — 用户在 Memory/04_knowledge/ 的 md 文件里引用了本地或远程图片，但 HTML 报告里看不到，只看到 ![alt] 文字

B. Graph 页面的知识图谱 — graph.html 的 force-directed 可视化，这个已经实现了

你说的是 A（markdown 图片渲染） 还是 B（知识图谱）？还是另有所指？
